data.prompt:
You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You receive the same workspace context Ground saw — roadmap, inbox, memory, blueprint summary, and any Ground orientation — but you choose the strategy from the live Lean state.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level. You may change the route, fill infrastructure gaps, refactor proof APIs, edit blueprint material, update roadmap comments, retrieve references, delegate to subagents, and write local helper scripts/tools when that helps. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the write scope and shared `references/`, keep changes semantic, and leave no stray scratch files. Prefer ambitious progress over defensive avoidance.

Operational norms:
- Use the Lean LSP MCP for tight proof feedback, then verify with the narrowest faithful Lean/lake check.
- Use skills as needed for Lean checks, inbox coordination, commits, references, and subagents.
- Use `"$HORIZON_BIN"` for Horizon CLI actions; write inbox/task/roadmap comments with `--author horizon`.
- Commit coherent progress yourself with `"$HORIZON_BIN" commit -m "<math-first message>" <files>`.
- Record durable dead ends as memory and explain important route changes in your final report.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-commit — Record your own work in the workspace ledger with semantic commits via `horizon commit` — small, meaningful, math-first messages; safe under concurrent runs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, archive, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting — How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session — block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions before proving — the local `horizon search` index plus the Lean LSP MCP search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/state-search/hammer-premise). Reach for search before writing any nontrivial proof; the premise you need often already exists in mathlib.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- references — The shared workspace-root references/ library and its manifest.yaml — how to find, read, and cite original sources with \source{...}. Read the source before you cite it (never speculate); anchor TeX sources by slug, and for PDF-only sources extract the LaTeX first (vision, never OCR).
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge at MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Write scope — you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: .archon-horizon/runs/0010/sessions/0071-horizon-T12

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

# Latest Ground orientation
This was saved as `recommendation.md` by the last Ground session. Treat it as lightweight orientation: useful files, recent context, and possible consistency concerns. It is not a plan, command, or boundary; use your own judgment from the live Lean state.
# Orientation — run 0010 T12 (after r8 reconcile)

- Useful context: the rank-`e` stratum now exists as a closed subscheme in `MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/EntryIdealStratum.lean` (806 LOC, sorry-free, axiom-clean, build green 8584 jobs; Ground-verified). Key decls: `strataIdeal`, `map_strataIdeal_basicOpen` (quasi-coherence, noetherian-free), `stratumι` (`IsClosedImmersion`), `mem_range_stratumι_iff` (support = rank exactly `e`), `pointRank`, `Ideal.fiberRank_baseChange` (flatness-free). Full next-step route (Stage D/E, exact Mathlib lemma names) is in file-memory `t12-rank-stratum-landed`.

- Relevant frontier: the sole residual `sorry` of the `FlatteningStratification` cone is `flatLocusStratification_universal` (`AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GenericFlatnessGeometric.lean:2045`). The stratum construction supplies its geometric input; what remains is the **factorization/representability** layer (morphisms `T → X` with flat constant-rank pullback factor uniquely through `stratumι` via `IsClosedImmersion.lift`) plus stratum flatness and the clopen rank decomposition + `Sigma` assembly.

- Lean/blueprint consistency: the blueprint chapter `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex` gained section `sec:flatstrat_rank_stratum` (24 `\leanok` nodes, 1-to-1 with the Lean; AJC 0 dangling). The universal theorem `thm:flattening_stratification_universal` keeps its proof un-`\leanok`: its prose asserts a representability the Lean does not yet formalize — that is exactly the open Stage D. The `ChartsCover G e` hypothesis (surfaced as `def:charts_cover`) forces fibre rank `≤ e` everywhere and is not derived from noetherian+coherent; a bridge lemma "coherent on noetherian ⇒ `e`-presentation charts" is absent.

- Environment: idle-box builds are cheap (load ~2, `lake build` of the one module ≈ minutes); `pullback_app_isoTensor` (public, general morphism) in `QuotScheme.lean` and `rankAtStalk_eq`/`isLocallyConstant_rankAtStalk` (Mathlib) are the load-bearing premises for Stage D/E. v4.31 wall recipes (congrArg+Eq.mp over rw/▸ on `mk'` terms; canonical OreLocalization base-algebra instances) are in file-memory `v431-instance-wall-recipes-r2`.

# Task focus
Task: T12
Title: Picard scheme representability (FGA) — Pic_{C/k} representable, locally of finite type
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Suggested scope: projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge; files=AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GrassmannianQuot.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean
References: roadmap=AJC.picrep

Prove **representability of the relative Picard functor by a scheme** (FGA / Grothendieck FGA 232 / Theorem 4.8 in Kleiman) — roadmap cone `AJC.picrep`. For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve $C/k$ with a $k$-rational point, the relative Picard functor is representable by a $k$-scheme locally of finite type:

$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable by a scheme, locally of finite type}.$$

# Roadmap slice
- AJC.albanese [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Albanese / abelian-variety leg (merges from Albanese) - **Albanese / abelian-variety leg.** The Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ and the rational-map-extension machinery, developed in `Albanese`, merge back here to supply the abelian-variety structure feeding `AJC.jacobian`.

- AJC.cech [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Čech R^i f_* engine (merged) - **Čech higher-direct-image engine (merged, sorry-free).**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\qquad(f\ \text{separated quasi-compact, finite affine cover}).$$
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` + `pushPull` functoriality, proved in `Cech-Cohomology` and merged into AJC — axiom-clean. The engine cone is sorry-free on Lean v4.31 (the former migration-interim sorries closed by T8). Residual base-change sorries live in `AJC.fbc`, not here.

- AJC.fbc [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Flat base change (Stacks 02KH) - **Flat base change (Stacks 02KH), Čech route.** For flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). **02KG affine-reduction heart closed**: the per-$\sigma$ heart `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChange` (ring-pushout carve + corner-sections tensor rewrite + the affine brick `affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange`), together with `openImmersion_beckChevalley`/`bareBC`, the affine essential-image node (`Cohomology/AffinePushPullEssImage.lean`, `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma`), the RHS tilde leaf `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChanged_pushforward_iso_tilde`, and arbitrary-morphism pullback quasi-coherence (`Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean`, 01BG) are sorry-free and axiom-clean. The superseded canonical-mate route was pruned; `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean` is now sorry-free. Residual toward `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` — **3 leaf sorries** (all in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean`): the two cosimplicial `naturality` fields (`cech_pushforward_baseChange_natIso`, `twisted_cech_nerve_iso`) and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact — **not** an adjointness freebie, see `I-0076`). See task **T2** and memory `I-0083`.

- AJC.grquot [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged) - **Relative Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged, sorry-free).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)(T)\;\cong\;\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{locally free rank }d\,\}\quad(\texttt{Grassmannian.represents}).$$
Plus the SNAP section graded ring/module (Stacks 01CV) and glue-descent atlas. Merged from `GR-Quot-Closure`.

- AJC.jacobian [pending/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Jacobian = Albanese (top goal) - **North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

- AJC.linebundle [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Line-bundle comparison-iso / Pic^sharp group (A.1.c.sub) - **Line-bundle comparison-iso substrate (A.1.c.sub).** Gives $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ its abelian-group law via the tensor/dual comparison
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$
Finished in `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`; merges back into AJC via task **T1** (closes the 5 residual `TensorObjSubstrate`/`PullbackTensorComp` sorries).

- AJC.pic0av [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Pic^0 is an abelian variety (A.3) - **$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ is an abelian variety (A.3).** Kleiman §5 tangent-space identification, then structure:
$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (Chevalley–Rosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

- AJC.picrep [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Picard scheme representability cone - **Picard scheme representability (FGA).** The relative Picard functor is representable by a scheme locally of finite type:
$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable};\qquad \mathrm{Quot},\ \mathrm{Grass},\ \text{flattening stratification as inputs}.$$
Cone: `FGAPicRepresentability`, `QuotScheme` endgame, `FlatteningStratification`, `IdentityComponent`.
 tasks=T12
- AJC.substrate [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Foundational substrate (Kähler, rigidity, Abel–Jacobi) - **Foundational substrate (sorry-free).** Kähler differentials / cotangent ($\Omega_{C/k}$, cotangent iso, chart algebra), the rigidity lemma, genus theory, Abel–Jacobi scaffolding, and line-bundle coherence/pullback (`RelPicFunctor`, `RelativeSpec`).


# Opened inbox
(no accepted inbox items)

# Memory
- **leandag audit convention: the DAG engine registers only STATEMENT-level `\uses`; proof-level `\uses` are ignored.**

- Consequence: a blueprint node whose only dependencies are declared inside its proof block shows as **isolated** in `horizon leandag`, even though the math dependency is real and the proof is complete. This is expected, not a blueprint defect.
- Observed run 0006 (Albanese): 297 nodes, **178 isolated**, but **0 dangling** and 287 proved — the high isolated count is entirely this artifact.
- **The health metric to trust is `dangling` (should be 0) and `proved`, NOT `isolated`.** Do not chase isolated nodes as missing edges; if you want them linked in the DAG, lift the relevant `\uses` from the proof block up to the statement line.
- Same engine limitation was noted by the T6 Horizon session; recorded here so future Ground audits don't misread it.
- **tangentSpaceIso (A.3.iii) is NOT directly provable — critical path runs through the relative Pic functor, and a naive FGA rewire is a FALSE-axiom trap.**

- `Pic0.tangentSpaceIso` (`Pic0AbelianVariety.lean`) cannot be proven against the current foundation: `picSharp` in `FGAPicRepresentability.lean` is `Classical.choice` of a `⟨sorry⟩` `Nonempty` — a fully opaque functor. No H¹ comparison can attach to it. Do not re-probe the sorry directly.
- **Critical path** (in order): (1) ~~real relative Pic presheaf~~ DONE session 0015 (`PicSharp.relPresheaf` in `RelPicFunctor.lean`, `H_T`-coset carrier, axiom-clean); (2) étale sheafification target — Mathlib v4.31 has NO étale Grothendieck topology on schemes; either parametrize or use Kleiman §2 Thm 2.5: for C/k with a **k-rational section**, `Pic^♯(T) → Pic^♯_ét(T)` is already bijective, so representability can be stated against `relPresheaf` directly under a section hypothesis; (3) rewire FGA `picSharp` := (sheafified) relative functor — universe bump `Type u → Type (u+1)` in `HasPicSharp`/`HasPicScheme` is viable (`Functor.RepresentableBy` is universe-polymorphic; `Equiv` is cross-universe); (4) `Pic(C_ε) ≅ H¹(C_ε, O^×)` cocycle classification + truncated exponential split sequence `0 → O_C → O^×_{C_ε} → O^×_C → 1` for the kernel = `H¹(C,O_C)` computation (Kleiman Thm 5.11) — none of this exists yet, largest remaining block.
- **TRAP**: wiring `picSharp := PicSharp.presheaf` (the absolute functor, iso-class carrier) would make `PicSharpRepresentable`'s sorry a mathematically FALSE statement (absolute `Pic(C×T)` is not representable — it differs from `Hom(T, Pic)` by `Pic(T)`). Any rewire must use the relative (and in general sheafified) functor.
- Proof-pattern notes: the `Quotient.lift`/`Quotient.sound` + iso-chain style (pullbackCongr/pullbackId/pullbackComp `.app`, D4′ `pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial`, `pullback_pullback_eq`) compiled essentially first-try — no v4.31 transparency walls in this corner. `pullback.lift_fst/snd` are NOT simp (only `@[reassoc]`); pass them + their `_assoc` forms to `simp` explicitly after `pullback.hom_ext`.
- Milne 3.1/3.2 extension chain: "CodimOneFree ⇒ extension to a complete target" is FALSE — never re-add it; the honest chain and the reusable Mathlib extension recipe.

- **Do not reintroduce** an `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`-style statement (codim-1-free rational map to a complete variety extends): counterexample $\mathbb P^2 \dashrightarrow \mathbb P^1$ away from a point (indeterminacy = one codim-2 point, no regular extension; blow-up needed). Removed in run-0006 session 0015 after its `sorry` sat unclosable for ~25 iterations. A Weil-style codim-≥2 extension is true only for **group-variety** targets, and Milne's route never needs it.
- **Honest chain** (all in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` + `Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`): Milne 3.1 = `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` (proved, valuative); Milne 3.3 (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`, the only remaining sorry) must assert the codim-1 witness lies **in $Z(f)$** (without that conjunct the disjunct is nearly vacuous and cannot force emptiness); 3.3 + 3.1 ⇒ $Z(f)=\emptyset$ (`av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`); then extension. **Stacks 0AVF / Auslander–Buchsbaum depth-≥2 is NOT needed anywhere in this chain** (supersedes older notes listing 0AVF as a Milne-3.1 blocker).
- **Recipe: extension from empty indeterminacy** (`existsUnique_hom_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`, reduced X / separated Y): witness `X.topIso.inv ≫ X.homOfLE hdom.ge ≫ f.toPartialMap.hom` where `hdom : f.toPartialMap.domain = ⊤` (`toPartialMap.domain = f.domain` is `rfl`); rational-class equality via `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mpr ⟨⊤, …⟩` + `simp only [PartialMap.restrict_hom, Scheme.Hom.toPartialMap, Scheme.topIso_hom, Scheme.ι_toIso_inv_assoc, Scheme.homOfLE_homOfLE_assoc]`; uniqueness via `hom_ext_of_toRationalMap_eq` (= `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mp` + `Opens.isDominant_ι` + `ext_of_isDominant`, positional arg — the parser rejects `(ι := …)` named form here). Both lemmas need `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` BEFORE the docstring (`(restrict …).domain` / `Hom.toPartialMap` defeq walls).
- **Standing lesson**: before attacking a long-lived typed `sorry`, sanity-check the statement for truth (hunt small counterexamples); a false pinned statement silently blocks a whole cone and can smuggle wrong `\uses` (here it dragged a phantom `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`/0AVF dependency into the blueprint for ~30 iterations).
- **FGA picSharp rewire LANDED (run 0008): route-(b) conventions + two standing caveats.**

State after the rewire (`Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`, **4 real `sorry`s**, all TRUE statements — Ground-verified run 0008 reconcile):

- `picSharp C := PicSharp.relPresheaf C ⋙ forget AddCommGrpCat` (`Type (u+1)`-valued, REAL). `Scheme.HasRationalPoint C` = Nonempty section subtype of `C.hom`; the ONE genuine FGA sorry is `instHasPicScheme [HasRationalPoint C]`. The other three are typed-sorry carriers: `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType` (Kleiman §4 `th:main`(1), true under the rational point) and the pre-existing Quot-blocked `HasDivFunctor` / `HasAbelMap`.
- `instPicSharpRepresentable` proved by `choose_spec`; `groupSchemeStructure : CommGrpObj (PicScheme C)` proved via `CommGrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` + a `Multiplicative.ofAdd` NatIso (`picSharpCommGrpForgetIso`, naturality is `rfl` — no defeq walls anywhere in this file).
- Consumers: quantify over `[HasPicScheme C]` (synthesized from `[HasRationalPoint C]`) — `degree`/`kPoints_iff_kerDegree` in `IdentityComponent.lean` now carry it. `Pic0Scheme C := GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)` is now a real def (axiom-clean), gated on `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType`.

**Caveat 1 — pointless curves:** `JacobianWitness` (`Jacobian.lean`) requires the abelian variety $J$ to exist UNconditionally, but route (b) only builds `PicScheme` for pointed $C$. For a genus-$\ge 1$ curve without a rational point the eventual `picardJacobianWitness` proof needs Galois/étale descent from a finite extension where $C$ gains a point (or a pointed restatement of the north star). Not blocking anything now — record before anyone tries to close `picardJacobianWitness` directly.

**Caveat 2 — `smoothProperQuotient` statement is WEAKER than Kleiman lm:qt and unprovable as stated:** Mathlib v4.31 has NO quasi-projectivity vocabulary, so hypothesis (2) of lm:qt is missing from the Lean statement; without it the statement is FALSE (Hironaka-type free ℤ/2 action on a smooth proper non-projective 3-fold: smooth proper equivalence relation, étale-quotient sheaf a non-scheme algebraic space). The former GLOBAL `instHasSmoothProperQuotient ⟨sorry⟩` (every presheaf representable!) was deleted this run (Ground-confirmed gone); the class is a use-site hypothesis only. Do NOT reintroduce a global instance, and do NOT try to prove the theorem as stated.
- **Stacks 01BG general pullback-qcoh CLOSED + FBC RHS tilde leaf CLOSED (run 0003 session 0013, T2) — supersedes all "general-morphism pullback qcoh is absent / only in sorry-tainted QuotScheme" notes.**

- `Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean` (new, ~185 LOC, axiom-clean): `pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom (g : Y ⟶ X)` for ARBITRARY scheme morphisms. Recipe: localize the pullback, not the presentation — per cover member `A`, `(g^*F)|_{g⁻¹A} ≅ (g.resLE A W)^*(F|_A)` via `pullbackComp`+`pullbackCongr (resLE_comp_ι g le_rfl)`+`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`, transport the global presentation of `F|_A` by `SheafOfModules.Presentation.map` (15-underscore @-application, copy `presentationRestrictSliceOfOver`'s shape), then the CechTermAcyclic over-bridge tail (`modulesOverOpensEquivalence` / `overOpensIsoRestrict` / `overOpensFunctorUnitIso` / counit) and `IsQuasicoherent.of_coversTop` on the preimage cover.
- Enablers now available: `opensMap_final` (`(Opens.map φ).Final` for ANY continuous map — structured-arrow posets are ⊔-directed, `Functor.final_of_isFiltered_structuredArrow`) and `pullbackUnitIso : g^*𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_Y` (Mathlib's `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` iso under `[F.Final]`). Gotcha: re-synthesis of `IsIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit …)` at USE sites can fail (defeq wall in the instance-arg unification) — pass the instance explicitly via `@asIso _ _ _ _ _ (pullbackObjUnitToUnit_isIso_hom g)`.
- FBC leaf `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChanged_pushforward_iso_tilde` (622-line sorry) closed as literally `(pushforward f').mapIso (twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma …) ≪≫ pushPullObj_coverInter_pushforward_iso_tilde f' 𝒰' (g'^*F) (pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom g' F hF) σ` — the def had to MOVE below `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma` in the file. FBC leaves 5 → 4.
- Iteration cost: `lake env lean` on a file importing Mathlib+`CechTermAcyclic` ≈ 4 min under load ~65; the 2-module `lake build` (new file + `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional`) ≈ 25 min.
- **`pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (FBC leaf, `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean:186`) is NOT a cheap adjointness win — Ground's recommendation had the variance backwards. Route analysis (session 0013), do not re-derive.**

- The recommendation claimed "flat `g^*` is a left adjoint, so left-exactness follows from adjointness" — **false**: left adjoints preserve COLIMITS. Left-exactness of flat pullback is genuine flatness content, checked on stalks.
- No dodge exists at the presheaf level: `PresheafOfModules.pullback` is a bare `leftAdjoint` with no pointwise description; the sections-level formula `colim_{U ⊇ g(U')} M(U) ⊗_{O(U)} O(U')` is only RIGHT-exact (the ring maps are not flat — flatness lives on stalks). Sheafification + `SheafOfModules.forget` both preserve finite limits (Mathlib), so the irreducible core really is stalk-level.

This is a ONE-SHOT headless session: when you produce your final report the session ENDS and you will NOT be re-invoked. Nothing calls you back when a background job finishes, and any background process you started is killed when the session exits. So you cannot 'kick off a build and wait to be notified' — that notification never comes here, and the build dies.
Therefore: if a build, subagent, monitor, or shell command produces a result your conclusion depends on, you MUST get that result WITHIN this session — run it in the FOREGROUND and block on it (read its exit code), or actively poll until it has definitively finished, before writing the report. A heavy `lake build` can take many minutes; budget for that and wait it out. If it genuinely cannot finish in time, do NOT imply success: record the unfinished build under `## Issues`, state what's still unknown, and leave the explicit next action — never write a conclusion that assumes a pending result.

Finish with a brief run-local report. Recommended sections: `## Summary`, `## Progress`, `## Issues`, `## Why I stopped`, and `## Next`; add or rename other sections if another shape is clearer, but keep `## Progress` and `## Why I stopped`. In `## Progress`, use only inline `-` bullets, one per relevant file or target, e.g. `- FileA.lean: 4 sorries -> 3 sorries; closed the base case.` or `- FileB.lean: No change because dependency X is blocked.` Do not use nested bullets or wrapped multi-line bullets there. In `## Why I stopped`, start with either `Task complete:` when the assigned objective is genuinely complete, or `Task not complete:` when work remains. If there is a plausible next action inside the session's scope and budget, take it before stopping; a clean commit or green partial check is not by itself a reason to stop. Prefer short bullets, and keep each bullet around 20 words or fewer. Always mention bugs, build failures, broken proofs, suspicious code issues, blocked dependencies, unresolved assumptions, and checks that failed or were not run. For this {role} session, make the report complete enough that a human or Ground can understand the session before opening raw logs.

1996	/-- **Universal property of the flat-locus stratification (the `n = 0`
1997	flattening stratification)** [Nitsure §4, special case, parts (i) + (ii)].

1999	For `S` noetherian and `𝓕` a coherent `𝓞_S`-module there is a *finite*
2000	locally-closed stratification `{S_f}` of `S` — immersions, set-theoretically
2001	covering `|S|`, pairwise disjoint, `𝓕|_{S_f}` flat over `S_f` — such that,
2002	writing `i : ∐ S_f ⟶ S` for `Sigma.desc` of the inclusions, every morphism
2003	`φ : T ⟶ S` for which `φ^*𝓕` is flat over `T` factors *uniquely* through
2004	`i`.
2005	
2006	Source: [Nitsure], §4, proof of the flattening-stratification theorem,
2007	special case `n = 0` (Nakayama prolongation of a fibre basis to a local
2008	presentation `𝓞_V^{⊕m} →ψ 𝓞_V^{⊕e} → 𝓕|_V → 0`, the closed subscheme
2009	`V_e ⊆ V` cut out by the entry ideal of `ψ`, base change: `f^*𝓕` is locally
2010	free of rank `e` iff `f^*ψ = 0` iff `f` factors through `V_e`; the local
2011	strata glue by their universal property).  Flatness replaces
2012	locally-free-of-rank-`e`: for finitely presented `𝓕` they agree, with the
2013	rank decomposing `T` into clopen pieces.
2014	
2015	Statement repair (run 0010, T12 r7): the previous statement generalized
2016	Nitsure's part (ii) from `𝓕` on `ℙⁿ_S` to an arbitrary *proper*
2017	`π : X ⟶ S`.  That generality is not in the cited source (Nitsure proves
2018	the theorem for `ℙⁿ_S`, hence for projective `π`) and no reference for the
2019	proper case is in the workspace library; moreover the reduced strata of
2020	`flatLocusReduction` provably cannot witness it (over `S = Spec k[ε]` with
2021	`𝓕 = 𝓞_S` flat, `𝟙 S` must factor through the stratification, forcing the
2022	canonical non-reduced stratum structure).  The statement is therefore
2023	specialized to `π = 𝟙 S` — exactly Nitsure's special case, which his
2024	general-case proof consumes.  The projective upgrade (the Route-A consumer
2025	shape, `π = pr_T : C ×_k T → T` for a projective curve `C`) needs `ℙⁿ_S`

2034	theorem flatLocusStratification_universal {S : Scheme.{u}} [IsNoetherian S]
2035	    (F : S.Modules) [F.IsFinitePresentation] :
2036	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (S_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, S_ f ⟶ S),
2037	      (∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)) ∧
2038	      (∀ s : S, ∃ f, s ∈ Set.range (ι f).base) ∧
2039	      (∀ f g, f ≠ g → Disjoint (Set.range (ι f).base) (Set.range (ι g).base)) ∧
2040	      (∀ f, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (𝟙 (S_ f))
2041	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (ι f)).obj F)) ∧
2042	      (∀ {T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T ⟶ S),
2043	        Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (𝟙 T) ((Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj F) →
2044	        ∃! ψ : T ⟶ ∐ S_, ψ ≫ Sigma.desc ι = φ) := by
2045	  sorry
2046	
2047	/-- **Flattening stratification for a coherent sheaf on a relative
2048	curve** [Nitsure §4 corollary; Route~A consumer A.2.a entry-point].
2049	
2050	Let `k` be a field, `C` a smooth proper curve over `k` (encoded as
2051	`C : Over (Spec k)` with `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]` and
2052	`[IsProper C.hom]`), and `T` a noetherian `k`-scheme. For any coherent
2053	sheaf `𝓕` on the relative curve `C ×_k T`, the flattening
2054	stratification conclusion of `flatteningStratification` applies to
2055	`π = pr_T : C ×_k T → T` and `𝓕`: there is a finite locally-closed
2056	stratification `{T_f ⊆ T}` set-theoretically covering `T` disjointly,
2057	such that `𝓕|_{C ×_k T_f}` is flat over `T_f` for each `f`.

2059	The body invokes `flatteningStratification` on the base-changed
2060	morphism `pullback.snd C.hom T.hom : (C ×_k T) ⟶ T`, using that
2061	`IsProper (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom)` holds by base change of
2062	`IsProper C.hom`; hence this corollary is proved (no `sorry`). -/
2063	theorem flatteningStratification.ofCurve {k : Type u} [Field k]
2064	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
2065	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
2066	    (T : Over (Spec (.of k))) [IsNoetherian T.left]
2067	    (F : (Limits.pullback C.hom T.hom).Modules) [F.IsFinitePresentation] :
2068	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (T_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, T_ f ⟶ T.left),
2069	      (∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)) ∧
2070	      (∀ t : T.left, ∃ f, t ∈ Set.range (ι f).base) ∧
2071	      (∀ f g, f ≠ g → Disjoint (Set.range (ι f).base) (Set.range (ι g).base)) ∧
2072	      (∀ f, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat
2073	        (pullback.snd (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom) (ι f))
2074	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback
2075	          (pullback.fst (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom) (ι f))).obj F)) :=
2076	  flatteningStratification (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom) F
2077	
2078	end AlgebraicGeometry

6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.EntryIdeal
8	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.QuotScheme
9	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.PullbackQuasicoherent
10	
11	/-!
12	# Rank strata of a coherent module: geometric substrate (Nitsure §4, `n = 0`)
13	
14	This file builds the geometric layer between the entry-ideal commutative
15	algebra of `AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.EntryIdeal` and the universal property
16	of the flat-locus stratification
17	(`AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusStratification_universal` in
18	`AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.GenericFlatnessGeometric`), following the special
19	case `n = 0` of the flattening-stratification theorem in [Nitsure, §4].
20	
21	## Contents
22	
23	* §1 — presentation transport (`Module.MatrixPresentation.congr`) and
24	  fiber-rank bricks: monotonicity under `e`-generator presentations
25	  (`fiberRank_le`), invariance under linear equivalences
26	  (`Ideal.fiberRank_congr`), base change
27	  (`Ideal.fiberRank_baseChange`: the fiber of `A ⊗[R] M` at a prime `q`
28	  has the same dimension as the fiber of `M` at `q ∩ R`), and the
29	  localized-module corollary (`Ideal.fiberRank_of_isLocalizedModule`).
30	* §2 — the point ↔ prime dictionary for affine opens:
31	  membership in `Scheme.zeroLocus`/`Scheme.basicOpen` in terms of
32	  `IsAffineOpen.primeIdealOf`.
33	* §3 — presentation charts: affine opens `V` on which the section module
34	  `Γ(G, V)` admits an `e`-generator matrix presentation; stability under
35	  basic-open localization (via the quasi-coherent section-localization
36	  engine `Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen` of
37	  `AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.QuotScheme`); the point-rank function and its
38	  chart-independence.
39	
40	Blueprint chapter: `Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex`,
41	§`sec:flatstrat_universal`.
42	Source: [Nitsure], §4, proof of the flattening-stratification theorem,
43	special case `n = 0` (`references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/`
44	`nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex`, L1849–L1885).
45	-/
46	
47	universe u
48	
49	open TensorProduct
50	
51	/-! ## §1 Presentation transport and fiber-rank bricks -/
52	
53	namespace Module.MatrixPresentation
54	
55	section Congr
56	
57	variable {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
58	variable {N : Type u} [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N] {e m : ℕ}
59	
60	/-- Transport of a matrix presentation along a linear equivalence of the
61	presented module; the relation matrix — hence the entry ideal — is
62	unchanged. -/
63	noncomputable def congr (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m) (σ : M ≃ₗ[R] N) :
64	    MatrixPresentation R N e m where
65	  relMatrix := P.relMatrix
66	  proj := σ.toLinearMap ∘ₗ P.proj
67	  surjective_proj := σ.surjective.comp P.surjective_proj
68	  exact_mulVecLin_proj := fun y => by
69	    simp only [LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearEquiv.coe_coe,
70	      LinearEquiv.map_eq_zero_iff]
71	    exact P.exact_mulVecLin_proj y
72	
73	@[simp] lemma congr_relMatrix (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m)
74	    (σ : M ≃ₗ[R] N) : (P.congr σ).relMatrix = P.relMatrix := rfl
75	
76	@[simp] lemma congr_entryIdeal (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m)
77	    (σ : M ≃ₗ[R] N) : (P.congr σ).entryIdeal = P.entryIdeal := rfl
78	
79	/-- An `e`-generator presentation bounds the fiber rank at every prime by
80	`e`: the base-changed projection is a surjection `κ(p)^e ↠ κ(p) ⊗ M`. -/
81	theorem fiberRank_le (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m) (p : Ideal R)
82	    [p.IsPrime] : p.fiberRank M ≤ e := by
83	  have hsurj := (P.baseChange p.ResidueField).surjective_proj
84	  have h := LinearMap.finrank_le_finrank_of_surjective
85	    (f := (P.baseChange p.ResidueField).proj) hsurj
86	  simpa [Ideal.fiberRank] using h
87	
88	end Congr
89	
90	end Module.MatrixPresentation
91	
92	namespace Ideal
93	
94	section FiberRankCongr
95	
96	variable {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
97	variable {N : Type u} [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N]
98	
99	/-- The fiber rank is invariant under linear equivalences. -/
100	lemma fiberRank_congr (p : Ideal R) [p.IsPrime] (σ : M ≃ₗ[R] N) :
101	    p.fiberRank M = p.fiberRank N :=
102	  LinearEquiv.finrank_eq (LinearEquiv.baseChange R p.ResidueField M N σ)
103	
104	/-- Congruence of the fiber rank in the prime (avoids the dependent-motive
105	failure of `rw` through the `IsPrime` instance). -/
106	lemma fiberRank_congr_ideal {p q : Ideal R} [p.IsPrime] [q.IsPrime]
107	    (h : p = q) : p.fiberRank M = q.fiberRank M := by
108	  subst h; rfl
109	
110	end FiberRankCongr
111	
112	section FiberRankBaseChange
113	
114	variable {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {A : Type u} [CommRing A] [Algebra R A]
115	variable (M : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
116	
117	/-- **Base-change invariance of the fiber rank**: for an `R`-algebra `A`
118	and a prime `q` of `A` lying over `p = q ∩ R`, the fiber of `A ⊗[R] M` at
119	`q` is the base change of the fiber of `M` at `p` along the residue-field
120	extension `κ(p) → κ(q)`; in particular the fiber dimensions agree.  No
121	flatness or finiteness hypotheses. -/
122	theorem fiberRank_baseChange (q : Ideal A) [q.IsPrime] :
123	    q.fiberRank (A ⊗[R] M) = (q.comap (algebraMap R A)).fiberRank M := by
124	  set p := q.comap (algebraMap R A) with hp
125	  -- the residue-field extension `κ(p) → κ(q)`; the `R`- and `A`-algebra
126	  -- structures on the residue fields are the canonical ones
127	  letI : Algebra p.ResidueField q.ResidueField :=
128	    (Ideal.ResidueField.map p q (algebraMap R A) rfl).toAlgebra
129	  haveI : IsScalarTower R p.ResidueField q.ResidueField := by
130	    refine .of_algebraMap_eq fun r => ?_
131	    show algebraMap R q.ResidueField r =
132	      Ideal.ResidueField.map p q (algebraMap R A) rfl (algebraMap R p.ResidueField r)
133	    rw [Ideal.ResidueField.map_algebraMap,
134	      IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply R A q.ResidueField]
135	  -- cancel the two base changes
136	  have e1 : q.Fiber (A ⊗[R] M) ≃ₗ[q.ResidueField]
137	      q.ResidueField ⊗[R] M :=
138	    AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange R A q.ResidueField q.ResidueField M
139	  have e2 : q.ResidueField ⊗[R] M ≃ₗ[q.ResidueField]
140	      q.ResidueField ⊗[p.ResidueField] (p.Fiber M) :=
141	    (AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange R p.ResidueField q.ResidueField
142	      q.ResidueField M).symm
143	  rw [Ideal.fiberRank, LinearEquiv.finrank_eq (e1.trans e2),
144	    Module.finrank_baseChange, Ideal.fiberRank]
145	
146	end FiberRankBaseChange
147	
148	section FiberRankLocalized
149	
150	variable {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
151	variable {R' : Type u} [CommRing R'] [Algebra R R'] (S : Submonoid R)
152	variable [IsLocalization S R']
153	variable {N : Type u} [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N] [Module R' N]
154	variable [IsScalarTower R R' N]
155	
156	/-- Localized modules have the same fiber ranks: for a localization
157	`R' = S⁻¹R`, a localized module `N = S⁻¹M` and a prime `q` of `R'`, the
158	fiber rank of `N` at `q` equals that of `M` at the contraction `q ∩ R`. -/
159	theorem fiberRank_of_isLocalizedModule (l : M →ₗ[R] N)
160	    [IsLocalizedModule S l] (q : Ideal R') [q.IsPrime] :
161	    q.fiberRank N = (q.comap (algebraMap R R')).fiberRank M := by
162	  have hbc := IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange S R' l
163	  exact (Ideal.fiberRank_congr q hbc.equiv.symm).trans
164	    (Ideal.fiberRank_baseChange M q)
165	
166	end FiberRankLocalized
167	
168	end Ideal
169	
170	/-! ## §2 The point ↔ prime dictionary on affine opens -/
171	
172	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen
173	
174	variable {X : Scheme.{u}} {U : X.Opens} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
175	
176	include hU in
177	/-- A point of an affine open lies in the zero locus of an ideal of
178	sections iff its prime contains the ideal. -/
179	lemma mem_zeroLocus_iff_le_primeIdealOf (I : Ideal Γ(X, U)) (x : U) :
180	    (x : X) ∈ X.zeroLocus (U := U) (I : Set Γ(X, U)) ↔
181	      I ≤ (hU.primeIdealOf x).asIdeal := by
182	  constructor
183	  · intro hx
184	    have h4 : (x : X) ∈ hU.fromSpec '' PrimeSpectrum.zeroLocus (I : Set Γ(X, U)) := by
185	      rw [hU.fromSpec_image_zeroLocus]
186	      exact ⟨hx, x.2⟩
187	    obtain ⟨y, hy, hyx⟩ := h4
188	    have hinj : Function.Injective hU.fromSpec :=
189	      hU.fromSpec.isOpenEmbedding.injective
190	    have hyeq : y = hU.primeIdealOf x :=
191	      hinj (by rw [hyx, hU.fromSpec_primeIdealOf x])
192	    subst hyeq
193	    exact fun s hs => (PrimeSpectrum.mem_zeroLocus _ _).mp hy hs
194	  · intro hle
195	    have h1 : hU.primeIdealOf x ∈ PrimeSpectrum.zeroLocus (I : Set Γ(X, U)) :=
196	      (PrimeSpectrum.mem_zeroLocus _ _).mpr fun s hs => hle hs
197	    have h2 : (x : X) ∈ hU.fromSpec '' PrimeSpectrum.zeroLocus (I : Set Γ(X, U)) :=
198	      ⟨_, h1, hU.fromSpec_primeIdealOf x⟩
199	    rw [hU.fromSpec_image_zeroLocus] at h2
200	    exact h2.1
201	
202	include hU in
203	/-- A point of an affine open lies in a basic open iff the section avoids
204	its prime. -/
205	lemma mem_basicOpen_iff_notMem_primeIdealOf (f : Γ(X, U)) (x : U) :
206	    (x : X) ∈ X.basicOpen f ↔ f ∉ (hU.primeIdealOf x).asIdeal := by
207	  have hpre : hU.primeIdealOf x ∈ hU.fromSpec ⁻¹ᵁ X.basicOpen f ↔
208	      f ∉ (hU.primeIdealOf x).asIdeal := by
209	    rw [hU.fromSpec_preimage_basicOpen]
210	    exact PrimeSpectrum.mem_basicOpen _ _
211	  refine Iff.trans ?_ hpre
212	  show _ ↔ hU.fromSpec (hU.primeIdealOf x) ∈ X.basicOpen f
213	  rw [hU.fromSpec_primeIdealOf x]
214	
215	end AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen
216	
217	/-! ## §3 Presentation charts and the point rank -/
218	
219	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
220	
221	open Module CategoryTheory
222	
223	/-- The `appLE` of the identity morphism is the plain restriction map. -/
224	lemma Scheme.id_appLE {X : Scheme.{u}} {U V : X.Opens}
225	    (e : V ≤ (𝟙 X : X ⟶ X) ⁻¹ᵁ U) :
226	    (𝟙 X : X ⟶ X).appLE U V e =
227	      X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (show V ≤ U from e)).op := by
228	  simp only [Scheme.Hom.appLE, Scheme.Hom.id_app]
229	  exact Category.id_comp _
230	
231	namespace Scheme.Modules
232	
233	variable {X : Scheme.{u}} (G : X.Modules)
234	
235	/-- The canonical `Γ(X, U)`-module structure on the sections of `G` over a
236	basic open of `U` (restriction of scalars along the restriction map);
237	matches the canonical algebra instance
238	`Scheme.algebra_section_section_basicOpen`. -/
239	noncomputable local instance moduleSectionBasicOpen
240	    {U : X.Opens} (f : Γ(X, U)) :
241	    Module Γ(X, U) Γ(G, X.basicOpen f) :=
242	  Module.compHom _ (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f)).op).hom
243	
244	local instance isScalarTowerSectionBasicOpen {U : X.Opens} (f : Γ(X, U)) :
245	    IsScalarTower Γ(X, U) Γ(X, X.basicOpen f) Γ(G, X.basicOpen f) :=
246	  .of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ => rfl
247	
248	/-- An affine open `V` is an **`e`-presentation chart** for the module `G`
249	if the section module `Γ(G, V)` admits a matrix presentation with `e`
250	generators [Nitsure §4, `n = 0`: the local right-exact presentation
251	`𝒪_V^{⊕m} →ψ 𝒪_V^{⊕e} → F|_V → 0`]. -/
252	def IsPresentationChart (e : ℕ) (V : X.affineOpens) : Prop :=
253	  ∃ mm : ℕ, Nonempty (MatrixPresentation Γ(X, V.1) Γ(G, V.1) e mm)
254	
255	variable [G.IsQuasicoherent]
256	
257	/-- The section module of a quasi-coherent module over a basic open, as a
258	matrix presentation: an `e`-generator presentation over an affine open
259	localizes to an `e`-generator presentation over each basic open, with the
260	restricted relation matrix. -/
261	noncomputable def MatrixPresentationBasicOpen {e mm : ℕ} {V : X.affineOpens}
262	    (P : MatrixPresentation Γ(X, V.1) Γ(G, V.1) e mm) (f : Γ(X, V.1)) :
263	    MatrixPresentation Γ(X, X.basicOpen f) Γ(G, X.basicOpen f) e mm :=
264	  haveI := V.2.isLocalization_basicOpen f
265	  haveI := Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen G V.2 f
266	  (P.baseChange Γ(X, X.basicOpen f)).congr
267	    (IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange (Submonoid.powers f) Γ(X, X.basicOpen f)
268	      (restrictBasicOpenₗ G f)).equiv
269	
270	@[simp] lemma matrixPresentationBasicOpen_relMatrix {e mm : ℕ}
271	    {V : X.affineOpens} (P : MatrixPresentation Γ(X, V.1) Γ(G, V.1) e mm)
272	    (f : Γ(X, V.1)) :
273	    (MatrixPresentationBasicOpen G P f).relMatrix =
274	      P.relMatrix.map (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f)).op).hom :=
275	  rfl
276	
277	/-- Presentation charts are stable under passing to basic opens. -/
278	theorem IsPresentationChart.basicOpen {e : ℕ} {V : X.affineOpens}
279	    (h : IsPresentationChart G e V) (f : Γ(X, V.1)) :
280	    IsPresentationChart G e (X.affineBasicOpen f) := by
281	  obtain ⟨mm, ⟨P⟩⟩ := h
282	  exact ⟨mm, ⟨MatrixPresentationBasicOpen G P f⟩⟩
283	
284	/-- The dimension of the fiber of `G` at a point `x` of an affine open
285	chart `V`: the fiber rank of the section module `Γ(G, V)` at the prime of
286	`x` [Nitsure §4: `e = dim_{κ(s)} F(s)`]. -/
287	noncomputable def chartFiberRank {V : X.affineOpens} (x : X)
288	    (hx : x ∈ V.1) : ℕ :=
289	  ((V.2.primeIdealOf ⟨x, hx⟩).asIdeal).fiberRank Γ(G, V.1)
290	
291	omit [SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent G] in
292	lemma chartFiberRank_congr_chart {V W : X.affineOpens} (h : V = W) (x : X)
293	    (hxV : x ∈ V.1) (hxW : x ∈ W.1) :
294	    chartFiberRank G x hxV = chartFiberRank G x hxW := by
295	  subst h; rfl
296	
297	/-- The chart fiber rank is unchanged by passing to a basic open of the
298	chart. -/
299	theorem chartFiberRank_basicOpen {V : X.affineOpens} (f : Γ(X, V.1))
300	    (x : X) (hx : x ∈ X.basicOpen f) :
301	    chartFiberRank G (V := V) x (X.basicOpen_le f hx) =
302	      chartFiberRank G (V := X.affineBasicOpen f) x hx := by
303	  haveI := V.2.isLocalization_basicOpen f
304	  haveI := Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen G V.2 f
305	  have hloc := Ideal.fiberRank_of_isLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
306	    (restrictBasicOpenₗ G f)
307	    (((V.2.basicOpen f).primeIdealOf ⟨x, hx⟩).asIdeal)
308	  have hle : X.basicOpen f ≤ (𝟙 X : X ⟶ X) ⁻¹ᵁ V.1 := X.basicOpen_le f
309	  have h1 := IsAffineOpen.comap_primeIdealOf_appLE (f := (𝟙 X : X ⟶ X))
310	    V.1 V.2 (X.basicOpen f) (V.2.basicOpen f) hle hx
311	  rw [Scheme.id_appLE] at h1
312	  have hpt : V.2.primeIdealOf ⟨(𝟙 X : X ⟶ X) x, hle hx⟩ =
313	      V.2.primeIdealOf ⟨x, X.basicOpen_le f hx⟩ := by
314	    congr 1
315	  have hcomap :
316	      (((V.2.basicOpen f).primeIdealOf ⟨x, hx⟩).asIdeal).comap
317	          (algebraMap Γ(X, V.1) Γ(X, X.basicOpen f)) =
318	        (V.2.primeIdealOf ⟨x, X.basicOpen_le f hx⟩).asIdeal := by
319	    have h2 : algebraMap Γ(X, V.1) Γ(X, X.basicOpen f) =
320	        (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f)).op).hom := rfl
321	    rw [h2, ← hpt]
322	    exact congrArg PrimeSpectrum.asIdeal h1
323	  calc chartFiberRank G (V := V) x (X.basicOpen_le f hx)
324	      = ((((V.2.basicOpen f).primeIdealOf ⟨x, hx⟩).asIdeal).comap
325	          (algebraMap Γ(X, V.1) Γ(X, X.basicOpen f))).fiberRank Γ(G, V.1) :=
326	        Ideal.fiberRank_congr_ideal hcomap.symm
327	    _ = (((V.2.basicOpen f).primeIdealOf ⟨x, hx⟩).asIdeal).fiberRank
328	          Γ(G, X.basicOpen f) := hloc.symm
329	    _ = chartFiberRank G (V := X.affineBasicOpen f) x hx := rfl
330	
331	/-- **Chart-independence of the fiber rank**: two affine charts through the
332	same point give the same fiber rank.  Proof: refine to a common basic open
333	(`exists_basicOpen_le_affine_inter`) and localize both sides. -/
334	theorem chartFiberRank_eq {V W : X.affineOpens} (x : X)
335	    (hxV : x ∈ V.1) (hxW : x ∈ W.1) :
336	    chartFiberRank G x hxV = chartFiberRank G x hxW := by
337	  obtain ⟨f, g, hfg, hxf⟩ :=
338	    exists_basicOpen_le_affine_inter V.2 W.2 x ⟨hxV, hxW⟩
339	  have hxg : x ∈ X.basicOpen g := by rw [← hfg]; exact hxf
340	  have h1 := chartFiberRank_basicOpen G (V := V) f x hxf
341	  have h2 := chartFiberRank_basicOpen G (V := W) g x hxg
342	  have h3 : chartFiberRank G (V := X.affineBasicOpen f) x hxf =
343	      chartFiberRank G (V := X.affineBasicOpen g) x hxg :=
344	    chartFiberRank_congr_chart G (Subtype.ext hfg) x hxf hxg
345	  calc chartFiberRank G x hxV
346	      = chartFiberRank G (V := V) x (X.basicOpen_le f hxf) := rfl
347	    _ = chartFiberRank G (V := X.affineBasicOpen f) x hxf := h1
348	    _ = chartFiberRank G (V := X.affineBasicOpen g) x hxg := h3
349	    _ = chartFiberRank G (V := W) x (X.basicOpen_le g hxg) := h2.symm
350	    _ = chartFiberRank G x hxW := rfl
351	
352	variable (X) in
353	/-- The **point rank** of a quasi-coherent module: the dimension of its
354	fiber at the point, computed through any affine chart
355	(`pointRank_eq_chartFiberRank`). -/
356	noncomputable def pointRank (x : X) : ℕ :=
357	  chartFiberRank G x (V := ⟨(exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset
358	      (x := x) (U := ⊤) trivial).choose,
359	      (exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (x := x) (U := ⊤)
360	        trivial).choose_spec.1⟩)
361	    (exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (x := x) (U := ⊤)
362	      trivial).choose_spec.2.1
363	
364	theorem pointRank_eq_chartFiberRank {V : X.affineOpens} (x : X)
365	    (hx : x ∈ V.1) : pointRank X G x = chartFiberRank G x hx :=
366	  chartFiberRank_eq G x _ hx
367	
368	/-! ## §4 Locality and chart-independence of entry-ideal membership -/
369	
370	omit [SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent G] in
371	/-- Restriction of sections is transitive (any parallel pair of `Opens`
372	homs agree, so the composite collapses). -/
373	lemma presheaf_res_res {A B C : X.Opens} (j : B ⟶ A) (i : C ⟶ B)
374	    (k : C ⟶ A) (x : Γ(X, A)) :
375	    X.presheaf.map i.op (X.presheaf.map j.op x) = X.presheaf.map k.op x := by
376	  rw [← CommRingCat.comp_apply, ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp,
377	    Subsingleton.elim (i ≫ j) k]
378	
379	/-- Transport of a matrix presentation of the sections of `G` along an
380	equality of opens. -/
381	def transportOpens {U V : X.Opens} (h : U = V) {e mm : ℕ}
382	    (P : MatrixPresentation Γ(X, U) Γ(G, U) e mm) :
383	    MatrixPresentation Γ(X, V) Γ(G, V) e mm := h ▸ P
384	
385	omit [SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent G] in
386	/-- The entry ideal of a transported presentation is the image of the
387	entry ideal under the `eqToHom` restriction. -/
388	lemma entryIdeal_transportOpens {U V : X.Opens} (h : U = V) {e mm : ℕ}
389	    (P : MatrixPresentation Γ(X, U) Γ(G, U) e mm) :
390	    (transportOpens G h P).entryIdeal =
391	      P.entryIdeal.map (X.presheaf.map (eqToHom h.symm).op).hom := by
392	  subst h
393	  simp only [transportOpens, eqToHom_refl, op_id, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]
394	  exact (Ideal.map_id _).symm
395	
396	variable {e : ℕ}
397	
398	/-- The entry ideal of the basic-open restriction of a presentation is the
399	image ideal of the entry ideal. -/
400	lemma entryIdeal_matrixPresentationBasicOpen {mm : ℕ} {V : X.affineOpens}
401	    (P : MatrixPresentation Γ(X, V.1) Γ(G, V.1) e mm) (f : Γ(X, V.1)) :
402	    (MatrixPresentationBasicOpen G P f).entryIdeal =
403	      P.entryIdeal.map (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f)).op).hom := by
404	  haveI := V.2.isLocalization_basicOpen f
405	  haveI := Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen G V.2 f
406	  rw [MatrixPresentationBasicOpen, Module.MatrixPresentation.congr_entryIdeal,
407	    Module.MatrixPresentation.entryIdeal_baseChange]
408	  rfl
409	
410	/-- **Locality of entry-ideal membership on a chart**: if every point of
411	the chart has a basic open on which the restriction of `r` lands in the
412	entry ideal of the restricted presentation, then `r` is in the entry
413	ideal. -/
414	theorem mem_entryIdeal_of_locally {mm : ℕ} {V : X.affineOpens}
415	    (P : MatrixPresentation Γ(X, V.1) Γ(G, V.1) e mm) (r : Γ(X, V.1))
416	    (H : ∀ x ∈ V.1, ∃ g : Γ(X, V.1), x ∈ X.basicOpen g ∧
417	      X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le g)).op r ∈
418	        (MatrixPresentationBasicOpen G P g).entryIdeal) :
419	    r ∈ P.entryIdeal := by
420	  classical
421	  choose g hxg hgmem using H
422	  -- the chosen basic opens cover the chart, so the `g x` span the unit
423	  -- ideal
424	  have hspan : Ideal.span (Set.range fun x : V.1 => g x.1 x.2) = ⊤ := by
425	    rw [← V.2.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff]
426	    refine le_antisymm (iSup_le fun r => X.basicOpen_le _) fun y hy => ?_
427	    refine TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mpr
428	      ⟨⟨_, ⟨⟨y, hy⟩, rfl⟩⟩, hxg y hy⟩
429	  refine Submodule.mem_of_span_eq_top_of_smul_pow_mem P.entryIdeal _ hspan r
430	    (fun ⟨_, x, rfl⟩ => ?_)
431	  -- membership after localization at `g x` gives a power `g x ^ n • r ∈ I`
432	  haveI := V.2.isLocalization_basicOpen (g x.1 x.2)
433	  have h1 := hgmem x.1 x.2
434	  rw [entryIdeal_matrixPresentationBasicOpen] at h1
435	  have h2 : algebraMap Γ(X, V.1) Γ(X, X.basicOpen (g x.1 x.2)) r ∈
436	      P.entryIdeal.map (algebraMap Γ(X, V.1) Γ(X, X.basicOpen (g x.1 x.2))) := h1
437	  obtain ⟨s, hs, hmem⟩ :=
438	    (IsLocalization.algebraMap_mem_map_algebraMap_iff
439	      (Submonoid.powers (g x.1 x.2)) _ _ _).mp h2
440	  obtain ⟨n, rfl⟩ := hs
441	  exact ⟨n, by simpa [smul_eq_mul] using hmem⟩
442	
443	omit [SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent G] in
444	/-- Restriction along `U ≤ U` is the identity. -/
445	lemma presheaf_map_self {U : X.Opens} (h : U ≤ U) (r : Γ(X, U)) :
446	    X.presheaf.map (homOfLE h).op r = r := by
447	  rw [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE h) (𝟙 U), op_id,
448	    CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]
449	  rfl
450	
451	/-- **Chart-to-chart transfer of the entry-ideal condition**: if the
452	restriction of `r : Γ(X, U)` to a presentation chart `W ≤ U` lies in the
453	entry ideal of `P_W`, then at every point of `W ⊓ V` for a second chart
454	`V ≤ U` there is a basic open of `V` on which the restriction of `r` lies
455	in the entry ideal of the restricted `P_V`.  Presentation-independence of
456	the entry ideal (`Module.MatrixPresentation.entryIdeal_eq`) at a common
457	basic open is the engine. -/
458	theorem exists_basicOpen_mem_entryIdeal_of_chart {U : X.Opens}
459	    (r : Γ(X, U)) {V W : X.affineOpens} (hVU : V.1 ≤ U) (hWU : W.1 ≤ U)
460	    {mmV mmW : ℕ}
461	    (PV : MatrixPresentation Γ(X, V.1) Γ(G, V.1) e mmV)
462	    (PW : MatrixPresentation Γ(X, W.1) Γ(G, W.1) e mmW)
463	    (hW : X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hWU).op r ∈ PW.entryIdeal)
464	    (x : X) (hxV : x ∈ V.1) (hxW : x ∈ W.1) :
465	    ∃ g : Γ(X, V.1), x ∈ X.basicOpen g ∧
466	      X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le g)).op
467	          (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hVU).op r) ∈
468	        (MatrixPresentationBasicOpen G PV g).entryIdeal := by
469	  obtain ⟨f, g, hfg, hxf⟩ :=
470	    exists_basicOpen_le_affine_inter W.2 V.2 x ⟨hxW, hxV⟩
471	  have hbofU : X.basicOpen f ≤ U := (X.basicOpen_le f).trans hWU
472	  have hbogU : X.basicOpen g ≤ U := (X.basicOpen_le g).trans hVU
473	  refine ⟨g, hfg ▸ hxf, ?_⟩
474	  -- rewrite the doubly-restricted section as an `eqToHom`-transport of the
475	  -- `W`-side restriction
476	  have hz : X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le g)).op
477	      (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hVU).op r) =
478	      X.presheaf.map (eqToHom hfg.symm).op
479	        (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f)).op
480	          (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hWU).op r)) := by
481	    rw [presheaf_res_res (homOfLE hVU)
482	      (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le g)) (homOfLE hbogU),
483	      presheaf_res_res (homOfLE hWU)
484	      (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f)) (homOfLE hbofU),
485	      presheaf_res_res (homOfLE hbofU)
486	      (eqToHom hfg.symm) (homOfLE hbogU)]
487	  rw [hz,
488	    ← Module.MatrixPresentation.entryIdeal_eq
489	      (transportOpens G hfg (MatrixPresentationBasicOpen G PW f))
490	      (MatrixPresentationBasicOpen G PV g),
491	    entryIdeal_transportOpens]
492	  refine Ideal.mem_map_of_mem _ ?_
493	  rw [entryIdeal_matrixPresentationBasicOpen]
494	  exact Ideal.mem_map_of_mem _ hW
495	
496	/-! ## §5 The strata ideal -/
497	
498	variable (e) in
499	/-- The rank-`e` **strata ideal** at an affine open `U`: the intersection,
500	over all `e`-generator presentation charts `V ≤ U`, of the preimages of the
501	entry ideals under restriction [Nitsure §4, `n = 0`: the ideal sheaf of the
502	canonical scheme structure on the rank-`e` locus]. -/
503	noncomputable def strataIdeal (U : X.affineOpens) : Ideal Γ(X, U.1) :=
504	  ⨅ (V : X.affineOpens) (h : V.1 ≤ U.1) (mm : ℕ)
505	    (P : MatrixPresentation Γ(X, V.1) Γ(G, V.1) e mm),
506	    P.entryIdeal.comap (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE h).op).hom
507	
508	omit [SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent G] in
509	lemma mem_strataIdeal_iff {U : X.affineOpens} {r : Γ(X, U.1)} :
510	    r ∈ strataIdeal G e U ↔ ∀ (V : X.affineOpens) (h : V.1 ≤ U.1) (mm : ℕ)
511	      (P : MatrixPresentation Γ(X, V.1) Γ(G, V.1) e mm),
512	      X.presheaf.map (homOfLE h).op r ∈ P.entryIdeal := by
513	  simp only [strataIdeal, Submodule.mem_iInf, Ideal.mem_comap]
514	
515	/-- **Evaluation of the strata ideal on a presentation chart**: on an
516	`e`-presentation chart the strata ideal is the entry ideal of any (hence
517	every) `e`-generator presentation. -/
518	theorem strataIdeal_eq_entryIdeal {V : X.affineOpens} {mm : ℕ}
519	    (P : MatrixPresentation Γ(X, V.1) Γ(G, V.1) e mm) :
520	    strataIdeal G e V = P.entryIdeal := by
521	  refine le_antisymm ?_ ?_
522	  · intro r hr
523	    have h := (mem_strataIdeal_iff G).mp hr V le_rfl mm P
524	    rwa [presheaf_map_self] at h
525	  · intro r hr
526	    rw [mem_strataIdeal_iff]
527	    intro W hWV mmW Q
528	    refine mem_entryIdeal_of_locally G Q _ (fun x hx => ?_)
529	    exact exists_basicOpen_mem_entryIdeal_of_chart G r hWV le_rfl Q P
530	      (by rwa [presheaf_map_self]) x hx (hWV hx)
531	
532	/-- Membership in the strata ideal follows from membership at a family of
533	charts covering `U`. -/
534	theorem mem_strataIdeal_of_charts {U : X.affineOpens} (r : Γ(X, U.1))
535	    {ι : Type*} (Vc : ι → X.affineOpens) (hVcU : ∀ i, (Vc i).1 ≤ U.1)
536	    (mmc : ι → ℕ)
537	    (Pc : ∀ i, MatrixPresentation Γ(X, (Vc i).1) Γ(G, (Vc i).1) e (mmc i))
538	    (hmem : ∀ i, X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hVcU i)).op r ∈ (Pc i).entryIdeal)
539	    (hcov : ∀ x ∈ U.1, ∃ i, x ∈ (Vc i).1) :
540	    r ∈ strataIdeal G e U := by
541	  rw [mem_strataIdeal_iff]
542	  intro W hWU mmW Q
543	  refine mem_entryIdeal_of_locally G Q _ (fun x hx => ?_)
544	  obtain ⟨i, hxi⟩ := hcov x (hWU hx)
545	  exact exists_basicOpen_mem_entryIdeal_of_chart G r hWU (hVcU i) Q (Pc i)
546	    (hmem i) x hx hxi
547	
548	variable (e) in
549	/-- The standing hypothesis of the strata construction: every point of `X`
550	lies in an `e`-presentation chart.  Holds on the union of all
551	`e`-presentation charts, the ambient space of the rank-`e` stratum. -/
552	def ChartsCover : Prop :=
553	  ∀ x : X, ∃ V : X.affineOpens, x ∈ V.1 ∧ IsPresentationChart G e V
554	
555	/-- Under `ChartsCover`, every affine open of `X` is covered by finitely
556	many `e`-presentation charts contained in it. -/
557	theorem ChartsCover.exists_finite_charts (hcov : ChartsCover G e)
558	    (U : X.affineOpens) :
559	    ∃ (n : ℕ) (Vc : Fin n → X.affineOpens) (mmc : Fin n → ℕ)
560	      (Pc : ∀ i, MatrixPresentation Γ(X, (Vc i).1) Γ(G, (Vc i).1) e (mmc i)),
561	      (∀ i, (Vc i).1 ≤ U.1) ∧ ∀ x ∈ U.1, ∃ i, x ∈ (Vc i).1 := by
562	  classical
563	  -- the subtype of presentation charts contained in `U`
564	  set J := {V : X.affineOpens // V.1 ≤ U.1 ∧ IsPresentationChart G e V}
565	  -- they cover `U`: shrink a global chart through a simultaneous basic open
566	  have hJcov : (U.1 : Set X) ⊆ ⋃ j : J, ((j.1.1 : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) : Set X) := by
567	    intro x hx
568	    obtain ⟨V, hxV, hVchart⟩ := hcov x
569	    obtain ⟨f, g, hfg, hxf⟩ :=
570	      exists_basicOpen_le_affine_inter U.2 V.2 x ⟨hx, hxV⟩
571	    have hle : (X.affineBasicOpen g).1 ≤ U.1 := by
572	      show X.basicOpen g ≤ U.1
573	      rw [← hfg]; exact X.basicOpen_le f
574	    refine Set.mem_iUnion.mpr
575	      ⟨⟨X.affineBasicOpen g, hle, hVchart.basicOpen G g⟩, ?_⟩
576	    show x ∈ X.basicOpen g
577	    rw [← hfg]; exact hxf
578	  -- extract a finite subcover by quasi-compactness of the affine open
579	  obtain ⟨T, hT⟩ := U.2.isCompact.elim_finite_subcover
580	    (fun j : J => ((j.1.1 : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) : Set X))
581	    (fun j => j.1.1.2) hJcov
582	  -- enumerate and choose presentations
583	  obtain ⟨n, eT⟩ : ∃ n, Nonempty (T ≃ Fin n) := ⟨T.card, ⟨T.equivFin⟩⟩
584	  obtain ⟨eT⟩ := eT
585	  choose mmc Pc using fun j : T => (j.1.2.2 : IsPresentationChart G e j.1.1)
586	  refine ⟨n, fun i => (eT.symm i).1.1, fun i => mmc (eT.symm i),
587	    fun i => (Pc (eT.symm i)).some, fun i => (eT.symm i).1.2.1,
588	    fun x hx => ?_⟩
589	  obtain ⟨j, hjT, hxj⟩ := Set.mem_iUnion₂.mp (hT hx)
590	  exact ⟨eT ⟨j, hjT⟩, by simpa using hxj⟩
591	
592	/-- Restriction to the relative basic open computes the localization
593	fraction: restricting `mk' y (f₀ ^ n)` from `X.basicOpen f₀` to the basic
594	open of `f₀|_V` inside a smaller affine `V` gives
595	`mk' (y|_V) ((f₀|_V) ^ n)`. -/
596	theorem res_mk'_basicOpen {U : X.affineOpens} (f₀ : Γ(X, U.1))
597	    {V : X.affineOpens} (hVU : V.1 ≤ U.1) (y : Γ(X, U.1)) (n : ℕ)
598	    (h₁ : X.basicOpen (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hVU).op f₀) ≤ X.basicOpen f₀) :
599	    haveI := U.2.isLocalization_basicOpen f₀
600	    haveI := V.2.isLocalization_basicOpen (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hVU).op f₀)
601	    X.presheaf.map (homOfLE h₁).op
602	        (IsLocalization.mk' Γ(X, X.basicOpen f₀) y
603	          (⟨f₀ ^ n, pow_mem (Submonoid.mem_powers f₀) n⟩ :
604	            Submonoid.powers f₀)) =
605	      IsLocalization.mk' Γ(X, X.basicOpen (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hVU).op f₀))
606	        (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hVU).op y)
607	        (⟨(X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hVU).op f₀) ^ n,
608	          pow_mem (Submonoid.mem_powers _) n⟩ :
609	          Submonoid.powers (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hVU).op f₀)) := by
610	  haveI := U.2.isLocalization_basicOpen f₀
611	  haveI := V.2.isLocalization_basicOpen (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hVU).op f₀)
612	  set fv := X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hVU).op f₀ with hfv
613	  rw [eq_comm, IsLocalization.mk'_eq_iff_eq_mul]
614	  -- both sides are restrictions from `Γ(X, X.basicOpen f₀)`
615	  have hbase : (algebraMap Γ(X, V.1) Γ(X, X.basicOpen fv)) =
616	      (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le fv)).op).hom := rfl
617	  have hcalc : ∀ z : Γ(X, U.1),
618	      algebraMap Γ(X, V.1) Γ(X, X.basicOpen fv)
619	          (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hVU).op z) =
620	        X.presheaf.map (homOfLE h₁).op
621	          (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f₀)).op z) := by
622	    intro z
623	    rw [hbase]
624	    show X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le fv)).op
625	        (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hVU).op z) = _
626	    rw [presheaf_res_res (homOfLE hVU) (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le fv))
627	        (homOfLE ((X.basicOpen_le fv).trans hVU)),
628	      presheaf_res_res (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f₀)) (homOfLE h₁)
629	        (homOfLE ((X.basicOpen_le fv).trans hVU))]
630	  rw [hcalc y]
631	  have hmul : (algebraMap Γ(X, V.1) Γ(X, X.basicOpen fv)) ↑(⟨fv ^ n,
632	      pow_mem (Submonoid.mem_powers _) n⟩ : Submonoid.powers fv) =
633	      X.presheaf.map (homOfLE h₁).op
634	        (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f₀)).op (f₀ ^ n)) := by
635	    show algebraMap Γ(X, V.1) Γ(X, X.basicOpen fv) (fv ^ n) = _
636	    rw [map_pow, hcalc f₀, ← map_pow, ← map_pow]
637	  rw [hmul, ← map_mul]
638	  congr 1
639	  -- `mk' y (f₀^n) * (f₀^n)|_{D₀} = y|_{D₀}` is the defining property
640	  have hspec := IsLocalization.mk'_spec Γ(X, X.basicOpen f₀) y
641	    (⟨f₀ ^ n, pow_mem (Submonoid.mem_powers f₀) n⟩ : Submonoid.powers f₀)
642	  calc X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f₀)).op y
643	      = IsLocalization.mk' Γ(X, X.basicOpen f₀) y
644	          (⟨f₀ ^ n, pow_mem (Submonoid.mem_powers f₀) n⟩ :
645	            Submonoid.powers f₀) *
646	        algebraMap Γ(X, U.1) Γ(X, X.basicOpen f₀) (f₀ ^ n) := hspec.symm
647	    _ = _ := rfl
648	
649	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
650	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
651	/-- **The strata ideal localizes along basic opens** (quasi-coherence of
652	the strata ideal; the `map_ideal_basicOpen` field of the ideal-sheaf
653	datum).  [Nitsure §4, `n = 0`: the local strata glue by their universal
654	property.] -/
655	theorem map_strataIdeal_basicOpen (hcov : ChartsCover G e)
656	    (U : X.affineOpens) (f₀ : Γ(X, U.1)) :
657	    (strataIdeal G e U).map
658	        (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f₀)).op).hom =
659	      strataIdeal G e (X.affineBasicOpen f₀) := by
660	  haveI := U.2.isLocalization_basicOpen f₀
661	  refine le_antisymm ?_ ?_
662	  · -- restriction preserves the chart conditions
663	    rw [Ideal.map_le_iff_le_comap]
664	    intro r hr
665	    refine (mem_strataIdeal_iff G).mpr (fun W hWD mmW Q => ?_)
666	    have hWU : W.1 ≤ U.1 := hWD.trans (X.basicOpen_le f₀)
667	    have h := (mem_strataIdeal_iff G).mp hr W hWU mmW Q
668	    have heq := presheaf_res_res (X := X) (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f₀))
669	      (homOfLE hWD) (homOfLE hWU) r
670	    show X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hWD).op
671	      (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f₀)).op r) ∈ Q.entryIdeal
672	    exact heq.symm ▸ h
673	  · -- a section satisfying the conditions over the basic open comes from
674	    -- the strata ideal after clearing a power of `f₀`
675	    intro r' hr'
676	    obtain ⟨⟨y, s⟩, hys⟩ := IsLocalization.mk'_surjective
677	      (M := Submonoid.powers f₀) (S := Γ(X, X.basicOpen f₀)) r'
678	    dsimp only at hys
679	    obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := s.2
680	    have hs : s = ⟨f₀ ^ n, pow_mem (Submonoid.mem_powers f₀) n⟩ :=
681	      Subtype.ext hn.symm
682	    rw [hs] at hys
683	    -- finitely many charts covering `U`
684	    obtain ⟨N, Vc, mmc, Pc, hVcU, hcovU⟩ :=
685	      ChartsCover.exists_finite_charts G hcov U
686	    -- per chart, a power of `f₀` clears the denominator
687	    have hper : ∀ i : Fin N, ∃ m : ℕ,
688	        X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hVcU i)).op (f₀ ^ m * y) ∈
689	          (Pc i).entryIdeal := by
690	      intro i
691	      haveI := (Vc i).2.isLocalization_basicOpen
692	        (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hVcU i)).op f₀)
693	      have h₁ : X.basicOpen (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hVcU i)).op f₀) ≤
694	          X.basicOpen f₀ := by
695	        rw [Scheme.basicOpen_res]
696	        exact inf_le_right
697	      -- the strata condition at the chart `D(f₀|_V) ≤ D(f₀)`
698	      have hi := (mem_strataIdeal_iff G).mp hr'
699	        (X.affineBasicOpen (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hVcU i)).op f₀)) h₁
700	        (mmc i) (MatrixPresentationBasicOpen G (Pc i)
701	          (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hVcU i)).op f₀))
702	      rw [← hys] at hi
703	      have hres := res_mk'_basicOpen f₀ (hVcU i) y n h₁
704	      have hi' := (congrArg (fun z =>
705	        z ∈ (MatrixPresentationBasicOpen G (Pc i)
706	          (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hVcU i)).op f₀)).entryIdeal) hres).mp hi
707	      have hE := entryIdeal_matrixPresentationBasicOpen G (Pc i)
708	        (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hVcU i)).op f₀)
709	      have hi'' := (congrArg (fun I => IsLocalization.mk'
710	        Γ(X, X.basicOpen (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hVcU i)).op f₀))
711	        (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hVcU i)).op y)
712	        (⟨(X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hVcU i)).op f₀) ^ n,
713	          pow_mem (Submonoid.mem_powers _) n⟩ :
714	          Submonoid.powers (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hVcU i)).op f₀)) ∈ I)
715	        hE).mp hi'
716	      obtain ⟨t, ht, hmem⟩ :=
717	        (IsLocalization.mk'_mem_map_algebraMap_iff
718	          (Submonoid.powers (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hVcU i)).op f₀))
719	          _ _ _ _).mp hi''
720	      obtain ⟨m, rfl⟩ := ht
721	      refine ⟨m, ?_⟩
722	      rw [map_mul, map_pow]
723	      exact hmem
724	    choose mc hmc using hper
725	    set m := Finset.univ.sup mc with hm
726	    have hmem : ∀ i : Fin N,
727	        X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hVcU i)).op (f₀ ^ m * y) ∈
728	          (Pc i).entryIdeal := by
729	      intro i
730	      have hle : mc i ≤ m := Finset.le_sup (Finset.mem_univ i)
731	      have hsplit : f₀ ^ m * y = f₀ ^ (m - mc i) * (f₀ ^ mc i * y) := by
732	        rw [← mul_assoc, ← pow_add, Nat.sub_add_cancel hle]
733	      rw [hsplit, map_mul]
734	      exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (hmc i)
735	    have hfin : f₀ ^ m * y ∈ strataIdeal G e U :=
736	      mem_strataIdeal_of_charts G _ Vc hVcU mmc Pc hmem hcovU
737	    have := (IsLocalization.mk'_mem_map_algebraMap_iff (Submonoid.powers f₀)
738	      Γ(X, X.basicOpen f₀) (strataIdeal G e U) y
739	      (⟨f₀ ^ n, pow_mem (Submonoid.mem_powers f₀) n⟩ :
740	        Submonoid.powers f₀)).mpr
741	      ⟨f₀ ^ m, pow_mem (Submonoid.mem_powers f₀) m, hfin⟩
742	    rw [hys] at this
743	    exact this
744	
745	/-! ## §6 The rank stratum as a closed subscheme -/
746	
747	variable (e) in
748	/-- The rank-`e` **strata ideal sheaf datum** [Nitsure §4, `n = 0`: the
749	canonical scheme structure on the rank-`e` stratum]. -/
750	noncomputable def strataData (hcov : ChartsCover G e) : X.IdealSheafData where
751	  ideal := strataIdeal G e
752	  map_ideal_basicOpen U f₀ := map_strataIdeal_basicOpen G hcov U f₀
753	
754	@[simp] lemma strataData_ideal (hcov : ChartsCover G e) (U : X.affineOpens) :
755	    (strataData G e hcov).ideal U = strataIdeal G e U := rfl
756	
757	variable (e) in
758	/-- The **rank-`e` stratum** of a quasi-coherent module `G`: the closed
759	subscheme of `X` cut out by the entry ideals of local `e`-generator
760	presentations. -/
761	noncomputable def stratum (hcov : ChartsCover G e) : Scheme.{u} :=
762	  (strataData G e hcov).subscheme
763	
764	variable (e) in
765	/-- The closed immersion of the rank-`e` stratum. -/
766	noncomputable def stratumι (hcov : ChartsCover G e) :
767	    stratum G e hcov ⟶ X :=
768	  (strataData G e hcov).subschemeι
769	
770	instance (hcov : ChartsCover G e) :
771	    IsClosedImmersion (stratumι G e hcov) :=
772	  inferInstanceAs (IsClosedImmersion (strataData G e hcov).subschemeι)
773	
774	/-- **The support of the rank-`e` stratum is the rank-`e` locus**: a point
775	lies in the image of the stratum iff the fiber of `G` there has dimension
776	exactly `e` [Nitsure §4, part (i), `n = 0` case]. -/
777	theorem mem_range_stratumι_iff (hcov : ChartsCover G e) (x : X) :
778	    x ∈ Set.range (stratumι G e hcov) ↔ pointRank X G x = e := by
779	  obtain ⟨V, hxV, hchart⟩ := hcov x
780	  obtain ⟨mm, ⟨P⟩⟩ := hchart
781	  have h0 : x ∈ Set.range (stratumι G e hcov) ↔
782	      x ∈ (strataData G e hcov).support := by
783	    change x ∈ Set.range ((strataData G e hcov).subschemeι) ↔ _
784	    rw [Scheme.IdealSheafData.range_subschemeι]
785	    exact Iff.rfl
786	  have h1 : x ∈ (strataData G e hcov).support ↔
787	      x ∈ X.zeroLocus (U := V.1)
788	        (((strataData G e hcov).ideal V : Ideal Γ(X, V.1)) :
789	          Set Γ(X, V.1)) :=
790	    Scheme.IdealSheafData.mem_support_iff_of_mem hxV
791	  have hIP : ((strataData G e hcov).ideal V : Ideal Γ(X, V.1)) =
792	      P.entryIdeal := strataIdeal_eq_entryIdeal G P
793	  have h2 : x ∈ X.zeroLocus (U := V.1)
794	      (((strataData G e hcov).ideal V : Ideal Γ(X, V.1)) : Set Γ(X, V.1)) ↔
795	      P.entryIdeal ≤ (V.2.primeIdealOf ⟨x, hxV⟩).asIdeal := by
796	    rw [hIP]
797	    exact V.2.mem_zeroLocus_iff_le_primeIdealOf P.entryIdeal ⟨x, hxV⟩
798	  have h3 : P.entryIdeal ≤ (V.2.primeIdealOf ⟨x, hxV⟩).asIdeal ↔
799	      pointRank X G x = e := by
800	    rw [P.entryIdeal_le_prime_iff, pointRank_eq_chartFiberRank G x hxV]
801	    exact Iff.rfl
802	  exact ((h0.trans h1).trans h2).trans h3
803	
804	end Scheme.Modules
805	
806	end AlgebraicGeometry
