63	universe u v u' v'
64	
65	open TensorProduct
66	
67	namespace Module
68	
69	variable {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
70	
71	/-- A **matrix presentation** of the `R`-module `M` by `e` generators and
72	`m` relations: a relation matrix `ψ : Matrix (Fin e) (Fin m) R` and a
73	surjection `π : R^e → M` whose kernel is the column span of `ψ`, i.e.
74	`R^m —ψ→ R^e —π→ M → 0` is exact.  [Nitsure §4, n = 0 case: the local
75	presentation `𝒪_V^{⊕m} →ψ 𝒪_V^{⊕e} → F|_V → 0`.] -/
76	structure MatrixPresentation (R : Type u) [CommRing R]
77	    (M : Type v) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] (e m : ℕ) where
78	  /-- The relation matrix `ψ`. -/
79	  relMatrix : Matrix (Fin e) (Fin m) R
80	  /-- The projection `R^e → M` picking out the `e` generators. -/
81	  proj : (Fin e → R) →ₗ[R] M
82	  surjective_proj : Function.Surjective proj
83	  exact_mulVecLin_proj : Function.Exact relMatrix.mulVecLin proj
84	
85	namespace MatrixPresentation
86	
87	variable {e m m' : ℕ}
88	
89	lemma ker_proj (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m) :
90	    LinearMap.ker P.proj = LinearMap.range P.relMatrix.mulVecLin :=
91	  LinearMap.exact_iff.mp P.exact_mulVecLin_proj
92	
93	/-- A module admitting a matrix presentation is finitely presented. -/
94	lemma finitePresentation (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m) :
95	    Module.FinitePresentation R M := by
96	  refine Module.finitePresentation_of_surjective P.proj P.surjective_proj ?_
97	  rw [P.ker_proj, LinearMap.range_eq_map]
98	  exact Module.Finite.fg_top.map _
99	
100	/-- The **entry ideal** of a matrix presentation: the ideal generated by all
101	entries of the relation matrix (the ideal of `1 × 1` minors; for an
102	`e`-generator presentation this is the Fitting ideal `Fitt_{e-1} M`).
103	[Nitsure §4: "the ideal sheaf generated by the entries `ψ_{i,j}`".] -/
104	def entryIdeal (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m) : Ideal R :=
105	  Ideal.span (Set.range fun ij : Fin e × Fin m ↦ P.relMatrix ij.1 ij.2)
106	
107	lemma entryIdeal_le_iff {P : MatrixPresentation R M e m} {I : Ideal R} :
108	    P.entryIdeal ≤ I ↔ ∀ i j, P.relMatrix i j ∈ I := by
109	  rw [entryIdeal, Ideal.span_le]
110	  constructor
111	  · intro h i j
112	    exact h ⟨(i, j), rfl⟩
113	  · rintro h _ ⟨⟨i, j⟩, rfl⟩
114	    exact h i j
115	
116	lemma relMatrix_mem_entryIdeal (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m)
117	    (i : Fin e) (j : Fin m) : P.relMatrix i j ∈ P.entryIdeal :=
118	  entryIdeal_le_iff.mp le_rfl i j
119	
120	section BaseChange
121	
122	variable (A : Type u') [CommRing A] [Algebra R A]
123	
124	/-- Vanishing of the base-changed relation matrix is containment of the
125	entry ideal in the kernel: `ψ ⊗ A = 0 ↔ I(ψ) ⊆ ker (R → A)`. -/
126	lemma relMatrix_map_eq_zero_iff {P : MatrixPresentation R M e m} :
127	    P.relMatrix.map (algebraMap R A) = 0 ↔
128	      P.entryIdeal ≤ RingHom.ker (algebraMap R A) := by
129	  rw [entryIdeal_le_iff, ← Matrix.ext_iff]
130	  simp [RingHom.mem_ker]
131	
132	/-- Base change intertwines `mulVecLin` of the mapped matrix with the
133	base-changed `mulVecLin`, through the scalar-extension isomorphisms
134	`A ⊗[R] (ι → R) ≃ (ι → A)`. -/
135	lemma mulVecLin_map_algebraMap (ψ : Matrix (Fin e) (Fin m) R) :
136	    (ψ.map (algebraMap R A)).mulVecLin =
137	      (TensorProduct.piScalarRight R A A (Fin e)).toLinearMap
138	        ∘ₗ ψ.mulVecLin.baseChange A
139	        ∘ₗ (TensorProduct.piScalarRight R A A (Fin m)).symm.toLinearMap := by
140	  refine LinearMap.pi_ext' fun j ↦ LinearMap.ext_ring ?_
141	  ext i
142	  simp only [LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearMap.coe_single, LinearEquiv.coe_toLinearMap,
143	    TensorProduct.piScalarRight_symm_single, LinearMap.baseChange_tmul,
144	    TensorProduct.piScalarRight_apply, TensorProduct.piScalarRightHom_tmul,
145	    Matrix.mulVecLin_apply, Matrix.mulVec, Matrix.map_apply]
146	  rw [dotProduct_single, dotProduct_single]
147	  simp [Algebra.smul_def]
148	
149	/-- The base change of a matrix presentation along an `R`-algebra `A`:
150	`A^m —ψ⊗A→ A^e —π⊗A→ A ⊗[R] M → 0` (right-exactness of the tensor
151	product).  [Nitsure §4: "the pulled-back sequence is exact by
152	right-exactness of tensor products".] -/
153	noncomputable def baseChange (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m) :
154	    MatrixPresentation A (A ⊗[R] M) e m where
155	  relMatrix := P.relMatrix.map (algebraMap R A)
156	  proj := P.proj.baseChange A ∘ₗ
157	    (TensorProduct.piScalarRight R A A (Fin e)).symm.toLinearMap
158	  surjective_proj := by
159	    have h1 : Function.Surjective (P.proj.baseChange A) := by
160	      rw [LinearMap.baseChange_eq_ltensor]
161	      exact LinearMap.lTensor_surjective A P.surjective_proj
162	    exact h1.comp (TensorProduct.piScalarRight R A A (Fin e)).symm.surjective
163	  exact_mulVecLin_proj := by
164	    rw [mulVecLin_map_algebraMap]
165	    have hex : Function.Exact (P.relMatrix.mulVecLin.baseChange A)
166	        (P.proj.baseChange A) := by
167	      simp only [LinearMap.baseChange_eq_ltensor]
168	      exact lTensor_exact A P.exact_mulVecLin_proj P.surjective_proj
169	    intro y
170	    simp only [LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearEquiv.coe_toLinearMap]
171	    rw [hex ((TensorProduct.piScalarRight R A A (Fin e)).symm y)]
172	    constructor
173	    · rintro ⟨x, hx⟩
174	      refine ⟨TensorProduct.piScalarRight R A A (Fin m) x, ?_⟩
175	      simp only [LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearEquiv.coe_toLinearMap]
176	      rw [LinearEquiv.symm_apply_apply, hx, LinearEquiv.apply_symm_apply]
177	    · rintro ⟨x, hx⟩
178	      simp only [LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearEquiv.coe_toLinearMap] at hx
179	      refine ⟨(TensorProduct.piScalarRight R A A (Fin m)).symm x, ?_⟩
180	      rw [← hx, LinearEquiv.symm_apply_apply]
181	
182	@[simp]
183	lemma baseChange_relMatrix (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m) :
184	    (P.baseChange A).relMatrix = P.relMatrix.map (algebraMap R A) := rfl
185	
186	/-- The entry ideal of the base-changed presentation is the image of the
187	entry ideal. -/
188	lemma entryIdeal_baseChange (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m) :
189	    (P.baseChange A).entryIdeal = P.entryIdeal.map (algebraMap R A) := by
190	  rw [entryIdeal, entryIdeal, Ideal.map_span]
191	  congr 1
192	  ext x
193	  simp [Matrix.map_apply, eq_comm]
194	
195	end BaseChange
196	
197	section Vanishing
198	
199	/-- If the relation matrix vanishes, the projection of the presentation is
200	an isomorphism `R^e ≃ M`. -/
201	noncomputable def projEquiv (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m)
202	    (h : P.relMatrix = 0) : (Fin e → R) ≃ₗ[R] M :=
203	  LinearEquiv.ofBijective P.proj
204	    ⟨by
205	      rw [← LinearMap.ker_eq_bot, P.ker_proj, h]
206	      simp, P.surjective_proj⟩
207	
208	@[simp]
209	lemma projEquiv_apply (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m) (h : P.relMatrix = 0)
210	    (x : Fin e → R) : P.projEquiv h x = P.proj x := rfl
211	
212	/-- If the relation matrix vanishes, `M` is free (of rank `e`). -/
213	lemma free_of_relMatrix_eq_zero (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m)
214	    (h : P.relMatrix = 0) : Module.Free R M :=
215	  Module.Free.of_equiv (P.projEquiv h)
216	
217	/-- A ring with a prime ideal is nontrivial. -/
218	private lemma nontrivial_of_primeSpectrum (p : PrimeSpectrum R) : Nontrivial R :=
219	  ⟨0, 1, fun h01 ↦ p.isPrime.ne_top
220	    ((Ideal.eq_top_iff_one _).mpr (h01 ▸ p.asIdeal.zero_mem))⟩
221	
222	/-- If the relation matrix vanishes, `M` has rank `e` at every prime. -/
223	lemma rankAtStalk_eq_of_relMatrix_eq_zero (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m)
224	    (h : P.relMatrix = 0) (p : PrimeSpectrum R) :
225	    Module.rankAtStalk M p = e := by
226	  haveI : Nontrivial R := nontrivial_of_primeSpectrum p
227	  calc Module.rankAtStalk M p
228	      = Module.rankAtStalk (R := R) (Fin e → R) p :=
229	        (congrFun (Module.rankAtStalk_eq_of_equiv (P.projEquiv h)) p).symm
230	    _ = e := by simp
231	
232	end Vanishing
233	
234	section HardDirection
235	
236	/-- **The hard direction of Nitsure's local universal property** (the step
237	left implicit in [Nitsure §4]): if `M` is flat and has rank `e` at every
238	prime, then the relation matrix of *any* presentation of `M` by `e`
239	generators vanishes.

1380	namespace Scheme
1381	
1382	/-! ## §1. Coherent-sheaf flatness over a base
1383	
1384	For a morphism `f : X ⟶ S` of schemes and a coherent (more generally:
1385	quasi-coherent) `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕`, the *coherent-sheaf flatness* predicate
1386	`CoherentSheafFlat f 𝓕` captures Stacks 00HB lifted to the sheaf level:
1387	for every point `x ∈ X` the stalk `𝓕_x` is a flat `𝓞_{S, f(x)}`-module
1388	via the composite `𝓞_{S, f(x)} → 𝓞_{X, x} → End(𝓕_x)`.
1389	
1390	Affine-locally (Stacks 00HB equivalence): on an affine open `U = Spec A
1391	⊆ S` and an affine open `V = Spec B ⊆ f⁻¹U` with `𝓕|_V = M̃` for a
1392	finite `B`-module `M`, the condition becomes `Module.Flat A M` with the
1393	`A`-module structure inherited via the ring map `A → B`. The
1394	file-internal encoding uses this affine-local form.
1395	
1396	Blueprint reference: `def:coherent_sheaf_flat`. -/
1397	
1398	/-- A coherent sheaf `𝓕` on `X` is **flat over `S`** for a morphism
1399	`f : X ⟶ S` if, for every affine open `U ⊆ S` and every affine open
1400	`V ⊆ f⁻¹U`, the `𝓞_X`-module of sections `Γ(𝓕, V)` is flat as a
1401	`Γ(S, U)`-module, where the `Γ(S, U)`-action is the restriction of
1402	scalars along the ring morphism `Γ(S, U) → Γ(X, V)` (the `appLE`
1403	morphism of `f`).
1404	
1405	This is the affine-local form of Stacks 00HB (a coherent sheaf is flat
1406	over the base if every stalk is flat over the corresponding base
1407	stalk); the affine-local form and stalk form are equivalent for
1408	*quasi-coherent* sheaves on a noetherian base, which is the regime of
1409	the flattening-stratification theorem.
1410	
1411	iter-177+: the body of subsequent flat-locus theorems may package this
1412	predicate as an instance-driven typeclass `Module.Flat`-style if the
1413	sheafified-tensor infrastructure lands in Mathlib; for the iter-176
1414	file-skeleton it is encoded as a `Prop` quantifier. -/
1415	def CoherentSheafFlat {X S : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules) : Prop :=
1416	  ∀ {U : S.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen U) {V : X.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen V)
1417	    (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U),
1418	    letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V) := Module.compHom _ (f.appLE U V e).hom
1419	    Module.Flat Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V)
1420	
1421	end Scheme
1422	
1423	/-! ## §2. Generic flatness (Nitsure §4) — see `GenericFlatnessGeometric.lean`
1424	
1425	The geometric generic-flatness theorem `AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness`
1426	(blueprint `thm:generic_flatness`) is stated and **proved** in the separate
1427	file `AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.GenericFlatnessGeometric`, as the geometric
1428	glue over the algebraic engine of this file: it consumes the qcqs
1429	section-localization engine of `AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.QuotScheme`
1430	(Stacks 01P0/01PC/01I8), which this file deliberately does not import so
1431	that the instance environment of the dévissage proofs above stays fixed. -/
1432	
1433	/-! ## §3–§6. Existence, universality, curve specialisation — moved
1434	
1435	The geometric sections (Nitsure §4 sub-lemmas `flatLocusStratification` and
1436	`flatLocusReduction`, the assembly `flatLocusAssembly`, the main theorem
1437	`flatteningStratification`, its universal property, and the curve
1438	specialisation `flatteningStratification.ofCurve`) live in
1439	`AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.GenericFlatnessGeometric`: the proof of Lemma 6
1440	(`flatLocusReduction`, run 0010 T12 r6) consumes `genericFlatness`, whose
1441	geometric glue requires the QuotScheme engine that this file deliberately
1442	does not import (see §2 above).  Blueprint `\lean{}` pointers are unchanged
1443	— they pin fully qualified declaration names, which are preserved. -/
1444	
1445	end AlgebraicGeometry

840	
841	/-! ## §5. Flatness over the base is affine-local (cover ⟹ all pairs)
842	
843	If a quasi-coherent module `G` on `Y ⟶ T` has flat sections on one family of
844	affine chart pairs `(Uc j ⊆ T, Wc j ⊆ q⁻¹Uc j)` whose fibre-side charts cover
845	`Y`, then *every* affine pair `(U, W)` has flat sections — Stacks 00HT-style
846	affine-locality, in the two-layer basic-open reduction style of
847	`flat_section_pair`: cover `W` by opens simultaneously basic in `W` and in a
848	chart, lying above base basic opens common to `U` and the chart's base
849	`Uc j`; per piece, restrict the chart flatness fibre-side (mixed-base
850	stability `Module.Flat.of_isLocalizedModule_algebra`), then exchange the base
851	ring twice (`Module.flat_iff_of_isLocalization`); glue with
852	`Module.flat_of_isLocalized_span`. -/
853	
854	section FlatAffineLocal
855	
856	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
857	-- Heartbeat headroom: per-piece instance provisioning under binders, as in
858	-- `flat_section_pair`.
859	/-- **Flatness over the base is affine-local.**  Chart flatness on an affine
860	cover implies flatness on every affine pair. -/
861	theorem flat_section_of_affine_cover {T Y : Scheme.{u}} (q : Y ⟶ T) (G : Y.Modules)
862	    [G.IsQuasicoherent] {ι : Type u}
863	    (Wc : ι → Y.Opens) (hWc : ∀ j, IsAffineOpen (Wc j))
864	    (Uc : ι → T.Opens) (hUc : ∀ j, IsAffineOpen (Uc j))
865	    (eWc : ∀ j, Wc j ≤ q ⁻¹ᵁ Uc j)
866	    (hcover : ∀ y : Y, ∃ j, y ∈ Wc j)
867	    (hflat : ∀ j,
868	      letI : Module Γ(T, Uc j) Γ(G, Wc j) :=
869	        Module.compHom _ (q.appLE (Uc j) (Wc j) (eWc j)).hom
870	      Module.Flat Γ(T, Uc j) Γ(G, Wc j))
871	    {U : T.Opens} (hU : IsAffineOpen U) {W : Y.Opens} (hW : IsAffineOpen W)
872	    (e : W ≤ q ⁻¹ᵁ U) :
873	    letI : Module Γ(T, U) Γ(G, W) := Module.compHom _ (q.appLE U W e).hom
874	    Module.Flat Γ(T, U) Γ(G, W) := by
875	  letI : Module Γ(T, U) Γ(G, W) := Module.compHom _ (q.appLE U W e).hom
876	  -- per-point choice of a simultaneous basic open
877	  have Hx : ∀ x : ↥W, ∃ (b : Γ(Y, W)) (j : ι) (a : Γ(T, U)) (a' : Γ(T, Uc j))
878	      (c : Γ(Y, Wc j)),
879	      Y.basicOpen b = Y.basicOpen c ∧ (x : Y) ∈ Y.basicOpen b ∧
880	      T.basicOpen a = T.basicOpen a' ∧
881	      Y.basicOpen b ≤ q ⁻¹ᵁ (T.basicOpen a) := by
882	    intro x
883	    obtain ⟨j, hxj⟩ := hcover x.1
884	    have hxU : q.base x.1 ∈ U := e x.2
885	    have hxUj : q.base x.1 ∈ Uc j := eWc j hxj
886	    obtain ⟨a, a', haa', hqa⟩ :=
887	      AlgebraicGeometry.exists_basicOpen_le_affine_inter hU (hUc j) (q.base x.1)
888	        ⟨hxU, hxUj⟩
889	    have hxT : x.1 ∈ (W ⊓ (q ⁻¹ᵁ T.basicOpen a ⊓ Wc j) : Y.Opens) :=
890	      ⟨x.2, ⟨hqa, hxj⟩⟩
891	    obtain ⟨b₁, hb₁le, hxb₁⟩ := hW.exists_basicOpen_le

236	/-- **The hard direction of Nitsure's local universal property** (the step
237	left implicit in [Nitsure §4]): if `M` is flat and has rank `e` at every
238	prime, then the relation matrix of *any* presentation of `M` by `e`
239	generators vanishes.
240	
241	The surjection `π : R^e ↠ M` between finite projectives of the same
242	everywhere-rank splits, the complement `K = ker π` is a finite projective
243	of everywhere-rank `0`, hence zero, so `ker π = range ψ = 0` and all
244	entries of `ψ` vanish. -/
245	theorem relMatrix_eq_zero_of_flat (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m)
246	    [Module.Flat R M]
247	    (h : ∀ p : PrimeSpectrum R, Module.rankAtStalk M p = e) :
248	    P.relMatrix = 0 := by
249	  haveI := P.finitePresentation
250	  haveI : Module.Projective R M := Module.Flat.projective_of_finitePresentation
251	  -- split the projection
252	  obtain ⟨s, hs⟩ := Module.projective_lifting_property P.proj LinearMap.id
253	    P.surjective_proj
254	  have hs' : ∀ x : M, P.proj (s x) = x := fun x ↦ congr($hs x)
255	  -- `R^e ≃ M × ker` via the splitting
256	  let E : (Fin e → R) ≃ₗ[R] M × LinearMap.ker P.proj :=
257	    { toFun := fun x ↦ (P.proj x, ⟨x - s (P.proj x), by simp [hs']⟩)
258	      map_add' := fun x y ↦ by
259	        ext <;> simp [add_sub_add_comm]
260	      map_smul' := fun r x ↦ by
261	        ext <;> simp [smul_sub]
262	      invFun := fun nk ↦ s nk.1 + nk.2.1
263	      left_inv := fun x ↦ by simp
264	      right_inv := fun nk ↦ by
265	        have hk : P.proj nk.2.1 = 0 := nk.2.2
266	        ext <;> simp [hs', hk] }
267	  -- the kernel is finite projective (a direct summand of `R^e`)
268	  haveI : Module.Projective R (LinearMap.ker P.proj) := by
269	    refine Module.Projective.of_split (LinearMap.ker P.proj).subtype
270	      ((LinearMap.snd R M (LinearMap.ker P.proj)) ∘ₗ E.toLinearMap) ?_
271	    refine LinearMap.ext fun ⟨x, hx⟩ ↦ Subtype.ext ?_
272	    have hx0 : P.proj x = 0 := hx
273	    simp [E, hx0]
274	  haveI : Module.Finite R (LinearMap.ker P.proj) := by
275	    have hsurj : Function.Surjective
276	        ((LinearMap.snd R M (LinearMap.ker P.proj)) ∘ₗ E.toLinearMap) := fun k ↦
277	      ⟨E.symm (0, k), by simp⟩
278	    exact Module.Finite.of_surjective _ hsurj
279	  -- rank bookkeeping: `e = e + rankAtStalk (ker) p` at every prime
280	  have hrank : ∀ p : PrimeSpectrum R,
281	      Module.rankAtStalk (R := R) (LinearMap.ker P.proj) p = 0 := by
282	    intro p
283	    haveI : Nontrivial R := nontrivial_of_primeSpectrum p
284	    have h1 : Module.rankAtStalk (R := R) (Fin e → R) p = e := by
285	      simp
286	    have h2 : Module.rankAtStalk (R := R) (Fin e → R) p
287	        = Module.rankAtStalk M p + Module.rankAtStalk (LinearMap.ker P.proj) p := by
288	      rw [congrFun (Module.rankAtStalk_eq_of_equiv E) p,
289	        congrFun (Module.rankAtStalk_prod (R := R) (M := M) (LinearMap.ker P.proj)) p,
290	        Pi.add_apply]
291	    have h3 := h p
292	    omega
293	  -- everywhere-rank-zero finite flat module is zero
294	  haveI : Subsingleton (LinearMap.ker P.proj) :=
295	    Module.rankAtStalk_eq_zero_iff_subsingleton.mp (funext hrank)
296	  -- hence the kernel is trivial and the relation matrix vanishes
297	  have hker : LinearMap.ker P.proj = ⊥ := by
298	    rw [Submodule.eq_bot_iff]
299	    intro x hx
300	    simpa using congrArg Subtype.val (Subsingleton.elim
301	      (⟨x, hx⟩ : LinearMap.ker P.proj) ⟨0, (LinearMap.ker P.proj).zero_mem⟩)
302	  have hrange : LinearMap.range P.relMatrix.mulVecLin = ⊥ := by
303	    rw [← P.ker_proj]
304	    exact hker
305	  rw [LinearMap.range_eq_bot] at hrange
306	  ext i j
307	  have := congr($hrange (Pi.single j 1) i)
308	  simpa [Matrix.mulVecLin_apply, Matrix.mulVec_single_one] using this
309	
310	end HardDirection
311	
312	section Independence
313	
314	/-- Auxiliary half of presentation-independence: the entry ideal of one
315	`e`-generator presentation is contained in that of another. -/
316	theorem entryIdeal_le_entryIdeal (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m)
317	    (Q : MatrixPresentation R M e m') :
318	    Q.entryIdeal ≤ P.entryIdeal := by
319	  -- base change both to `A := R ⧸ P.entryIdeal`
320	  set A := R ⧸ P.entryIdeal
321	  -- the base change of `P` has vanishing relation matrix
322	  have hP : (P.baseChange A).relMatrix = 0 := by
323	    rw [baseChange_relMatrix, relMatrix_map_eq_zero_iff]
324	    intro x hx
325	    rw [RingHom.mem_ker, Ideal.Quotient.algebraMap_eq, Ideal.Quotient.eq_zero_iff_mem]
326	    exact hx
327	  -- hence `A ⊗ M` is free of everywhere-rank `e`
328	  haveI : Module.Free A (A ⊗[R] M) := (P.baseChange A).free_of_relMatrix_eq_zero hP
329	  -- apply the hard direction to the base change of `Q`
330	  have hQ : (Q.baseChange A).relMatrix = 0 :=
331	    (Q.baseChange A).relMatrix_eq_zero_of_flat
332	      ((P.baseChange A).rankAtStalk_eq_of_relMatrix_eq_zero hP)
333	  rw [baseChange_relMatrix, relMatrix_map_eq_zero_iff] at hQ
334	  refine hQ.trans ?_
335	  intro x hx
336	  rwa [RingHom.mem_ker, Ideal.Quotient.algebraMap_eq,
337	    Ideal.Quotient.eq_zero_iff_mem] at hx
338	
339	/-- **Presentation-independence of the entry ideal** (the Fitting-ideal
340	miracle for `1 × 1` minors of an `e`-generator presentation): any two
341	presentations of `M` by `e` generators have the same entry ideal.
342	[Nitsure §4: implicit in "the resulting strata glue together by their
343	universal property".] -/
344	theorem entryIdeal_eq (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m)
345	    (Q : MatrixPresentation R M e m') :
346	    P.entryIdeal = Q.entryIdeal :=
347	  le_antisymm (entryIdeal_le_entryIdeal Q P) (entryIdeal_le_entryIdeal P Q)
348	
349	end Independence
350	
351	end MatrixPresentation
352	
353	end Module
354	
355	/-! ## The fiber rank and the point-set description of the strata -/
356	
357	namespace Ideal
358	
359	variable {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
360	
361	/-- The **fiber rank** of a module `M` at a prime `p`: the dimension of the
362	fiber `κ(p) ⊗[R] M` over the residue field `κ(p)`.  [Nitsure §4:
363	`e = dim_{κ(s)} F|_s`.] -/
364	noncomputable def fiberRank (p : Ideal R) [p.IsPrime]
365	    (M : Type v) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] : ℕ :=
366	  Module.finrank p.ResidueField (p.Fiber M)
367	
368	end Ideal
369	
370	namespace Module.MatrixPresentation
371	
372	variable {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
373	variable {e m : ℕ}
374	
375	/-- **Point-set description of the rank-`e` stratum** [Nitsure §4, part (i)
376	of the theorem, `n = 0` case]: a prime `p` contains the entry ideal of an
377	`e`-generator presentation iff the fiber of `M` at `p` has dimension
378	exactly `e`. -/
379	theorem entryIdeal_le_prime_iff (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m)
380	    (p : Ideal R) [p.IsPrime] :
381	    P.entryIdeal ≤ p ↔ p.fiberRank M = e := by
382	  have hker : RingHom.ker (algebraMap R p.ResidueField) = p :=
383	    Ideal.ker_algebraMap_residueField p
384	  constructor
385	  · -- entries in `p` ⟹ the fiber presentation has zero relation matrix
386	    intro hle
387	    have h0 : (P.baseChange p.ResidueField).relMatrix = 0 := by
388	      rw [baseChange_relMatrix, relMatrix_map_eq_zero_iff, hker]
389	      exact hle
390	    rw [Ideal.fiberRank, ← LinearEquiv.finrank_eq
391	      ((P.baseChange p.ResidueField).projEquiv h0)]
392	    simp
393	  · -- fiber dimension `e` ⟹ the fiber relation matrix vanishes (hard
394	    -- direction over the residue field), so the entries lie in `p`
395	    intro hrank
396	    have h0 : (P.baseChange p.ResidueField).relMatrix = 0 := by
397	      refine (P.baseChange p.ResidueField).relMatrix_eq_zero_of_flat ?_
398	      intro q
399	      rw [Module.rankAtStalk_eq_finrank_of_free]
400	      exact hrank
401	    rw [baseChange_relMatrix, relMatrix_map_eq_zero_iff, hker] at h0
402	    exact h0
403	
404	end Module.MatrixPresentation
405	
406	/-! ## Nakayama prolongation
407	
408	For `M` finitely presented over `R` and a prime `p`, there is `g ∉ p` such
409	that every localization of `M` away from `g` admits a matrix presentation
410	with exactly `fiberRank p M` generators.  This is the "prolongation of a
411	fiber basis" step of [Nitsure §4] (Nakayama applied twice: once to lift a
412	fiber basis to local generators, once to finitely present the kernel). -/
413	
414	namespace Module.FinitePresentation
415

4823	  -- (where `φ = g.appLE V U e`) and applying Step 2 (`pullback_tildeIso`,
4824	  -- Stacks 01HQ) gives `(Spec.map φ)^* tilde Γ(N, V) ≅
4825	  --   tilde (Γ(Y, U) ⊗_{Γ(X, V)} Γ(N, V))`.
4826	  -- Identifying the two compositions via the commutative square

4829	  -- section back to `U` itself. Evaluating tilde at `⊤` via

4832	  -- `Γ(Y, U) ⊗_{Γ(X, V)} Γ(N, V)`. The intertwining at `1 ⊗ x` (the
4833	  -- Beck-Chevalley check) follows from naturality of the adjunction
4834	  -- unit `pullback_app_isoTensor_unitAtV`.

4840	    pullback_of_openImmersion_iso_restrict

4854	  --                                                   [(pullbackComp).symm]

4856	  -- ≅ tilde (TensorProduct Γ(X,V) Γ(Y,U) Γ(N,V))     [step2 (Stacks 01HQ)]
4857	  let composedIso :=
4858	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp _hU.fromSpec g).app N ≪≫
4859	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr h_eq).app N ≪≫

data.content:
62	Also see `Scheme.IdealSheafData.mkOfMemSupportIff` for a constructor with the condition on the
63	support being (usually) easier to prove.
64	-/
65	structure IdealSheafData (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type u where
66	  /-- The component of an ideal sheaf at an affine open. -/
67	  ideal : ∀ U : X.affineOpens, Ideal Γ(X, U)
68	  /-- Also see `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IdealSheafData.map_ideal` -/
69	  map_ideal_basicOpen : ∀ (U : X.affineOpens) (f : Γ(X, U)),
70	    (ideal U).map (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE <| X.basicOpen_le f).op).hom =
71	      ideal (X.affineBasicOpen f)
72	  /-- The support of an ideal sheaf. Use `IdealSheafData.support` instead for most occasions. -/
73	  supportSet : Set X := ⋂ U, X.zeroLocus (U := U.1) (ideal U)
74	  supportSet_eq_iInter_zeroLocus : supportSet = ⋂ U, X.zeroLocus (U := U.1) (ideal U) := by rfl
75	
76	namespace IdealSheafData
77	
78	@[ext]
79	protected lemma ext {I J : X.IdealSheafData} (h : I.ideal = J.ideal) : I = J := by
80	  obtain ⟨i, _, s, hs⟩ := I
81	  obtain ⟨j, _, t, ht⟩ := J
82	  subst h
83	  congr
84	  rw [hs, ht]
85	
86	section Order
87	
88	instance : PartialOrder (IdealSheafData X) := PartialOrder.lift ideal fun _ _ ↦ IdealSheafData.ext
89	
90	lemma le_def {I J : IdealSheafData X} : I ≤ J ↔ ∀ U, I.ideal U ≤ J.ideal U := .rfl
91	
92	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
93	instance : CompleteSemilatticeSup (IdealSheafData X) where
94	  sSup s :=
95	  { ideal := sSup (ideal '' s),
96	    map_ideal_basicOpen := by
97	      have : sSup (ideal '' s) = ⨆ i : s, ideal i.1 := by
98	        conv_lhs => rw [← Subtype.range_val (s := s), ← Set.range_comp]
99	        rfl
100	      simp only [this, iSup_apply, Ideal.map_iSup, map_ideal_basicOpen, implies_true] }
101	  isLUB_sSup _ := .of_image (f := ideal) le_def (isLUB_sSup _)
102	
103	/-- The largest ideal sheaf contained in a family of ideals. -/
104	def ofIdeals (I : ∀ U : X.affineOpens, Ideal Γ(X, U)) : IdealSheafData X :=
105	  sSup { J : IdealSheafData X | J.ideal ≤ I }
106	
107	lemma ideal_ofIdeals_le (I : ∀ U : X.affineOpens, Ideal Γ(X, U)) :
108	    (ofIdeals I).ideal ≤ I :=
109	  sSup_le (Set.forall_mem_image.mpr fun _ ↦ id)
110	
111	/-- The Galois coinsertion between ideal sheaves and arbitrary families of ideals. -/
112	protected def gci : GaloisCoinsertion ideal (ofIdeals (X := X)) where
113	  choice I hI :=
114	  { ideal := I
115	    map_ideal_basicOpen U f :=
116	      (ideal_ofIdeals_le I).antisymm hI ▸ (ofIdeals I).map_ideal_basicOpen U f }
117	  gc _ _ := ⟨(le_sSup ·), (le_trans · (ideal_ofIdeals_le _))⟩
118	  u_l_le _ := sSup_le fun _ ↦ id
119	  choice_eq I hI := IdealSheafData.ext (hI.antisymm (ideal_ofIdeals_le I))
120	
121	lemma strictMono_ideal : StrictMono (ideal (X := X)) := IdealSheafData.gci.strictMono_l
122	lemma ideal_mono : Monotone (ideal (X := X)) := strictMono_ideal.monotone
123	lemma ofIdeals_mono : Monotone (ofIdeals (X := X)) := IdealSheafData.gci.gc.monotone_u
124	lemma ofIdeals_ideal (I : IdealSheafData X) : ofIdeals I.ideal = I := IdealSheafData.gci.u_l_eq _
125	lemma le_ofIdeals_iff {I : IdealSheafData X} {J} : I ≤ ofIdeals J ↔ I.ideal ≤ J :=
126	  IdealSheafData.gci.gc.le_iff_le.symm
127	
128	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
129	instance : OrderTop (IdealSheafData X) where
130	  top.ideal := ⊤
131	  top.map_ideal_basicOpen := by simp [Ideal.map_top]

685	open IdealSheafData
686	
687	variable {Y Z : Scheme.{u}}
688	
689	/-- The kernel of a morphism,
690	defined as the largest (quasi-coherent) ideal sheaf contained in the component-wise kernel.
691	This is usually only well-behaved when `f` is quasi-compact. -/
692	def Hom.ker (f : X.Hom Y) : IdealSheafData Y :=
693	  ofIdeals fun U ↦ RingHom.ker (f.app U).hom
694	
695	lemma Hom.ideal_ker_le (f : X.Hom Y) (U : Y.affineOpens) :
696	    f.ker.ideal U ≤ RingHom.ker (f.app U).hom :=
697	  ideal_ofIdeals_le _ _
698	
699	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
700	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
701	@[simp]
702	lemma Hom.ker_apply (f : X.Hom Y) [QuasiCompact f] (U : Y.affineOpens) :
703	    f.ker.ideal U = RingHom.ker (f.app U).hom := by
704	  let I : IdealSheafData Y := ⟨fun U ↦ RingHom.ker (f.app U).hom, ?_, _, rfl⟩
705	  · exact congr($(ofIdeals_ideal I).ideal U)
706	  intro U s
707	  apply le_antisymm
708	  · refine Ideal.map_le_iff_le_comap.mpr fun x hx ↦ ?_
709	    simp_rw [RingHom.comap_ker, ← CommRingCat.hom_comp, Scheme.affineBasicOpen_coe, f.naturality,
710	      CommRingCat.hom_comp, ← RingHom.comap_ker]
711	    exact Ideal.ker_le_comap _ hx
712	  · intro x hx
713	    have := U.2.isLocalization_basicOpen s
714	    obtain ⟨x, ⟨_, n, rfl⟩, rfl⟩ := IsLocalization.exists_mk'_eq (.powers s) x
715	    refine (IsLocalization.mk'_mem_map_algebraMap_iff _ _ _ _ _).mpr ?_
716	    suffices ∃ (V : X.Opens) (hV : V = X.basicOpen ((f.app U).hom s)),
717	        letI := hV.trans_le (X.basicOpen_le _); ((f.app U).hom x |_ V) = 0 by
718	      obtain ⟨_, rfl, H⟩ := this
719	      obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := exists_pow_mul_eq_zero_of_res_basicOpen_eq_zero_of_isCompact
720	        X (U := f ⁻¹ᵁ U) (QuasiCompact.isCompact_preimage (f := f) _ U.1.2 U.2.isCompact)
721	        (f.app U x) (f.app U s) H
722	      exact ⟨_, ⟨n, rfl⟩, by simpa using hn⟩
723	    refine ⟨f ⁻¹ᵁ Y.basicOpen s, by simp, ?_⟩
724	    replace hx : (Y.presheaf.map (homOfLE (Y.basicOpen_le s)).op ≫ f.app _).hom x = 0 := by
725	      trans (f.app (Y.basicOpen s)).hom (algebraMap Γ(Y, U) _ x)
726	      · simp [-NatTrans.naturality, RingHom.algebraMap_toAlgebra]
727	      · simp only [Scheme.affineBasicOpen_coe, RingHom.mem_ker] at hx
728	        rw [← IsLocalization.mk'_spec' (M := .powers s), map_mul, hx, mul_zero]
729	    rwa [f.naturality] at hx

732	lemma Hom.le_ker_comp (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y.Hom Z) : g.ker ≤ (f ≫ g).ker := by
733	  refine ofIdeals_mono fun U ↦ ?_
734	  rw [Scheme.Hom.comp_app f g U, CommRingCat.hom_comp, ← RingHom.comap_ker]
735	  exact Ideal.ker_le_comap _

746	  (f.le_ker_comp g).antisymm' (((inv f).le_ker_comp _).trans (by simp))

750	    f.ker = ofIdealTop (RingHom.ker f.appTop.hom) := by

759	    Set.range f ⊆ f.ker.support := by

190	lemma isLocallyConstant_rankAtStalk_freeLocus [Module.FinitePresentation R M] :

193	  have : Module.Free _ _ := hx

195	    x.asIdeal.primeCompl (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap x.asIdeal.primeCompl M)
196	    (Localization.AtPrime x.asIdeal)
197	  refine ⟨Subtype.val ⁻¹' basicOpen f, (basicOpen f).2.preimage continuous_subtype_val, hf, ?_⟩
198	  rintro ⟨p, hp''⟩ hp
199	  let p' := Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid (Localization (.powers f)) p.asIdeal.primeCompl
200	  have hp' : Submonoid.powers f ≤ p.asIdeal.primeCompl := by
201	    simpa [Submonoid.powers_le, Ideal.primeCompl]

206	  have : IsScalarTower R (Localization.Away f) Rₚ :=
207	    IsLocalization.localization_isScalarTower_of_submonoid_le ..
208	  letI : Module (Localization.Away f) Mₚ := Module.compHom Mₚ (algebraMap _ Rₚ)

213	    ⟨fun r r' m ↦ show _ = algebraMap _ Rₚ r • r' • m by rw [← mul_smul, ← Algebra.smul_def]⟩

250	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap p.asIdeal.primeCompl N) _ e |>.finrank_eq

252	/-- If `M` is `R`-free, its rank at stalks is constant and agrees with the `R`-rank of `M`. -/
253	@[simp]
254	lemma rankAtStalk_eq_finrank_of_free [Module.Free R M] :
255	    rankAtStalk (R := R) M = Module.finrank R M := by
256	  ext p
257	  simp [rankAtStalk, finrank_of_isLocalizedModule_of_free _ p.asIdeal.primeCompl
258	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap p.asIdeal.primeCompl M)]
259	
260	lemma rankAtStalk_self [Nontrivial R] : rankAtStalk (R := R) R = 1 := by
261	  simp
262	
263	open LocalizedModule Localization
264	
265	/-- The rank of `Π i, M i` at a prime `p` is the sum of the ranks of `M i` at `p`. -/
266	lemma rankAtStalk_pi {ι : Type*} [Finite ι] (M : ι → Type*)
267	    [∀ i, AddCommGroup (M i)] [∀ i, Module R (M i)] [∀ i, Module.Flat R (M i)]
268	    [∀ i, Module.Finite R (M i)] (p : PrimeSpectrum R) :
269	    rankAtStalk (Π i, M i) p = ∑ᶠ i, rankAtStalk (M i) p := by
270	  cases nonempty_fintype ι
271	  let f : (Π i, M i) →ₗ[R] Π i, LocalizedModule p.asIdeal.primeCompl (M i) :=
272	    .pi (fun i ↦ mkLinearMap p.asIdeal.primeCompl (M i) ∘ₗ LinearMap.proj i)
273	  let e : LocalizedModule p.asIdeal.primeCompl (Π i, M i) ≃ₗ[Localization.AtPrime p.asIdeal]
274	      Π i, LocalizedModule p.asIdeal.primeCompl (M i) :=
275	    IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv p.asIdeal.primeCompl
276	      (mkLinearMap _ _) f |>.extendScalarsOfIsLocalization p.asIdeal.primeCompl _
277	  have (i : ι) : Free (Localization.AtPrime p.asIdeal)
278	      (LocalizedModule p.asIdeal.primeCompl (M i)) :=
279	    free_of_flat_of_isLocalRing
280	  simp_rw [rankAtStalk, e.finrank_eq, Module.finrank_pi_fintype, finsum_eq_sum_of_fintype]
281	
282	lemma rankAtStalk_eq_finrank_tensorProduct (p : PrimeSpectrum R) :
283	    rankAtStalk M p =
284	      finrank (Localization.AtPrime p.asIdeal) (Localization.AtPrime p.asIdeal ⊗[R] M) := by
285	  let e : LocalizedModule p.asIdeal.primeCompl M ≃ₗ[Localization.AtPrime p.asIdeal]
286	      Localization.AtPrime p.asIdeal ⊗[R] M :=
287	    LocalizedModule.equivTensorProduct p.asIdeal.primeCompl M
288	  rw [rankAtStalk, e.finrank_eq]

290	variable [Flat R M] [Module.Finite R M]

292	attribute [local instance] free_of_flat_of_isLocalRing

295	    rankAtStalk M p = 0 ↔ p ∉ support R M := by
296	  rw [notMem_support_iff]
297	  refine ⟨fun h ↦ ?_, fun h ↦ Module.finrank_zero_of_subsingleton⟩
298	  apply subsingleton_of_rank_zero (R := Localization.AtPrime p.asIdeal)
299	  dsimp [rankAtStalk] at h
300	  simp [← finrank_eq_rank, h]

303	    0 < rankAtStalk M p ↔ p ∈ support R M :=

305

307	    rankAtStalk (R := R) M = 0 ↔ Subsingleton M := by
308	  refine ⟨fun h ↦ ?_, fun _ ↦ rankAtStalk_eq_zero_of_subsingleton⟩
309	  simp_rw [← support_eq_empty_iff (R := R), Set.eq_empty_iff_forall_notMem]

314	/-- The rank of `M × N` at `p` is equal to the sum of the ranks. -/
315	lemma rankAtStalk_prod (N : Type*) [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N]
316	    [Module.Flat R N] [Module.Finite R N] :
317	    rankAtStalk (R := R) (M × N) = rankAtStalk M + rankAtStalk N := by
318	  ext p
319	  let e : LocalizedModule p.asIdeal.primeCompl (M × N) ≃ₗ[Localization.AtPrime p.asIdeal]
320	      LocalizedModule p.asIdeal.primeCompl M × LocalizedModule p.asIdeal.primeCompl N :=

322	      (.prodMap (mkLinearMap _ M) (mkLinearMap _ N)) |>.extendScalarsOfIsLocalization
323	      p.asIdeal.primeCompl _

326	lemma rankAtStalk_baseChange {S : Type*} [CommRing S] [Algebra R S] (p : PrimeSpectrum S) :
327	    rankAtStalk (S ⊗[R] M) p = rankAtStalk M (p.comap (algebraMap R S)) := by
328	  let q : PrimeSpectrum R := p.comap (algebraMap R S)
329	  let := Localization.AtPrime.algebraOfLiesOver q.asIdeal p.asIdeal
330	  let e : LocalizedModule p.asIdeal.primeCompl (S ⊗[R] M) ≃ₗ[Localization.AtPrime p.asIdeal]
331	      Localization.AtPrime p.asIdeal ⊗[Localization.AtPrime q.asIdeal]
332	        LocalizedModule q.asIdeal.primeCompl M :=
333	    LocalizedModule.equivTensorProduct _ _ ≪≫ₗ
334	      (AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange R S _ _ M) ≪≫ₗ
335	      (AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange R _ _ _ M).symm ≪≫ₗ
336	      (AlgebraTensorModule.congr (LinearEquiv.refl _ _)
337	        (LocalizedModule.equivTensorProduct _ M).symm)
338	  rw [rankAtStalk, e.finrank_eq]
339	  apply Module.finrank_baseChange

342	lemma rankAtStalk_tensorProduct (N : Type*) [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N] [Module.Finite R N]
343	    [Module.Flat R N] : rankAtStalk (M ⊗[R] N) = rankAtStalk M * rankAtStalk (R := R) N := by
344	  ext p
345	  let e : Localization.AtPrime p.asIdeal ⊗[R] (M ⊗[R] N) ≃ₗ[Localization.AtPrime p.asIdeal]
346	      (Localization.AtPrime p.asIdeal ⊗[R] M) ⊗[Localization.AtPrime p.asIdeal]
347	        (Localization.AtPrime p.asIdeal ⊗[R] N) :=
348	    (AlgebraTensorModule.assoc _ _ _ _ _ _).symm ≪≫ₗ
349	      (AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange _ _ _ _ _).symm
350	  rw [rankAtStalk_eq_finrank_tensorProduct, e.finrank_eq, finrank_tensorProduct,
351	    ← rankAtStalk_eq_finrank_tensorProduct, ← rankAtStalk_eq_finrank_tensorProduct, Pi.mul_apply]
352	
353	lemma rankAtStalk_tensorProduct_of_isScalarTower {S : Type*} [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
354	    (N : Type*) [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N] [Module S N] [IsScalarTower R S N]
355	    [Module.Finite S N] [Module.Flat S N] (p : PrimeSpectrum S) :
356	    rankAtStalk (N ⊗[R] M) p = rankAtStalk N p * rankAtStalk M (p.comap (algebraMap R S)) := by
357	  simp [rankAtStalk_eq_of_equiv (AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange R S S N M).symm,
358	    rankAtStalk_tensorProduct, rankAtStalk_baseChange]

360	/-- The rank of a module `M` at a prime `p` is equal to the dimension
361	of `κ(p) ⊗[R] M` as a `κ(p)`-module. -/
362	lemma rankAtStalk_eq (p : PrimeSpectrum R) :
363	    rankAtStalk M p = finrank p.asIdeal.ResidueField (p.asIdeal.Fiber M) := by
364	  let k := p.asIdeal.ResidueField
365	  let e : k ⊗[Localization.AtPrime p.asIdeal] (Localization.AtPrime p.asIdeal ⊗[R] M) ≃ₗ[k]
366	      k ⊗[R] M :=
367	    AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange _ _ _ _ _
368	  rw [← e.finrank_eq, finrank_baseChange, rankAtStalk_eq_finrank_tensorProduct]

371	lemma _root_.Ideal.finrank_fiber_eq_rankAtStalk (p : Ideal R) [hp : p.IsPrime] :
372	    finrank p.ResidueField (p.Fiber M) = rankAtStalk M ⟨p, hp⟩ :=
373	  (rankAtStalk_eq ⟨p, hp⟩).symm

375	lemma _root_.Ideal.finrank_fiber_eq_finrank [IsDomain R] (p : Ideal R) [p.IsPrime] :
376	    finrank p.ResidueField (p.Fiber M) = finrank R M := by
377	  let K := FractionRing R
378	  let Rp := Localization.AtPrime p
379	  let Mp := LocalizedModule.AtPrime p M
380	  rw [p.finrank_fiber_eq_rankAtStalk, rankAtStalk, ← (isBaseChange Rp Mp K).finrank_eq,
381	    (((LocalizedModule.equivTensorProduct p.primeCompl M).baseChange Rp K Mp _)).finrank_eq,
382	    (AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange R Rp K K M).finrank_eq, (isBaseChange R M K).finrank_eq]

92	    { __ := Ideal.quotientEquiv _ _ (Localization.tensorLeftAlgEquiv p.primeCompl S) (by
93	        rw [← Ideal.map_coe includeRight, Ideal.map_map]
94	        congr

25	variable {R S A B : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [CommRing A] [CommRing B]
26	variable [Algebra R A] [Algebra R B] (I : Ideal R) [I.IsPrime]
27	
28	/--
29	The residue field at a prime ideal, defined to be the residue field of the local ring
30	`Localization.Prime I`.
31	We also provide an `IsFractionRing (R ⧸ I) I.ResidueField` instance.
32	-/
33	abbrev Ideal.ResidueField : Type _ :=
34	  IsLocalRing.ResidueField (Localization.AtPrime I)
35	
36	/-- If `I = f⁻¹(J)`, then there is a canonical embedding `κ(I) ↪ κ(J)`. -/
37	noncomputable
38	abbrev Ideal.ResidueField.map (I : Ideal R) [I.IsPrime] (J : Ideal S) [J.IsPrime]
39	    (f : R →+* S) (hf : I = J.comap f) : I.ResidueField →+* J.ResidueField :=
40	  IsLocalRing.ResidueField.map (Localization.localRingHom I J f hf)
41	
42	@[simp]
43	lemma Ideal.ResidueField.map_algebraMap (I : Ideal R) [I.IsPrime] (J : Ideal S) [J.IsPrime]
44	    (f : R →+* S) (hf : I = J.comap f) (r : R) :

80	  `W` and in a chart and lie above base basic opens common to `U` and `U₀`
81	  (`exists_basicOpen_le_affine_inter`, twice), and
82	  `Module.flat_of_isLocalized_span` plus two `Module.flat_iff_of_isLocalization`
83	  exchanges assemble the pieces. -/
84	
85	section GenericFlatnessGeometricGlue
86	
87	/-- Elementwise fibre-side `appLE` coherence: restricting the image of `appLE`
88	equals `appLE` into the smaller open. -/
89	private lemma appLE_res_apply {S X : Scheme.{u}} (p : X ⟶ S) {U : S.Opens}
90	    {W W' : X.Opens} (e : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U) (h : W' ≤ W) (u : Γ(S, U)) :
91	    (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE h).op).hom ((p.appLE U W e).hom u) =
92	      (p.appLE U W' (h.trans e)).hom u := by
93	  have h1 := congrArg (fun (φ : Γ(S, U) ⟶ Γ(X, W')) => φ.hom u)
94	    (Scheme.Hom.appLE_map (f := p) e (homOfLE h).op)
95	  simpa only [CommRingCat.hom_comp, RingHom.comp_apply] using h1
96	
97	/-- Elementwise base-side `appLE` coherence: `appLE` of a restricted base section
98	equals `appLE` from the larger base open. -/
99	private lemma appLE_base_res_apply {S X : Scheme.{u}} (p : X ⟶ S) {U U' : S.Opens}
100	    {W : X.Opens} (h : U' ≤ U) (e' : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U') (e : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U) (u : Γ(S, U)) :
101	    (p.appLE U' W e').hom ((S.presheaf.map (homOfLE h).op).hom u) =
102	      (p.appLE U W e).hom u := by
103	  have h1 := congrArg (fun (φ : Γ(S, U) ⟶ Γ(X, W)) => φ.hom u)
104	    (Scheme.Hom.map_appLE (f := p) e' (homOfLE h).op)
105	  simpa only [CommRingCat.hom_comp, RingHom.comp_apply] using h1
106	
107	/-- Transport of section flatness along an equality of opens: the `Module.compHom`
108	structures via `appLE` agree definitionally because the `≤`-witness of `appLE` is
109	proof-irrelevant. -/
110	private theorem flat_sections_congr {S X : Scheme.{u}} (p : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules)
111	    {U₀ : S.Opens} {O₁ O₂ : X.Opens} (hEq : O₁ = O₂)
112	    (e₁ : O₁ ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀) (e₂ : O₂ ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀)
113	    (h : letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, O₁) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ O₁ e₁).hom
114	         Module.Flat Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, O₁)) :
115	    letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, O₂) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ O₂ e₂).hom
116	    Module.Flat Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, O₂) := by
117	  subst hEq
118	  exact h
119	
120	open TensorProduct in
121	/-- **Mixed-base stability of flatness under module localization.**  Let `B` be an
122	`R`-algebra, `N` a `B`-module that is flat over `R`, and `g : N →ₗ[B] N'` a
123	localization of `N` at a submonoid `p ⊆ B`.  Then `N'` is still flat over `R`.
124	
125	Mathlib's `Module.Flat.of_isLocalizedModule` treats only submonoids of `R`
126	itself; here the localization happens on the fibre side.  The proof rewrites
127	`𝟙 N' ⊗ f` as the `IsLocalizedModule.map` of `𝟙 N ⊗ f` (Mathlib's
128	`IsLocalizedModule.map_lTensor`, with the `IsLocalizedModule.rTensor` instances)
129	and uses that localization preserves injectivity
130	(`IsLocalizedModule.map_injective`). -/
131	theorem _root_.Module.Flat.of_isLocalizedModule_algebra {R B : Type v} [CommRing R]
132	    [CommRing B] [Algebra R B] (p : Submonoid B) {N N' : Type v} [AddCommGroup N]
133	    [Module R N] [Module B N] [IsScalarTower R B N] [AddCommGroup N'] [Module R N']
134	    [Module B N'] [IsScalarTower R B N'] (g : N →ₗ[B] N') [IsLocalizedModule p g]
135	    [Module.Flat R N] : Module.Flat R N' := by
136	  rw [Module.Flat.iff_lTensor_injectiveₛ]
137	  intro P _ _ I
138	  rw [← TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.coe_lTensor (A := B)]
139	  rw [← IsLocalizedModule.map_lTensor (S := p) (g := g) (f := I.subtype)]
140	  exact IsLocalizedModule.map_injective p
141	    (TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.rTensor R I g)
142	    (TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.rTensor R P g)
143	    (TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.lTensor B N I.subtype)
144	    (by
145	      rw [TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.coe_lTensor (A := B)]
146	      exact Module.Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap I.subtype
147	        Subtype.val_injective)
148	
149	set_option maxHeartbeats 1200000 in
150	-- Heartbeat headroom: several `IsLocalizedModule`/scalar-tower instance chains
151	-- at localized section modules (qcqs section-localization engine).
152	/-- **Chart-basic core of generic flatness.**  Let `Wj ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀` be an affine
153	chart whose section module localized at `g ∈ Γ(S, U₀)` is free over
154	`Γ(S, U₀)_g` (the output of algebraic generic freeness), and let `c ∈ Γ(X, Wj)`
155	cut a basic open lying above the base basic open `D(g)`.  Then the sections of
156	`F` over `D(c)` (presented as any open `O = D(c)`) are flat over `Γ(S, U₀)`.
157	
158	Route: `D(β) = Wj ⊓ p ⁻¹ᵁ D(g)` for `β := appLE g`; the section restriction
159	`Γ(F, Wj) → Γ(F, D(β))` is a localization at `powers β` over `Γ(X, Wj)`
160	(Stacks 01P0) hence at `powers g` over `Γ(S, U₀)` (base-ring descent), so
161	`Γ(F, D(β))` inherits freeness-hence-flatness over `Γ(S, U₀)` from the
162	hypothesis; finally `Γ(F, D(β)) → Γ(F, D(c))` is a fibre-side localization,
163	absorbed by mixed-base stability. -/
164	private theorem flat_section_chartBasic {S X : Scheme.{u}} (p : X ⟶ S)
165	    (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent] {U₀ : S.Opens}
166	    {Wj : X.Opens} (hWj : IsAffineOpen Wj) (eWj : Wj ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀) (g : Γ(S, U₀))
167	    (hfree :
168	      letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, Wj) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ Wj eWj).hom
169	      Module.Free (Localization.Away g)
170	        (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) Γ(F, Wj)))
171	    (c : Γ(X, Wj)) (hc : X.basicOpen c ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ (S.basicOpen g))
172	    {O : X.Opens} (hO : O = X.basicOpen c) (eO : O ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀) :
173	    letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, O) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ O eO).hom
174	    Module.Flat Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, O) := by
175	  subst hO
176	  letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, Wj) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ Wj eWj).hom
177	  -- the base element pushed into the chart ring, and its basic open
178	  set β : Γ(X, Wj) := (p.appLE U₀ Wj eWj).hom g with hβ
179	  have hDβ : X.basicOpen β = Wj ⊓ p ⁻¹ᵁ (S.basicOpen g) := by
180	    rw [hβ, show (p.appLE U₀ Wj eWj).hom g
181	        = (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE eWj).op).hom ((p.app U₀).hom g) from rfl]
182	    rw [Scheme.basicOpen_res, Scheme.preimage_basicOpen]
183	  have hDcβ : X.basicOpen c ≤ X.basicOpen β := by
184	    rw [hDβ]
185	    exact le_inf (X.basicOpen_le c) hc
186	  have eβ : X.basicOpen β ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀ := (X.basicOpen_le β).trans eWj
187	  -- (1) chart-side localization of sections at β (Stacks 01P0)
188	  letI : Module Γ(X, Wj) Γ(F, X.basicOpen β) :=
189	    Module.compHom _ (algebraMap Γ(X, Wj) Γ(X, X.basicOpen β))
190	  haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(X, Wj) Γ(X, X.basicOpen β) Γ(F, X.basicOpen β) :=
191	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul (fun _ _ => rfl)
192	  haveI hlocB : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers β)
193	      (Scheme.Modules.restrictBasicOpenₗ F β) :=
194	    Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isCompact F hWj.isCompact
195	      hWj.isQuasiSeparated β
196	  -- (2) `Γ(S, U₀)`-module structures and scalar towers
197	  letI : Algebra Γ(S, U₀) Γ(X, Wj) := (p.appLE U₀ Wj eWj).hom.toAlgebra
198	  letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, X.basicOpen β) :=
199	    Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ (X.basicOpen β) eβ).hom
200	  haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(S, U₀) Γ(X, Wj) Γ(F, Wj) :=
201	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul (fun _ _ => rfl)
202	  haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(S, U₀) Γ(X, Wj) Γ(F, X.basicOpen β) :=
203	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul (fun a n => by
204	      change (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le β)).op).hom
205	          ((p.appLE U₀ Wj eWj).hom a) • n
206	        = (p.appLE U₀ (X.basicOpen β) eβ).hom a • n
207	      rw [appLE_res_apply p eWj (X.basicOpen_le β) a])
208	  -- (3) base-ring descent of the localization, and flatness of `Γ(F, D(β))`
209	  haveI hlocA : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g)
210	      ((Scheme.Modules.restrictBasicOpenₗ F β).restrictScalars Γ(S, U₀)) := by
211	    refine isLocalizedModule_powers_restrictScalars_of_algebraMap g _ ?_
212	    exact hlocB
213	  haveI hfreeg : Module.Free (Localization.Away g)
214	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) Γ(F, Wj)) := hfree
215	  haveI hflatLoc : Module.Flat Γ(S, U₀)
216	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) Γ(F, Wj)) := by
217	    haveI : Module.Flat (Localization.Away g)
218	        (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) Γ(F, Wj)) := inferInstance
219	    exact (Module.flat_iff_of_isLocalization (Localization.Away g)
220	      (Submonoid.powers g) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) Γ(F, Wj))).mp this
221	  haveI hflatNβ : Module.Flat Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, X.basicOpen β) :=
222	    Module.Flat.of_linearEquiv
223	      (IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers g)
224	        ((Scheme.Modules.restrictBasicOpenₗ F β).restrictScalars Γ(S, U₀))).symm
225	  -- (4) restrict from `D(β)` to `D(c)` and absorb by mixed-base stability
226	  set c' : Γ(X, X.basicOpen β) :=
227	    (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le β)).op).hom c with hc'
228	  have hDc' : X.basicOpen c' = X.basicOpen c := by
229	    rw [hc', Scheme.basicOpen_res]
230	    exact inf_eq_right.mpr hDcβ
231	  have ec' : X.basicOpen c' ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀ := (X.basicOpen_le c').trans eβ
232	  letI : Module Γ(X, X.basicOpen β) Γ(F, X.basicOpen c') :=
233	    Module.compHom _ (algebraMap Γ(X, X.basicOpen β) Γ(X, X.basicOpen c'))
234	  haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(X, X.basicOpen β) Γ(X, X.basicOpen c')
235	      Γ(F, X.basicOpen c') :=
236	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul (fun _ _ => rfl)
237	  haveI hlocC : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers c')
238	      (Scheme.Modules.restrictBasicOpenₗ F c') :=
239	    Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isCompact F
240	      (hWj.basicOpen β).isCompact (hWj.basicOpen β).isQuasiSeparated c'
241	  letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, X.basicOpen c') :=
242	    Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ (X.basicOpen c') ec').hom
243	  letI : Algebra Γ(S, U₀) Γ(X, X.basicOpen β) :=
244	    (p.appLE U₀ (X.basicOpen β) eβ).hom.toAlgebra
245	  haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(S, U₀) Γ(X, X.basicOpen β) Γ(F, X.basicOpen β) :=
246	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul (fun _ _ => rfl)
247	  haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(S, U₀) Γ(X, X.basicOpen β) Γ(F, X.basicOpen c') :=
248	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul (fun a n => by
249	      change (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le c')).op).hom
250	          ((p.appLE U₀ (X.basicOpen β) eβ).hom a) • n
251	        = (p.appLE U₀ (X.basicOpen c') ec').hom a • n
252	      rw [appLE_res_apply p eβ (X.basicOpen_le c') a])
253	  haveI hflatC' : Module.Flat Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, X.basicOpen c') :=
254	    Module.Flat.of_isLocalizedModule_algebra (Submonoid.powers c')
255	      (Scheme.Modules.restrictBasicOpenₗ F c')
256	  exact flat_sections_congr p F hDc' ec' eO hflatC'
257	
258	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
259	-- Heartbeat headroom: per-piece instance provisioning under binders for the
260	-- `flat_of_isLocalized_span` application.
261	/-- **Two-layer basic-open reduction for generic flatness.**  Given affine charts
262	`Wc j ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀` covering `p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀`, all of whose section modules localized
263	at `f ∈ Γ(S, U₀)` are free (the output of algebraic generic freeness at a
264	common denominator `f`), every affine pair `U ≤ D(f)`, `W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U` has flat
265	sections: cover `W` by opens simultaneously basic in `W` and in a chart, lying
266	above base basic opens common to `U` and `U₀`; conclude with
267	`Module.flat_of_isLocalized_span` over `Γ(X, W)` and the two
268	`Module.flat_iff_of_isLocalization` exchanges on the base. -/
269	private theorem flat_section_pair {S X : Scheme.{u}} (p : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules)
270	    [F.IsQuasicoherent] {U₀ : S.Opens} (hU₀ : IsAffineOpen U₀)
271	    {ι : Type u} (Wc : ι → X.Opens) (hWc : ∀ j, IsAffineOpen (Wc j))
272	    (eWc : ∀ j, Wc j ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀)
273	    (hcover : ∀ x ∈ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀, ∃ j, x ∈ Wc j) (f : Γ(S, U₀))
274	    (hfree : ∀ j,
275	      letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, Wc j) :=
276	        Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ (Wc j) (eWc j)).hom
277	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f)
278	        (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) Γ(F, Wc j)))
279	    {U : S.Opens} (hU : IsAffineOpen U) (hUf : U ≤ S.basicOpen f)
280	    {W : X.Opens} (hW : IsAffineOpen W) (e : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U) :
281	    letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U W e).hom
282	    Module.Flat Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) := by
283	  letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U W e).hom
284	  have hUU₀ : U ≤ U₀ := hUf.trans (S.basicOpen_le f)
285	  have hWU₀ : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀ := e.trans (fun x hx => hUU₀ hx)
286	  -- per-point choice of a simultaneous basic open
287	  have Hx : ∀ x : ↥W, ∃ (b : Γ(X, W)) (a : Γ(S, U)) (a' : Γ(S, U₀)) (j : ι)
288	      (c : Γ(X, Wc j)),
289	      X.basicOpen b = X.basicOpen c ∧ (x : X) ∈ X.basicOpen b ∧
290	      S.basicOpen a = S.basicOpen a' ∧
291	      X.basicOpen b ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ (S.basicOpen a) := by
292	    intro x
293	    have hxU : p.base x.1 ∈ U := e x.2
294	    have hxU₀ : p.base x.1 ∈ U₀ := hUU₀ hxU
295	    obtain ⟨a, a', haa', hpa⟩ :=
296	      AlgebraicGeometry.exists_basicOpen_le_affine_inter hU hU₀ (p.base x.1)
297	        ⟨hxU, hxU₀⟩
298	    obtain ⟨j, hxj⟩ := hcover x.1 (hWU₀ x.2)
299	    have hxT : x.1 ∈ (W ⊓ (p ⁻¹ᵁ S.basicOpen a ⊓ Wc j) : X.Opens) :=

You are scouting a Lean 4 workspace. Working directory: /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge (a Lean project; Mathlib source at .lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/).

Answer these questions with verbatim evidence (file paths, line numbers, code):

1. Which files in the whole project (grep AlgebraicJacobian/ recursively) mention `flatLocusStratification_universal` or import `AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.GenericFlatnessGeometric`? In the blueprint: grep blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex for `flatLocusStratification_universal` and `thm:flattening_stratification_universal` — copy the full blueprint node (theorem statement + proof environment) and any \lean{...} anchor lines. Also copy the section header/label of `sec:flatstrat_rank_stratum` and list the \lean anchors it contains (just the names).
3. Mathlib Opens-restriction API: in .lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/ (Restrict.lean, OpenImmersion.lean and neighbors), report exact signatures for: `Scheme.Opens.ι` (the open-subscheme inclusion morphism, type), `Scheme.restrictFunctor`, `IsAffineOpen.ι_image` or how `U.ι ''ᵁ V` works, the equivalence/map between `(↑U).Opens`/affineOpens and opens of X below U (e.g. `Scheme.Opens.ι_preimage`? `IsOpenImmersion.affineOpensEquiv`? `Scheme.Opens.functor`?), and any lemma `IsAffineOpen.image_of_isOpenImmersion` (affine opens map to affine opens along open immersions). 7. Mathlib: `Module.Flat` transport lemmas: `Module.Flat.of_linearEquiv` / `Module.Flat.equiv` / `LinearEquiv.flat` — exact name and signature in current Mathlib (v4.31-era). Also `Module.Free.of_equiv`, and restriction-of-scalars flatness `Module.Flat.trans`.

Return raw data organized by item number.

1108	
1109	For an irreducible closed subset `Zc ⊆ S` with reduced subscheme `T`, generic
1110	flatness on the base-changed family `X ×_S T ⟶ T` produces a non-empty open
1111	`Ω ⊆ T` such that for *every* open `St ≤ Ω` the family restricted to the
1112	locally closed stratum `St ⟶ S` has flat pullback: chart flatness on the
1113	piece cover of `X ×_S St` comes from the generic-flatness output through the
1114	pasted pullback square (`IsPullback.of_bot`) and the per-piece transfer of
1115	§4, `flat_section_of_affine_cover` (§5) globalizes, and
1116	`coherentSheafFlat_of_iso` rewrites the pulled-back sheaf through
1117	`pullbackComp`. -/

1389	
1390	/-! ## §7b. Transport of coherent-sheaf flatness along base isomorphisms
1391	
1392	`CoherentSheafFlat` is insensitive to an isomorphism on the base leg: if a
1393	module sheaf is flat for `q ≫ w` with `w` an isomorphism of schemes, it is
1394	flat for `q`.  The section-level content is that flatness of a module
1395	transports along a factorization of the acting ring map through a bijective
1396	ring map (`Module.compHom` structures; the section module is unchanged). -/
1397	
1398	section IsoTransport
1399	
1400	/-- Flatness of a module transports along a factorization of the acting ring
1401	map through a bijective ring map: if `ψ = φ ∘ ρ` with `ρ : R →+* R'`
1402	bijective and `M` flat for the `R'`-action through `φ`, then `M` is flat for
1403	the `R`-action through `ψ`.  (`R'` is a free `R`-module of rank one via `ρ`,
1404	so this is `Module.Flat.trans` along the scalar tower `R → R' → M`.) -/
1405	private theorem flat_of_ringHom_comp_bijective {R R' O M : Type v} [CommRing R]
1406	    [CommRing R'] [CommRing O] [AddCommGroup M] [Module O M] (ρ : R →+* R')
1407	    (hρ : Function.Bijective ρ) (φ : R' →+* O) (ψ : R →+* O)
1408	    (hψ : ψ = φ.comp ρ)
1409	    (h : letI : Module R' M := Module.compHom M φ; Module.Flat R' M) :
1410	    letI : Module R M := Module.compHom M ψ; Module.Flat R M := by
1411	  subst hψ
1412	  letI : Module R' M := Module.compHom M φ
1413	  letI : Module R M := Module.compHom M (φ.comp ρ)
1414	  letI : Algebra R R' := ρ.toAlgebra
1415	  haveI : Module.Flat R' M := h
1416	  haveI : Module.Flat R R' := by
1417	    have hcoe : ⇑(Algebra.linearMap R R') = ⇑ρ := by
1418	      funext x
1419	      simp [RingHom.algebraMap_toAlgebra]
1420	    exact Module.Flat.of_linearEquiv
1421	      ((LinearEquiv.ofBijective (Algebra.linearMap R R')
1422	        (by rw [hcoe]; exact hρ)).symm)
1423	  haveI : IsScalarTower R R' M :=
1424	    ⟨fun r r' m => by
1425	      change φ (ρ r * r') • m = φ (ρ r) • (φ r' • m)
1426	      rw [map_mul, mul_smul]⟩
1427	  exact Module.Flat.trans R R' M
1428	
1429	/-- **Coherent-sheaf flatness absorbs an isomorphism on the base**: if `𝓖` is
1430	flat for `q ≫ w` with `w` an isomorphism of schemes, then `𝓖` is flat for
1431	`q`.  Applied below with `q = 𝟙 P` to convert flatness over an isomorphic
1432	copy of `P` (e.g. `pullback.snd` of a pullback along an identity) into
1433	flatness over `P` itself. -/
1434	theorem coherentSheafFlat_of_comp_isIso {P W W' : Scheme.{u}} (q : P ⟶ W)
1435	    (w : W ⟶ W') [IsIso w] (G : P.Modules)
1436	    (h : Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (q ≫ w) G) : Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat q G := by
1437	  intro U hU V hV eV
1438	  have hrange : U ≤ (inv w).opensRange := by
1439	    intro x hx
1440	    refine ⟨w.base x, ?_⟩
1441	    rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, IsIso.hom_inv_id]
1442	    simp
1443	  have hU' : IsAffineOpen ((inv w) ⁻¹ᵁ U) :=
1444	    hU.preimage_of_isOpenImmersion (inv w) hrange
1445	  have heq : (q ≫ w) ⁻¹ᵁ ((inv w) ⁻¹ᵁ U) = q ⁻¹ᵁ U := by
1446	    rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_preimage, Category.assoc, IsIso.hom_inv_id,
1447	      Category.comp_id]
1448	  have eV' : V ≤ (q ≫ w) ⁻¹ᵁ ((inv w) ⁻¹ᵁ U) := eV.trans heq.ge
1449	  have hflat := h hU' hV eV'

2202	\begin{proof}
2203	  \leanok
2204	  Choose an \(e\)-presentation chart \(V \ni x\) with presentation \(P\).  The
2205	  image of the closed immersion is the support of the ideal sheaf, which meets
2206	  \(V\) in the zero locus of \(I_e(V)\); and \(I_e(V) = I(\psi_P)\) by
2207	  \cref{thm:strata_ideal_eq_entry_ideal}.  A point of an affine open lies in
2208	  the zero locus of an ideal of sections iff its prime contains that ideal, so
2209	  \(x\) lies in the stratum iff \(I(\psi_P) \subseteq \mathfrak p_x\).  By
2210	  \cref{thm:entry_ideal_prime_iff} this holds iff the fibre rank of
2211	  \(\Gamma(\mathcal G, V)\) at \(\mathfrak p_x\) --- the chart fibre rank at
2212	  \(x\), equal to the point rank by
2213	  \cref{lem:point_rank_eq_chart_fiber_rank} --- equals \(e\).
2214	\end{proof}
2215	
2216	\section{Universal property of the strata}
2217	\label{sec:flatstrat_universal}
2218	
2219	\begin{theorem}
2220	
2221	\leanok
2222	[Universal property of the flat-locus stratification (case \(n = 0\))]
2223	  \label{thm:flattening_stratification_universal}
2224	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusStratification_universal}
2225	  \uses{def:coherent_sheaf_flat}
2226	  \source{nitsure-hilbert-quot}
2227	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme and \(\F\) a coherent \(\OO_S\)-module.
2228	  Then there exist a finite index set \(I\) and locally-closed immersions
2229	  \(\iota_f : S_f \to S\) (\(f \in I\)) with pairwise disjoint images
2230	  set-theoretically covering \(|S|\), such that the pullback of \(\F\)
2231	  along \(\iota_f\) is flat over \(S_f\) for every \(f\) (in the sense of
2232	  \cref{def:coherent_sheaf_flat}), and such that, writing
2233	  \(S' := \coprod_{f \in I} S_f\) and \(i : S' \to S\) for the morphism
2234	  assembled from the \(\iota_f\), the pair \((S', i)\) has the universal
2235	  property: every morphism \(\varphi : T \to S\) for which
2236	  \(\varphi^* \F\) is flat over \(T\) factors \emph{uniquely} through
2237	  \(i\).
2238	
2239	  This is part (ii) of Nitsure's flattening-stratification theorem in the
2240	  special case \(n = 0\), stated with flatness in place of local freeness of
2241	  constant rank: for a finitely presented module the two notions agree
2242	  locally, the rank decomposing \(T\) into clopen pieces.  Nitsure states an
2243	  ``if and only if''; the converse direction --- any morphism factoring
2244	  through \(i\) has flat pullback --- is stability of flatness under base
2245	  change and is recorded separately once needed.  The universal property for
2246	  a relative family (Nitsure's part (ii) for \(\F\) on \(\PP^n_S\), hence
2247	  for projective \(\pi : X \to S\)) additionally requires the
2248	  cohomology-and-base-change layer of \cref{lem:noetherian_induction_strata}
2249	  and is not stated separately here; for a general \emph{proper} family the
2250	  universal property is not asserted by any source in the reference library,
2251	  and the reduced strata of \cref{lem:flat_locus_reduction_lean} do not
2252	  witness it (over \(S = \Spec k[\varepsilon]\) with \(\F = \OO_S\) flat,
2253	  the identity must factor through the stratification, forcing the canonical
2254	  non-reduced stratum structure).
2255	\end{theorem}
2256	
2257	\begin{proof}
2258	  \uses{lem:flat_locus_open, def:stratum, def:stratum_immersion,
2259	    thm:mem_range_stratum, thm:map_strata_ideal_basic_open}
2260	  By the case \(n = 0\) of the flattening stratification
2261	  (\cref{lem:flat_locus_open}), with its canonical scheme structure: locally
2262	  on \(S\), a basis of \(\F \otimes \kappa(s)\) prolongs by Nakayama to a
2263	  right-exact presentation
2264	  \(\OO_V^{\oplus m} \xrightarrow{\psi} \OO_V^{\oplus e} \to \F|_V \to 0\)
2265	  on a neighbourhood \(V\) of \(s\), and the closed subscheme
2266	  \(V_e \subseteq V\) cut out by the ideal of matrix entries of \(\psi\)
2267	  represents the locus where \(\F\) becomes locally free of rank \(e\):
2268	  for \(f : T \to V\) the pulled-back presentation stays right-exact, so
2269	  \(f^*\F\) is locally free of rank \(e\) if and only if \(f^*\psi = 0\),
2270	  if and only if \(f\) factors through \(V_e\).  The local strata glue by
2271	  this representing property into locally-closed subschemes
2272	  \(S_e \subseteq S\) with \(|S_e|\) the rank-\(e\) locus; the family is
2273	  finite because the fibre rank of a coherent module is bounded on a
2274	  noetherian scheme, and the images are disjoint and cover \(|S|\) since
2275	  every point has a well-defined fibre rank.
2276	
2277	  For the universal property, let \(\varphi : T \to S\) have
2278	  \(\varphi^*\F\) flat over \(T\).  A finitely presented flat module is
2279	  locally free, and its rank is locally constant, so \(T\) decomposes into
2280	  clopen pieces \(T_e\) on which \(\varphi^*\F\) is locally free of
2281	  constant rank \(e\); by the representing property each restriction
2282	  \(\varphi|_{T_e}\) factors uniquely through \(S_e\), and the
2283	  factorizations assemble to \(\varphi = i \circ \tilde\varphi\) with
2284	  \(\tilde\varphi : T \to \coprod_e S_e\).  Uniqueness: the strata have
2285	  disjoint images, so any factorization through \(i\) induces the same
2286	  decomposition of \(T\) into clopen preimages, and on each piece the
2287	  component through \(S_e\) is unique because \(\iota_e\) is an immersion,
2288	  hence a monomorphism.
2289	\end{proof}
2290	
2291	\section{Application: relative curves}
2292	\label{sec:flatstrat_curve_application}
2293	
2294	In the Route~A consumer setting, the input to the flattening-stratification
2295	machinery is the pullback \(C \times_k T \to T\) where \(C\) is a smooth proper
2296	curve over a field \(k\) and \(T\) is any \(k\)-scheme.  The hypotheses of
2297	Nitsure's \cref{thm:flattening_stratification_exists} demand
2298	\emph{projective} relative dimension, not merely \emph{proper}; we record that
2299	no extra work is required to instantiate the theorem in our setting.
2300	
2301	\begin{lemma}
2302	[Smooth proper curve over \(k\) is projective]
2303	  \label{lem:smooth_proper_curve_projective}
2304	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.TODO.smoothProperCurveProjective}
2305	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], II.6.7 / II.7.6 (curve \(\Rightarrow\) quasi-projective;
2306	  % proper \(\Rightarrow\) projective for curves), and standard consequences in
2307	  % chapter III. The precise statement ``every smooth proper curve over a field
2308	  % is projective'' is one of the classical equivalences for \(\dim 1\) and is
2309	  % textbook material; this block is project-bespoke (no verbatim source
2310	  % quote is reproduced here as the result is folklore-level and cited only
2311	  % structurally for the Route~A reduction).
2312	  \textit{Source: classical; cf.\ [Hartshorne], II.6.7 \& II.7.6 (curves).}
2313	  Let \(C\) be a smooth proper curve over a field \(k\).  Then \(C \to \Spec k\) is
2314	  projective.  Consequently, for any noetherian \(k\)-scheme \(T\), the relative
2315	  curve \(C \times_k T \to T\) is projective in the sense of Grothendieck (there
2316	  exists a relatively very ample line bundle on \(C \times_k T\) over \(T\)).
2317	\end{lemma}
2318	
2319	\begin{proof}
2320	  Projectivity of a smooth proper curve over a field is classical
2321	  ([Hartshorne], II.6.7 / II.7.6; alternatively: every divisor of large
2322	  positive degree on a smooth proper curve \(C\) is very ample, providing the
2323	  embedding \(C \hookrightarrow \PP^N_k\) for some \(N\)). Pulling the ample line
2324	  bundle back along \(C \times_k T \to C\) produces a relatively very ample line
2325	  bundle on \(C \times_k T\) over \(T\).
2326	\end{proof}
2327	
2328	\begin{corollary}
2329	
2330	\leanok
2331	[Flattening stratification for a coherent sheaf on a relative curve]
2332	  \label{cor:flattening_stratification_curve}
2333	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification.ofCurve}
2334	  \uses{lem:smooth_proper_curve_projective, thm:flattening_stratification_exists}
2335	  Let \(k\) be a field, \(C\) a smooth proper curve over \(k\), and \(T\) a noetherian
2336	  \(k\)-scheme.  Let \(\F\) be a coherent sheaf on \(C \times_k T\). Then the
2337	  flattening-stratification conclusion of
2338	  \cref{thm:flattening_stratification_exists} applies: there is a finite
2339	  set \(I\) of Hilbert polynomials and locally-closed subschemes
2340	  \(T_f \hookrightarrow T\) for \(f \in I\), set-theoretically covering \(T\)
2341	  disjointly, such that (a) \(\F|_{C \times_k T_f}\) is flat over \(T_f\) for
2342	  each \(f\), and (b) any morphism \(\varphi : T' \to T\) such that
2343	  \(\varphi^* \F\) on \(C \times_k T'\) is \(T'\)-flat factors uniquely through some
2344	  \(T_f\).
2345	\end{corollary}
2346	
2347	\begin{proof}
2348	  
2349	  By \cref{lem:smooth_proper_curve_projective}, \(C \times_k T \to T\) is
2350	  projective, hence factors through a closed immersion into \(\PP^n_T\) for some
2351	  \(n \ge 0\).  Push \(\F\) forward along the closed immersion (extending by zero
2352	  outside the image) to a coherent sheaf \(\tilde \F\) on \(\PP^n_T\); this
2353	  operation preserves \(T\)-flatness in both directions because the closed
2354	  immersion is a finite morphism.  Apply
2355	  \cref{thm:flattening_stratification_exists} to \(\tilde \F\) on
2356	  \(\PP^n_T\) to obtain \(\{T_f\}\) with the stated universal property.
2357	\end{proof}
2358	
2359	\section{Mathlib status}
2360	\label{sec:flatstrat_mathlib_status}
2361	
2362	\emph{Formalization status (run 0010, T12 r7).}  The Lean-typed existence
2363	statement (\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification}: finite
2364	disjoint locally-closed cover with flat pullbacks, without the
2365	Hilbert-polynomial indexing) is proved sorry-free and axiom-clean, through
2366	\cref{lem:flat_locus_reduction_lean} and the transfer lemmas of
2367	\cref{subsec:flat_transfer_strata}; the Lean sections \S3--\S6 of the
2368	statements live in \texttt{GenericFlatnessGeometric.lean} because the proof
2369	consumes generic flatness, whose glue needs the QuotScheme engine.  The
2370	\(n = 0\) stratification \texttt{flatLocusStratification}
2371	(\cref{lem:flat_locus_stratification_lean}) is likewise proved axiom-clean,
2372	derived from the existence theorem at \(\pi = \mathrm{id}_S\) through the
2373	iso-insensitivity of the flatness predicate
2374	(\texttt{coherentSheafFlat\_of\_comp\_isIso}) and empty-scheme padding of
2375	the index set.  The remaining Lean gaps in this chapter are the \(n = 0\)
2376	universal property (\texttt{flatLocusStratification\_universal},
2377	\cref{thm:flattening_stratification_universal}, the canonical entry-ideal
2378	strata) and the Hilbert-polynomial-indexed refinement of the main statement,
2379	which additionally awaits the coherent-\(\chi\) substrate.
2380	
2381	Mathlib (master \texttt{b80f227}) provides:
2382	\begin{itemize}
2383	  \item \texttt{Module.Flat} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Flat.Basic}) --- the
2384	    module-theoretic notion of flatness, applicable affine-locally to the
2385	    stalks \(\F_x\).
2386	  \item \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Flat} ---
2387	    \texttt{IsFlat} as a morphism property and basic stability lemmas; this is
2388	    flatness of the structure morphism, \emph{not} flatness of a quasi-coherent
2389	    module over the base.
2390	  \item \texttt{Module.FaithfullyFlat} and the descent infrastructure that
2391	    consumes it.
2392	\end{itemize}
2393	
2394	\noindent What Mathlib does \emph{not} yet have, and that this chapter will
2395	introduce in the new file
2396	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean}:
2397	\begin{itemize}
2398	  \item A predicate \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat}
2399	    capturing \(S\)-flatness of a coherent \(\OO_X\)-module on a finite-type
2400	    \(S\)-scheme (\cref{def:coherent_sheaf_flat}). This is the
2401	    sheaf-level lift of \texttt{Module.Flat} via the affine-local
2402	    characterization in the second paragraph of the definition.
2403	  \item The constructive existence proof
2404	    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification}
2405	    (\cref{thm:flattening_stratification_exists}) from Nitsure~\S4, proved
2406	    through \texttt{flatLocusReduction}
2407	    (\cref{lem:flat_locus_reduction_lean}, the Noetherian-induction
2408	    reduction via generic flatness) and packaged with injective labels by
2409	    \texttt{flatLocusAssembly} (\cref{lem:flat_locus_assembly_lean}); the
2410	    \(n = 0\) case \texttt{flatLocusStratification}
2411	    (\cref{lem:flat_locus_stratification_lean}) is derived back from the
2412	    existence theorem at \(\pi = \mathrm{id}_S\).
2413	  \item The universal-property packaging
2414	    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusStratification\_universal}
2415	    (\cref{thm:flattening_stratification_universal}), bundling the \(n = 0\)
2416	    stratification with the unique factorisation of morphisms with flat
2417	    pullback through the stratum coproduct \(\coprod_f S_f \to S\); the
2418	    projective-family upgrade needs \(\PP^n_S\) vocabulary and
2419	    cohomology-and-base-change, and an eventual
2420	    \texttt{CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy}-style refinement remains
2421	    future work.
2422	  \item The dévissage layer of the algebraic generic-freeness lemma
2423	    (\cref{sec:generic_freeness_devissage}) is formalized and proved
2424	    (splicing, torsion base case, transport, prime-filtration assembly
2425	    \texttt{GenericFreeness.genericFlatnessAlgebraic}); the sole remaining
2426	    algebraic gap is the normalisation core
2427	    (\cref{lem:generically_free_domain_core}).
2428	  \item The relative-curve specialisation
2429	    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification.ofCurve}
2430	    (\cref{cor:flattening_stratification_curve}) for the Route~A
2431	    consumer.
2432	\end{itemize}
2433	
2434	Inputs from Mathlib that the proof consumes:
2435	\begin{itemize}
2436	  \item \texttt{CategoryTheory.Limits.Cofan} / \texttt{Sigma} for the coproduct
2437	    \(\coprod_f S_f\).
2438	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.IsLocallyClosedImmersion} (and the locally-closed
2439	    subscheme API) for the strata \(S_f \hookrightarrow S\).

159	per-slice obligation is `presentationPullbackSliceOfOver`. -/

176	    (presentationPullbackSliceOfOver g F (q.X i) (q.presentation i)).isQuasicoherent
