406	/-! ## Nakayama prolongation
407	
408	For `M` finitely presented over `R` and a prime `p`, there is `g ∉ p` such
409	that every localization of `M` away from `g` admits a matrix presentation
410	with exactly `fiberRank p M` generators.  This is the "prolongation of a
411	fiber basis" step of [Nitsure §4] (Nakayama applied twice: once to lift a
412	fiber basis to local generators, once to finitely present the kernel). -/
413	
414	namespace Module.FinitePresentation
415	
416	open Module
417	
418	variable {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
419	
420	/-- Every element of the fiber `κ(p) ⊗[R] M` is, up to a unit (an `r ∉ p`),
421	a pure tensor `1 ⊗ s`.  (The module version of
422	`Ideal.Fiber.exists_smul_eq_one_tmul`, which Mathlib states only for
423	algebras.) -/
424	private lemma exists_smul_eq_one_tmul (p : Ideal R) [p.IsPrime]
425	    (x : p.ResidueField ⊗[R] M) :
426	    ∃ r ∉ p, ∃ s : M, r • x = (1 : p.ResidueField) ⊗ₜ[R] s := by
427	  induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
428	  | zero =>
429	    exact ⟨1, fun h ↦ ‹p.IsPrime›.ne_top ((Ideal.eq_top_iff_one _).mpr h), 0, by simp⟩
430	  | tmul c m =>
431	    obtain ⟨y, rfl⟩ := IsLocalRing.residue_surjective c
432	    obtain ⟨⟨a, t⟩, rfl⟩ := IsLocalization.mk'_surjective p.primeCompl y
433	    refine ⟨t, t.2, a • m, ?_⟩
434	    have ht : (t : R) • (IsLocalRing.residue (Localization.AtPrime p)
435	          (IsLocalization.mk' (Localization.AtPrime p) a t))
436	        = algebraMap R p.ResidueField a := by
437	      rw [Algebra.smul_def,
438	        IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply R (Localization.AtPrime p) p.ResidueField,
439	        IsLocalRing.ResidueField.algebraMap_eq, ← map_mul, IsLocalization.mk'_spec',
440	        ← IsLocalRing.ResidueField.algebraMap_eq, ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply]
441	    calc (t : R) • (IsLocalRing.residue (Localization.AtPrime p)
442	          (IsLocalization.mk' (Localization.AtPrime p) a t) ⊗ₜ[R] m)
443	        = ((t : R) • IsLocalRing.residue (Localization.AtPrime p)
444	            (IsLocalization.mk' (Localization.AtPrime p) a t)) ⊗ₜ[R] m := by
445	          rw [TensorProduct.smul_tmul']
446	      _ = (algebraMap R p.ResidueField a) ⊗ₜ[R] m := by rw [ht]
447	      _ = (a • (1 : p.ResidueField)) ⊗ₜ[R] m := by
448	          rw [Algebra.algebraMap_eq_smul_one]
449	      _ = (1 : p.ResidueField) ⊗ₜ[R] (a • m) := TensorProduct.smul_tmul a 1 m
450	  | add x y hx hy =>
451	    obtain ⟨rx, hrx, sx, hsx⟩ := hx
452	    obtain ⟨ry, hry, sy, hsy⟩ := hy
453	    refine ⟨rx * ry, fun hmem ↦ ?_, ry • sx + rx • sy, ?_⟩
454	    · rcases ‹p.IsPrime›.mem_or_mem hmem with h | h
455	      · exact hrx h
456	      · exact hry h
457	    · rw [TensorProduct.tmul_add, smul_add]
458	      congr 1
459	      · rw [mul_comm, mul_smul, hsx, TensorProduct.tmul_smul]
460	      · rw [mul_smul, hsy, TensorProduct.tmul_smul]
461	
462	/-- **Nakayama prolongation of a fiber basis** [Nitsure §4, `n = 0` case,
463	first step]: for `M` finitely presented over `R` and `p` a prime, there is
464	`g ∉ p` such that every localization `(R', N)` of `(R, M)` away from `g`
465	admits a matrix presentation with exactly `e = fiberRank p M` generators.
466	
467	The statement quantifies over abstract localizations (`IsLocalization.Away`
468	/ `IsLocalizedModule`) so that geometric section rings `Γ(S, D(g))` and
469	section modules `Γ(F, D(g))` can be consumed directly. -/
470	theorem exists_matrixPresentation_of_isLocalizedModule
471	    [Module.FinitePresentation R M] (p : Ideal R) [p.IsPrime] :
472	    ∃ g : R, g ∉ p ∧
473	      ∀ (R' : Type u') [CommRing R'] [Algebra R R'] [IsLocalization.Away g R']
474	        (N : Type v') [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N] [Module R' N]
475	        [IsScalarTower R R' N] (l : M →ₗ[R] N)
476	        [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) l],
477	      ∃ mm : ℕ, Nonempty (MatrixPresentation R' N (p.fiberRank M) mm) := by
478	  classical
479	  -- a basis of the fiber, rescaled to consist of pure tensors `1 ⊗ mᵢ`
480	  let b : Basis (Fin (p.fiberRank M)) p.ResidueField (p.Fiber M) :=
481	    Module.finBasis p.ResidueField (p.Fiber M)
482	  have hb : ∀ i, ∃ r ∉ p, ∃ s : M, r • b i = (1 : p.ResidueField) ⊗ₜ[R] s := fun i ↦
483	    exists_smul_eq_one_tmul p (b i)
484	  choose r hr mvec hm using hb
485	  -- the images `1 ⊗ mᵢ` span the fiber
486	  have hunit : ∀ i, IsUnit (algebraMap R p.ResidueField (r i)) := by
487	    intro i
488	    rw [isUnit_iff_ne_zero, Ne, Ideal.algebraMap_residueField_eq_zero]
489	    exact hr i
490	  have hspan : Submodule.span p.ResidueField
491	      (Set.range fun i ↦ (1 : p.ResidueField) ⊗ₜ[R] mvec i) = ⊤ := by
492	    have hsm : Set.range (fun i ↦ (1 : p.ResidueField) ⊗ₜ[R] mvec i) =
493	        Set.range ⇑(b.unitsSMul fun i ↦ (hunit i).unit) := by
494	      apply congrArg
495	      funext i
496	      rw [← hm i, ← algebraMap_smul p.ResidueField (r i) (b i),
497	        Basis.unitsSMul_apply, Units.smul_def, IsUnit.unit_spec]
498	    rw [hsm]
499	    exact (b.unitsSMul fun i ↦ (hunit i).unit).span_eq
500	  -- the `mᵢ` generate `M` modulo Nakayama: the quotient has vanishing fiber
501	  have hQfiber : Subsingleton (p.ResidueField ⊗[R]
502	      (M ⧸ LinearMap.range (Fintype.linearCombination R mvec))) := by
503	    have hsurj : Function.Surjective
504	        ((LinearMap.range (Fintype.linearCombination R mvec)).mkQ.baseChange
505	          p.ResidueField) := by
506	      rw [LinearMap.baseChange_eq_ltensor]
507	      exact LinearMap.lTensor_surjective _ (Submodule.mkQ_surjective _)
508	    have hcomp : ((LinearMap.range (Fintype.linearCombination R mvec)).mkQ.baseChange
509	        p.ResidueField) ∘ₗ
510	        ((Fintype.linearCombination R mvec).baseChange p.ResidueField) = 0 := by
511	      rw [← LinearMap.baseChange_comp]
512	      have h0 : (LinearMap.range (Fintype.linearCombination R mvec)).mkQ ∘ₗ
513	          Fintype.linearCombination R mvec = 0 := by
514	        ext x
515	        simp only [LinearMap.comp_apply, Submodule.mkQ_apply, LinearMap.zero_apply,
516	          Submodule.Quotient.mk_eq_zero]
517	        exact ⟨_, rfl⟩
518	      rw [h0, LinearMap.baseChange_zero]
519	    have hrange : LinearMap.range
520	        ((Fintype.linearCombination R mvec).baseChange p.ResidueField) = ⊤ := by
521	      rw [← top_le_iff, ← hspan, Submodule.span_le]
522	      rintro _ ⟨i, rfl⟩
523	      exact ⟨(1 : p.ResidueField) ⊗ₜ Pi.single i 1, by
524	        simp [LinearMap.baseChange_tmul, Fintype.linearCombination_apply_single]⟩
525	    apply subsingleton_of_forall_eq 0
526	    intro x
527	    obtain ⟨y, rfl⟩ := hsurj x
528	    have hy : y ∈ LinearMap.range
529	        ((Fintype.linearCombination R mvec).baseChange p.ResidueField) :=
530	      hrange ▸ Submodule.mem_top
531	    obtain ⟨a, rfl⟩ := hy
532	    exact congr($hcomp a)
533	  -- Nakayama at `p`: the finite quotient localizes to zero at `p`
534	  haveI : Module.Finite R (M ⧸ LinearMap.range (Fintype.linearCombination R mvec)) :=
535	    Module.Finite.of_surjective _ (Submodule.mkQ_surjective _)
536	  have e2 : Localization.AtPrime p ⊗[R]
537	      (M ⧸ LinearMap.range (Fintype.linearCombination R mvec))
538	      ≃ₗ[Localization.AtPrime p] LocalizedModule p.primeCompl
539	        (M ⧸ LinearMap.range (Fintype.linearCombination R mvec)) :=
540	    (IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange p.primeCompl (Localization.AtPrime p)
541	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap p.primeCompl _)).equiv
542	  haveI : Module.Finite (Localization.AtPrime p) (LocalizedModule p.primeCompl
543	      (M ⧸ LinearMap.range (Fintype.linearCombination R mvec))) :=
544	    Module.Finite.equiv e2
545	  have hQpsub : Subsingleton (LocalizedModule p.primeCompl
546	      (M ⧸ LinearMap.range (Fintype.linearCombination R mvec))) := by
547	    haveI := hQfiber
548	    haveI : Subsingleton (p.ResidueField ⊗[Localization.AtPrime p]
549	        (LocalizedModule p.primeCompl
550	          (M ⧸ LinearMap.range (Fintype.linearCombination R mvec)))) :=
551	      Equiv.subsingleton (((TensorProduct.congr
552	        (LinearEquiv.refl (Localization.AtPrime p) p.ResidueField) e2.symm).toEquiv).trans
553	        (AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange R (Localization.AtPrime p) p.ResidueField
554	          p.ResidueField _).toEquiv)
555	    exact (IsLocalRing.subsingleton_tensorProduct (R := Localization.AtPrime p)).mp ‹_›
556	  -- extract `g ∉ p` annihilating the quotient
557	  obtain ⟨g, hgann, hgp⟩ : ∃ g ∈ Module.annihilator R
558	      (M ⧸ LinearMap.range (Fintype.linearCombination R mvec)), g ∉ p := by
559	    have hnmem : (⟨p, ‹_›⟩ : PrimeSpectrum R) ∉ Module.support R
560	        (M ⧸ LinearMap.range (Fintype.linearCombination R mvec)) :=
561	      Module.notMem_support_iff.mpr hQpsub
562	    rw [Module.mem_support_iff_of_finite] at hnmem
563	    exact SetLike.not_le_iff_exists.mp hnmem
564	  have hg : ∀ x : M, g • x ∈ LinearMap.range (Fintype.linearCombination R mvec) := by
565	    intro x
566	    have hx := Module.mem_annihilator.mp hgann (Submodule.Quotient.mk
567	      (p := LinearMap.range (Fintype.linearCombination R mvec)) x)
568	    rwa [← Submodule.Quotient.mk_smul, Submodule.Quotient.mk_eq_zero] at hx
569	  refine ⟨g, hgp, ?_⟩
570	  intro R' _ _ _ N _ _ _ _ l _
571	  -- `N` is finitely presented over `R'`
572	  haveI : Module.FinitePresentation R' N :=
573	    _root_.FinitePresentation.of_isBaseChange l
574	      (IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange (Submonoid.powers g) R' l)
575	  -- the images `l mᵢ` generate `N` over `R'`
576	  have hgen : Function.Surjective (Fintype.linearCombination R' fun i ↦ l (mvec i)) := by
577	    rw [← LinearMap.range_eq_top, Fintype.range_linearCombination, ← top_le_iff]
578	    intro n _
579	    obtain ⟨⟨x, s⟩, hxs⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj (Submonoid.powers g) l n
580	    have hs : IsUnit (algebraMap R R' (s : R)) := IsLocalization.map_units R' s
581	    have hgu : IsUnit (algebraMap R R' g) :=
582	      IsLocalization.map_units R' ⟨g, Submonoid.mem_powers g⟩
583	    -- `l (g • x)` lies in the `R'`-span of the `l mᵢ`
584	    have hmem : l (g • x) ∈ Submodule.span R' (Set.range fun i ↦ l (mvec i)) := by
585	      obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := hg x
586	      have hsum : l (g • x) = ∑ i, c i • l (mvec i) := by
587	        rw [← hc]
588	        simp [Fintype.linearCombination_apply, map_sum]
589	      rw [hsum]
590	      exact Submodule.sum_mem _ fun i _ ↦ Submodule.smul_of_tower_mem _ (c i)
591	        (Submodule.subset_span ⟨i, rfl⟩)
592	    -- clear the units `s` and `g`
593	    have hxs' : (s : R) • n = l x := hxs
594	    have hn : algebraMap R R' ((s : R) * g) • n = l (g • x) := by
595	      rw [map_mul, mul_smul, algebraMap_smul, algebraMap_smul, smul_comm, hxs',
596	        ← map_smul]
597	    have hu : IsUnit (algebraMap R R' ((s : R) * g)) := by
598	      rw [map_mul]
599	      exact hs.mul hgu
600	    have hnn : n = hu.unit⁻¹ • l (g • x) := by
601	      rw [eq_inv_smul_iff, Units.smul_def, IsUnit.unit_spec]
602	      exact hn
603	    rw [hnn]
604	    exact Submodule.smul_mem _ _ hmem
605	  -- the kernel is finitely generated; its generators are the relation matrix
606	  obtain ⟨T, hT⟩ := Module.FinitePresentation.fg_ker
607	    (Fintype.linearCombination R' fun i ↦ l (mvec i)) hgen
608	  refine ⟨T.card, ⟨{
609	    relMatrix := Matrix.of fun i j ↦ (T.equivFin.symm j : Fin (p.fiberRank M) → R') i
610	    proj := Fintype.linearCombination R' fun i ↦ l (mvec i)
611	    surjective_proj := hgen
612	    exact_mulVecLin_proj := ?_ }⟩⟩
613	  rw [LinearMap.exact_iff, ← hT]
614	  apply le_antisymm
615	  · -- each generator is a column of the matrix
616	    rw [Submodule.span_le]
617	    intro t ht
618	    obtain ⟨j, hj⟩ := T.equivFin.symm.surjective ⟨t, ht⟩
619	    refine ⟨Pi.single j 1, ?_⟩
620	    funext i
621	    simp only [Matrix.mulVecLin_apply, Matrix.mulVec, Matrix.of_apply]
622	    rw [dotProduct_single, mul_one, hj]
623	  · -- every matrix-vector product is a combination of the columns
624	    rintro _ ⟨x, rfl⟩
625	    have hcol : (Matrix.of fun i j ↦
626	          (T.equivFin.symm j : Fin (p.fiberRank M) → R') i).mulVecLin x =
627	        ∑ j, x j • ((T.equivFin.symm j : Fin (p.fiberRank M) → R')) := by
628	      funext i
629	      simp [Matrix.mulVec, dotProduct, Finset.sum_apply, mul_comm]
630	    rw [hcol]
631	    exact Submodule.sum_mem _ fun j _ ↦ Submodule.smul_mem _ _
632	      (Submodule.subset_span (T.equivFin.symm j).2)
633	
634	end Module.FinitePresentation

516	  letI modInst : Module Γ(X, V) Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj N, U) :=

32	
33	/-- A function between topological spaces is locally constant if the preimage of any set is open. -/
34	def IsLocallyConstant (f : X → Y) : Prop :=
35	  ∀ s : Set Y, IsOpen (f ⁻¹' s)
36	
37	namespace IsLocallyConstant
38	
39	open List in
40	protected theorem tfae (f : X → Y) :
41	    TFAE [IsLocallyConstant f,
42	      ∀ x, ∀ᶠ x' in 𝓝 x, f x' = f x,
43	      ∀ x, IsOpen { x' | f x' = f x },
44	      ∀ y, IsOpen (f ⁻¹' {y}),
45	      ∀ x, ∃ U : Set X, IsOpen U ∧ x ∈ U ∧ ∀ x' ∈ U, f x' = f x] := by
46	  tfae_have 1 → 4 := fun h y => h {y}
47	  tfae_have 4 → 3 := fun h x => h (f x)
48	  tfae_have 3 → 2 := fun h x => IsOpen.mem_nhds (h x) rfl
49	  tfae_have 2 → 5
50	  | h, x => by
51	    rcases mem_nhds_iff.1 (h x) with ⟨U, eq, hU, hx⟩
52	    exact ⟨U, hU, hx, eq⟩
53	  tfae_have 5 → 1
54	  | h, s => by
55	    refine isOpen_iff_forall_mem_open.2 fun x hx ↦ ?_
56	    rcases h x with ⟨U, hU, hxU, eq⟩
57	    exact ⟨U, fun x' hx' => mem_preimage.2 <| (eq x' hx').symm ▸ hx, hU, hxU⟩
58	  tfae_finish
59	
60	@[nontriviality]
61	theorem of_discrete [DiscreteTopology X] (f : X → Y) : IsLocallyConstant f := fun _ =>
62	  isOpen_discrete _
63	
64	theorem isOpen_fiber {f : X → Y} (hf : IsLocallyConstant f) (y : Y) : IsOpen { x | f x = y } :=
65	  hf {y}
66	
67	theorem isClosed_fiber {f : X → Y} (hf : IsLocallyConstant f) (y : Y) : IsClosed { x | f x = y } :=
68	  ⟨hf {y}ᶜ⟩
69	
70	theorem isClopen_fiber {f : X → Y} (hf : IsLocallyConstant f) (y : Y) : IsClopen { x | f x = y } :=
71	  ⟨isClosed_fiber hf _, isOpen_fiber hf _⟩
72	
73	theorem iff_exists_open (f : X → Y) :
74	    IsLocallyConstant f ↔ ∀ x, ∃ U : Set X, IsOpen U ∧ x ∈ U ∧ ∀ x' ∈ U, f x' = f x :=
75	  (IsLocallyConstant.tfae f).out 0 4
76	
77	theorem iff_eventually_eq (f : X → Y) : IsLocallyConstant f ↔ ∀ x, ∀ᶠ y in 𝓝 x, f y = f x :=
78	  (IsLocallyConstant.tfae f).out 0 1
79	
80	theorem exists_open {f : X → Y} (hf : IsLocallyConstant f) (x : X) :
81	    ∃ U : Set X, IsOpen U ∧ x ∈ U ∧ ∀ x' ∈ U, f x' = f x :=
82	  (iff_exists_open f).1 hf x
83	
84	protected theorem eventually_eq {f : X → Y} (hf : IsLocallyConstant f) (x : X) :
85	    ∀ᶠ y in 𝓝 x, f y = f x :=
86	  (iff_eventually_eq f).1 hf x
87	
88	theorem iff_isOpen_fiber_apply {f : X → Y} : IsLocallyConstant f ↔ ∀ x, IsOpen (f ⁻¹' {f x}) :=
89	  (IsLocallyConstant.tfae f).out 0 2
90	
91	theorem iff_isOpen_fiber {f : X → Y} : IsLocallyConstant f ↔ ∀ y, IsOpen (f ⁻¹' {y}) :=
92	  (IsLocallyConstant.tfae f).out 0 3

Here is the raw data. All file paths are absolute; base directory `.lake-packages/mathlib/Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/` is abbreviated as `AG/` below, but full paths are given per item.

---

## 1. `Scheme.IdealSheafData` — structure, order, ext

File: `/home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/.lake-packages/mathlib/Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/IdealSheaf/Basic.lean`

**Structure** (line 65):
```lean
structure IdealSheafData (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type u where
  /-- The component of an ideal sheaf at an affine open. -/
  ideal : ∀ U : X.affineOpens, Ideal Γ(X, U)
  /-- Also see `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IdealSheafData.map_ideal` -/
  map_ideal_basicOpen : ∀ (U : X.affineOpens) (f : Γ(X, U)),
    (ideal U).map (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE <| X.basicOpen_le f).op).hom =
      ideal (X.affineBasicOpen f)
  /-- The support of an ideal sheaf. Use `IdealSheafData.support` instead for most occasions. -/
  supportSet : Set X := ⋂ U, X.zeroLocus (U := U.1) (ideal U)
  supportSet_eq_iInter_zeroLocus : supportSet = ⋂ U, X.zeroLocus (U := U.1) (ideal U) := by rfl
```

**ext** (line 78):
```lean
@[ext]
protected lemma ext {I J : X.IdealSheafData} (h : I.ideal = J.ideal) : I = J := by
```

**Order** — `≤` is the lift of `ideal` under the componentwise order (line 88, 90):
```lean
instance : PartialOrder (IdealSheafData X) := PartialOrder.lift ideal fun _ _ ↦ IdealSheafData.ext

lemma le_def {I J : IdealSheafData X} : I ≤ J ↔ ∀ U, I.ideal U ≤ J.ideal U := .rfl
```
So `I ≤ J` unfolds definitionally to `∀ U, I.ideal U ≤ J.ideal U` (there is no separate `le_iff`; the name is `le_def`). There is a full `CompleteLattice` instance (line 168), plus `SemilatticeInf`, `OrderTop`, `OrderBot`, and an `IdemCommSemiring`/`IsOrderedRing` structure (`ideal_sup`, `ideal_inf`, `ideal_iSup`, `ideal_iInf` describe the operations). Additional affine-specialized order/ext lemmas: `le_of_isAffine` (line 475), `ext_of_isAffine` (line 482); and gluing-cover versions `le_of_iSup_eq_top` (line 242), `ext_of_iSup_eq_top` (line 269).

---

The closed-immersion instance for `subschemeι` lives in ClosedImmersion.lean (line 93):

## 3. `Scheme.Hom.ker` — definition and lemmas

File: `/home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/.lake-packages/mathlib/Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/IdealSheaf/Basic.lean`

**Definition** — yes, it is the `ofIdeals` (largest ideal sheaf below the componentwise kernel) construction (line 689):
```lean
/-- The kernel of a morphism,
defined as the largest (quasi-coherent) ideal sheaf contained in the component-wise kernel.
This is usually only well-behaved when `f` is quasi-compact. -/
def Hom.ker (f : X.Hom Y) : IdealSheafData Y :=
  ofIdeals fun U ↦ RingHom.ker (f.app U).hom
```

**`ker_apply`** — requires `[QuasiCompact f]` (line 702):
```lean
@[simp]
lemma Hom.ker_apply (f : X.Hom Y) [QuasiCompact f] (U : Y.affineOpens) :
    f.ker.ideal U = RingHom.ker (f.app U).hom := by ...
```
Without quasi-compactness only the inequality holds — `Hom.ideal_ker_le` (line 695):
```lean
lemma Hom.ideal_ker_le (f : X.Hom Y) (U : Y.affineOpens) :
    f.ker.ideal U ≤ RingHom.ker (f.app U).hom := ideal_ofIdeals_le _ _
```

**No `le_ker_iff` lemma exists.** The closest is the general Galois-connection lemma `le_ofIdeals_iff` (see item 7), used together with `ker = ofIdeals …`; plus `Hom.le_ker_comp` (line 732): `g.ker ≤ (f ≫ g).ker`. Other `ker` lemmas: `ker_eq_top_of_isEmpty` (737), `Hom.ker_eq_bot_of_isIso` (741), `Hom.ker_comp_of_isIso` (745), `ker_of_isAffine` (749, `f.ker = ofIdealTop (RingHom.ker f.appTop.hom)`), `Hom.range_subset_ker_support` (758), `Hom.ker_eq_top_iff_isEmpty` (770), `Hom.support_ker` (843, needs `[QuasiCompact f]`).

**`ker_subschemeι`** (the kernel of `subschemeι` equals the datum) is in Subscheme.lean line 566:
```lean
@[simp]
lemma ker_subschemeι : I.subschemeι.ker = I := by ext; simp [ker_subschemeι_app]
```
(There is no name `subschemeι_ker`; the name is `ker_subschemeι`.)

---

## 4. `IsClosedImmersion.lift`, `lift_fac`, uniqueness, and Mono

File: `/home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/.lake-packages/mathlib/Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/ClosedImmersion.lean`

```lean
-- line 201
/-- The universal property of closed immersions:
For a closed immersion `f : X ⟶ Z`, given any morphism of schemes `g : Y ⟶ Z` whose kernel
contains the kernel of `X` in `Z`, we can lift this morphism to a unique `Y ⟶ X` that
commutes with these maps. -/
noncomputable
def lift {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}}
    (f : X ⟶ Z) (g : Y ⟶ Z) [IsClosedImmersion f] (H : f.ker ≤ g.ker) : Y ⟶ X :=
  g.toImage ≫ Scheme.IdealSheafData.inclusion H ≫ inv f.toImage

-- line 211
@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma lift_fac {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}}
    (f : X ⟶ Z) (g : Y ⟶ Z) [IsClosedImmersion f] (H : f.ker ≤ g.ker) : lift f g H ≫ f = g := by ...
```

Hypothesis direction: `H : f.ker ≤ g.ker`, i.e. the closed-immersion's kernel is `≤` the kernel of the map being lifted (the closed subscheme `X` is "larger"/its ideal is smaller). `lift f g H : Y ⟶ X` and factors as `lift f g H ≫ f = g`.

**Uniqueness**: there is no dedicated `lift_uniq`/`lift_unique` lemma. Uniqueness follows from `f` being `Mono`. The Mono instance is not stated directly for `IsClosedImmersion`; it comes via `IsPreimmersion` (`IsClosedImmersion → IsPreimmersion`). Instance in `/home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/.lake-packages/mathlib/Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/Preimmersion.lean` line 63:
```lean
instance (priority := 900) {X Y} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsPreimmersion f] : Mono f :=
  SurjectiveOnStalks.mono_of_injective f.isEmbedding.injective
```

Related lift lemmas in ClosedImmersion.lean: `isIso_of_ker_eq` (line 218), `isIso_lift` (line 228, `IsIso (lift i₁ i₂ h.le)` when `i₁.ker = i₂.ker`).

---

## 6. `IsAffineOpen.preimage` under affine morphisms

File: `/home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/.lake-packages/mathlib/Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/Affine.lean` line 50:

    (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsAffineHom f] :
    IsAffineOpen (f ⁻¹ᵁ U) :=
  IsAffineHom.isAffine_preimage _ hU
```
This is the general affine-morphism version; since `IsClosedImmersion f → IsAffineHom f` (instance at ClosedImmersion.lean line 149), it applies to closed immersions. There is no closed-immersion-specific variant. Two open-immersion / iso variants also exist in `/home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/.lake-packages/mathlib/Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean`: `IsAffineOpen.preimage_of_isIso` (line 518) and `IsAffineOpen.preimage_of_isOpenImmersion` (line 523, requires `U ≤ f.opensRange`).

---

## 7. `IdealSheafData.ofIdeals` and `le_ofIdeals_iff`

File: `/home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/.lake-packages/mathlib/Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/IdealSheaf/Basic.lean`

```lean
-- line 103
/-- The largest ideal sheaf contained in a family of ideals. -/
def ofIdeals (I : ∀ U : X.affineOpens, Ideal Γ(X, U)) : IdealSheafData X :=
  sSup { J : IdealSheafData X | J.ideal ≤ I }

-- line 107
lemma ideal_ofIdeals_le (I : ∀ U : X.affineOpens, Ideal Γ(X, U)) :
    (ofIdeals I).ideal ≤ I := sSup_le (Set.forall_mem_image.mpr fun _ ↦ id)

-- line 112
/-- The Galois coinsertion between ideal sheaves and arbitrary families of ideals. -/
protected def gci : GaloisCoinsertion ideal (ofIdeals (X := X)) where ...

lemma strictMono_ideal : StrictMono (ideal (X := X)) := ...   -- line 121
lemma ideal_mono : Monotone (ideal (X := X)) := ...           -- line 122
lemma ofIdeals_mono : Monotone (ofIdeals (X := X)) := ...      -- line 123
lemma ofIdeals_ideal (I : IdealSheafData X) : ofIdeals I.ideal = I := ...  -- line 124

-- line 125   ← le_ofIdeals_iff
lemma le_ofIdeals_iff {I : IdealSheafData X} {J} : I ≤ ofIdeals J ↔ I.ideal ≤ J :=
  IdealSheafData.gci.gc.le_iff_le.symm
```

---

### Notes on things that do NOT exist
- No `le_iff` for `IdealSheafData`; the definitional characterization is `le_def` (Basic.lean:90).
- No `le_ker_iff` lemma; use `le_ofIdeals_iff` with the fact `Hom.ker = ofIdeals (fun U ↦ RingHom.ker …)`, or `Hom.ideal_ker_le` / `Hom.ker_apply`.
- No dedicated `lift_uniq`/uniqueness lemma for `IsClosedImmersion.lift`; uniqueness is obtained from `Mono f` (via the `IsPreimmersion ⇒ Mono` instance in Preimmersion.lean:63).
- No closed-immersion-specific `IsAffineOpen.preimage`; the affine-morphism lemma `IsAffineOpen.preimage` (Affine.lean:50) covers it since closed immersions are affine.
- The name is `ker_subschemeι` (Subscheme.lean:566), not `subschemeι_ker`.

content.text:
All data gathered. Here is the report, organized by item number. All code is verbatim with line numbers from the current working tree.

---

## Item 1 — Helper lemmas at top of GenericFlatnessGeometric.lean

File: `/home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GenericFlatnessGeometric.lean`

The four relevant top-of-file helpers are `appLE_res_apply` (89), `appLE_base_res_apply` (99), the definitional-agreement transport `flat_sections_congr` (110), and `Module.Flat.of_isLocalizedModule_algebra` (131).

```lean
 87 /-- Elementwise fibre-side `appLE` coherence: restricting the image of `appLE`
 88 equals `appLE` into the smaller open. -/
 89 private lemma appLE_res_apply {S X : Scheme.{u}} (p : X ⟶ S) {U : S.Opens}
 90     {W W' : X.Opens} (e : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U) (h : W' ≤ W) (u : Γ(S, U)) :
 91     (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE h).op).hom ((p.appLE U W e).hom u) =
 92       (p.appLE U W' (h.trans e)).hom u := by
 93   have h1 := congrArg (fun (φ : Γ(S, U) ⟶ Γ(X, W')) => φ.hom u)
 94     (Scheme.Hom.appLE_map (f := p) e (homOfLE h).op)
 95   simpa only [CommRingCat.hom_comp, RingHom.comp_apply] using h1
 96
 97 /-- Elementwise base-side `appLE` coherence: `appLE` of a restricted base section
 98 equals `appLE` from the larger base open. -/
 99 private lemma appLE_base_res_apply {S X : Scheme.{u}} (p : X ⟶ S) {U U' : S.Opens}
100     {W : X.Opens} (h : U' ≤ U) (e' : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U') (e : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U) (u : Γ(S, U)) :
101     (p.appLE U' W e').hom ((S.presheaf.map (homOfLE h).op).hom u) =
102       (p.appLE U W e).hom u := by
103   have h1 := congrArg (fun (φ : Γ(S, U) ⟶ Γ(X, W)) => φ.hom u)
104     (Scheme.Hom.map_appLE (f := p) e' (homOfLE h).op)
105   simpa only [CommRingCat.hom_comp, RingHom.comp_apply] using h1
106
107 /-- Transport of section flatness along an equality of opens: the `Module.compHom`
108 structures via `appLE` agree definitionally because the `≤`-witness of `appLE` is
109 proof-irrelevant. -/
110 private theorem flat_sections_congr {S X : Scheme.{u}} (p : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules)
111     {U₀ : S.Opens} {O₁ O₂ : X.Opens} (hEq : O₁ = O₂)
112     (e₁ : O₁ ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀) (e₂ : O₂ ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀)
113     (h : letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, O₁) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ O₁ e₁).hom
114          Module.Flat Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, O₁)) :
115     letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, O₂) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ O₂ e₂).hom
116     Module.Flat Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, O₂) := by
117   subst hEq
118   exact h
119
120 open TensorProduct in
121 /-- **Mixed-base stability of flatness under module localization.**  Let `B` be an
122 `R`-algebra, `N` a `B`-module that is flat over `R`, and `g : N →ₗ[B] N'` a
123 localization of `N` at a submonoid `p ⊆ B`.  Then `N'` is still flat over `R`.
124
125 Mathlib's `Module.Flat.of_isLocalizedModule` treats only submonoids of `R`
126 itself; here the localization happens on the fibre side.  The proof rewrites
127 `𝟙 N' ⊗ f` as the `IsLocalizedModule.map` of `𝟙 N ⊗ f` (Mathlib's
128 `IsLocalizedModule.map_lTensor`, with the `IsLocalizedModule.rTensor` instances)
129 and uses that localization preserves injectivity
130 (`IsLocalizedModule.map_injective`). -/
131 theorem _root_.Module.Flat.of_isLocalizedModule_algebra {R B : Type v} [CommRing R]
132     [CommRing B] [Algebra R B] (p : Submonoid B) {N N' : Type v} [AddCommGroup N]
133     [Module R N] [Module B N] [IsScalarTower R B N] [AddCommGroup N'] [Module R N']
134     [Module B N'] [IsScalarTower R B N'] (g : N →ₗ[B] N') [IsLocalizedModule p g]
135     [Module.Flat R N] : Module.Flat R N' := by
136   rw [Module.Flat.iff_lTensor_injectiveₛ]
137   intro P _ _ I
138   rw [← TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.coe_lTensor (A := B)]
139   rw [← IsLocalizedModule.map_lTensor (S := p) (g := g) (f := I.subtype)]
140   exact IsLocalizedModule.map_injective p
141     (TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.rTensor R I g)
142     (TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.rTensor R P g)
143     (TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.lTensor B N I.subtype)
144     (by
145       rw [TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.coe_lTensor (A := B)]
146       exact Module.Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap I.subtype
147         Subtype.val_injective)
```

Notes on the item-1 questions:
- The "appLE module structures agreeing definitionally" lemma is `flat_sections_congr` (line 110): it transports `Module.Flat` across an equality of opens `O₁ = O₂` where both sides carry `Module.compHom _ (p.appLE …).hom` structures. The proof is simply `subst hEq; exact h` — the two `compHom` structures agree definitionally because the `≤`-witnesses are proof-irrelevant.
- `Module.Flat.of_isLocalizedModule_algebra` (line 131) is the mixed-base stability lemma that bridges `Module.Flat` across a fibre-side `IsLocalizedModule`.
- The only lemma explicitly transporting `Module.Flat` across an equality of ring homs / factorization is `flat_of_ringHom_comp_bijective` (line 1405, reported under item 3), not present in the 90–200 range.

---

## Item 2 — `coherentSheafFlat_of_iso` (full body)

File: `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GenericFlatnessGeometric.lean`, lines 1076–1103. It transports `Module.Flat` across `G ≅ G'` by building a `LinearEquiv` from the sheaf-morphism apps and feeding `Module.Flat.of_linearEquiv`. Bridging lemmas used: `Module.Flat.of_linearEquiv`, `Scheme.Modules.Hom.app` (`e.hom`/`e.inv`), `Scheme.Modules.Hom.app_smul`, and the `e.inv_hom_id` / `e.hom_inv_id` congr arguments (simp set: `Scheme.Modules.Hom.comp_app`, `Scheme.Modules.Hom.id_app`, `AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp`, `AddMonoidHom.coe_comp`, `AddCommGrpCat.hom_id`, `AddMonoidHom.id_apply`).

```lean
1076 /-- Transport of `CoherentSheafFlat` along an isomorphism of module sheaves. -/
1077 lemma coherentSheafFlat_of_iso {T' Y' : Scheme.{u}} (q : Y' ⟶ T') {G G' : Y'.Modules}
1078     (e : G ≅ G') (h : Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat q G) : Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat q G' := by
1079   intro U hU V hV eV
1080   letI : Module Γ(T', U) Γ(G', V) := Module.compHom _ (q.appLE U V eV).hom
1081   letI : Module Γ(T', U) Γ(G, V) := Module.compHom _ (q.appLE U V eV).hom
1082   haveI hG : Module.Flat Γ(T', U) Γ(G, V) := h hU hV eV
1083   refine Module.Flat.of_linearEquiv (M := Γ(G, V)) (e := ?_)
1084   exact
1085     { toFun := fun x => (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app e.inv V).hom x
1086       invFun := fun x => (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app e.hom V).hom x
1087       left_inv := fun x => by
1088         have h1 := congrArg
1089           (fun (φ : G' ⟶ G') => (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app φ V).hom x) e.inv_hom_id
1090         simp only [Scheme.Modules.Hom.comp_app, Scheme.Modules.Hom.id_app,
1091           AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp, AddMonoidHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply,
1092           AddCommGrpCat.hom_id, AddMonoidHom.id_apply] at h1
1093         exact h1
1094       right_inv := fun x => by
1095         have h1 := congrArg
1096           (fun (φ : G ⟶ G) => (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app φ V).hom x) e.hom_inv_id
1097         simp only [Scheme.Modules.Hom.comp_app, Scheme.Modules.Hom.id_app,
1098           AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp, AddMonoidHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply,
1099           AddCommGrpCat.hom_id, AddMonoidHom.id_apply] at h1
1100         exact h1
1101       map_add' := fun x y => map_add _ x y
1102       map_smul' := fun r x => Scheme.Modules.Hom.app_smul e.inv
1103         ((q.appLE U V eV).hom r) x }
```

---

## Item 3 — `coherentSheafFlat_of_comp_isIso` (full statement + body) and its transport substrate

File: `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GenericFlatnessGeometric.lean`.

The section-level substrate is `flat_of_ringHom_comp_bijective` (line 1405), which transports `Module.Flat` across a factorization `ψ = φ.comp ρ` of the acting ring map through a bijective `ρ`. Key transport steps: it makes `R'` an `R`-algebra via `ρ.toAlgebra`, proves `Module.Flat R R'` via `Module.Flat.of_linearEquiv` of `LinearEquiv.ofBijective (Algebra.linearMap R R')`, establishes `IsScalarTower R R' M`, and concludes with `Module.Flat.trans R R' M`.

```lean
1400 /-- Flatness of a module transports along a factorization of the acting ring
1401 map through a bijective ring map: if `ψ = φ ∘ ρ` with `ρ : R →+* R'`
1402 bijective and `M` flat for the `R'`-action through `φ`, then `M` is flat for
1403 the `R`-action through `ψ`.  (`R'` is a free `R`-module of rank one via `ρ`,
1404 so this is `Module.Flat.trans` along the scalar tower `R → R' → M`.) -/
1405 private theorem flat_of_ringHom_comp_bijective {R R' O M : Type v} [CommRing R]
1406     [CommRing R'] [CommRing O] [AddCommGroup M] [Module O M] (ρ : R →+* R')
1407     (hρ : Function.Bijective ρ) (φ : R' →+* O) (ψ : R →+* O)
1408     (hψ : ψ = φ.comp ρ)
1409     (h : letI : Module R' M := Module.compHom M φ; Module.Flat R' M) :
1410     letI : Module R M := Module.compHom M ψ; Module.Flat R M := by
1411   subst hψ
1412   letI : Module R' M := Module.compHom M φ
1413   letI : Module R M := Module.compHom M (φ.comp ρ)
1414   letI : Algebra R R' := ρ.toAlgebra
1415   haveI : Module.Flat R' M := h
1416   haveI : Module.Flat R R' := by
1417     have hcoe : ⇑(Algebra.linearMap R R') = ⇑ρ := by
1418       funext x
1419       simp [RingHom.algebraMap_toAlgebra]
1420     exact Module.Flat.of_linearEquiv
1421       ((LinearEquiv.ofBijective (Algebra.linearMap R R')
1422         (by rw [hcoe]; exact hρ)).symm)
1423   haveI : IsScalarTower R R' M :=
1424     ⟨fun r r' m => by
1425       change φ (ρ r * r') • m = φ (ρ r) • (φ r' • m)
1426       rw [map_mul, mul_smul]⟩
1427   exact Module.Flat.trans R R' M
```

```lean
1429 /-- **Coherent-sheaf flatness absorbs an isomorphism on the base**: if `𝓖` is
1430 flat for `q ≫ w` with `w` an isomorphism of schemes, then `𝓖` is flat for
1431 `q`.  Applied below with `q = 𝟙 P` to convert flatness over an isomorphic
1432 copy of `P` (e.g. `pullback.snd` of a pullback along an identity) into
1433 flatness over `P` itself. -/
1434 theorem coherentSheafFlat_of_comp_isIso {P W W' : Scheme.{u}} (q : P ⟶ W)
1435     (w : W ⟶ W') [IsIso w] (G : P.Modules)
1436     (h : Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (q ≫ w) G) : Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat q G := by
1437   intro U hU V hV eV
1438   have hrange : U ≤ (inv w).opensRange := by
1439     intro x hx
1440     refine ⟨w.base x, ?_⟩
1441     rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, IsIso.hom_inv_id]
1442     simp
1443   have hU' : IsAffineOpen ((inv w) ⁻¹ᵁ U) :=
1444     hU.preimage_of_isOpenImmersion (inv w) hrange
1445   have heq : (q ≫ w) ⁻¹ᵁ ((inv w) ⁻¹ᵁ U) = q ⁻¹ᵁ U := by
1446     rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_preimage, Category.assoc, IsIso.hom_inv_id,
1447       Category.comp_id]
1448   have eV' : V ≤ (q ≫ w) ⁻¹ᵁ ((inv w) ⁻¹ᵁ U) := eV.trans heq.ge
1449   have hflat := h hU' hV eV'
1450   have hfact : q.appLE U V eV =
1451       (inv w).appLE U ((inv w) ⁻¹ᵁ U) le_rfl ≫
1452         (q ≫ w).appLE ((inv w) ⁻¹ᵁ U) V eV' := by
1453     rw [Scheme.Hom.appLE_comp_appLE]
1454     have hmor : (q ≫ w) ≫ inv w = q := by

  def _root_.AlgebraicGeometry.affineOpensRestrict {X : Scheme.{u}} (U : X.Opens) :
      U.toScheme.affineOpens ≃ { V : X.affineOpens // V ≤ U } :=
    (IsOpenImmersion.affineOpensEquiv U.ι).toEquiv.trans (Equiv.subtypeEquivProp (by simp))

- `IsAffineOpen.preimage` (restriction along general morphism) — `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/Affine.lean:50`.

---

## Item 8 — `IsLocallyConstant` fiber API

File `Mathlib/Topology/LocallyConstant/Basic.lean`, namespace `IsLocallyConstant` (`variable {f : X → Y}`):
```lean
-- line 34
def IsLocallyConstant (f : X → Y) : Prop := ∀ s : Set Y, IsOpen (f ⁻¹' s)
-- line 64
theorem isOpen_fiber {f : X → Y} (hf : IsLocallyConstant f) (y : Y) : IsOpen { x | f x = y } := hf {y}
-- line 67
theorem isClosed_fiber {f : X → Y} (hf : IsLocallyConstant f) (y : Y) : IsClosed { x | f x = y } := ⟨hf {y}ᶜ⟩
-- line 70
theorem isClopen_fiber {f : X → Y} (hf : IsLocallyConstant f) (y : Y) : IsClopen { x | f x = y } :=
  ⟨isClosed_fiber hf _, isOpen_fiber hf _⟩
-- line 88
theorem iff_isOpen_fiber_apply {f : X → Y} : IsLocallyConstant f ↔ ∀ x, IsOpen (f ⁻¹' {f x})
-- line 91
theorem iff_isOpen_fiber {f : X → Y} : IsLocallyConstant f ↔ ∀ y, IsOpen (f ⁻¹' {y})
```
Also `iff_exists_open` (73), `iff_eventually_eq` (77), `exists_open` (80), `IsLocallyConstant.eventually_eq` (84), plus the master `IsLocallyConstant.tfae` (line 40) listing all equivalent forms. `isOpen_fiber` / `isClopen_fiber` are exactly what you want to extract that a level set of a locally-constant function is open/clopen.

lemma of_equiv {R R' M M' : Type*} [Semiring R] [AddCommMonoid M] [Module R M]
    [Semiring R'] [AddCommMonoid M'] [Module R' M']
    {σ : R →+* R'} {σ' : R' →+* R} [RingHomInvPair σ σ'] [RingHomInvPair σ' σ]
    (e₂ : M ≃ₛₗ[σ] M') [Module.Free R M] :
    Module.Free R' M' := by
  let e₁ : R ≃+* R' := RingHomInvPair.toRingEquiv σ σ'
  let I := Module.Free.ChooseBasisIndex R M
  obtain ⟨e₃ : M ≃ₗ[R] I →₀ R⟩ := Module.Free.chooseBasis R M
  let e : M' ≃+ (I →₀ R') :=
    (e₂.symm.trans e₃).toAddEquiv.trans (Finsupp.mapRange.addEquiv (ι := I) e₁.toAddEquiv)
  have he (x) : e x = Finsupp.mapRange.addEquiv (ι := I) e₁.toAddEquiv (e₃ (e₂.symm x)) := rfl
  let e' : M' ≃ₗ[R'] (I →₀ R') :=
    { __ := e, map_smul' := fun m x ↦ Finsupp.ext fun i ↦ by simp [e₁, he, map_smulₛₗ] }
  exact of_basis (.ofRepr e')

## Item 2 — Blueprint (`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex`)

**The `flatLocusStratification_universal` node** — theorem `thm:flattening_stratification_universal`, lines 2219–2289. `\lean` anchor is line 2224: `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusStratification_universal}`.

Theorem environment (2219–2255):
```
\begin{theorem}
\leanok
[Universal property of the flat-locus stratification (case \(n = 0\))]
  \label{thm:flattening_stratification_universal}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusStratification_universal}
  \uses{def:coherent_sheaf_flat}
  \source{nitsure-hilbert-quot}
  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme and \(\F\) a coherent \(\OO_S\)-module.
  Then there exist a finite index set \(I\) and locally-closed immersions
  \(\iota_f : S_f \to S\) (\(f \in I\)) with pairwise disjoint images
  set-theoretically covering \(|S|\), such that the pullback of \(\F\)
  along \(\iota_f\) is flat over \(S_f\) for every \(f\) (in the sense of
  \cref{def:coherent_sheaf_flat}), and such that, writing
  \(S' := \coprod_{f \in I} S_f\) and \(i : S' \to S\) for the morphism
  assembled from the \(\iota_f\), the pair \((S', i)\) has the universal
  property: every morphism \(\varphi : T \to S\) for which
  \(\varphi^* \F\) is flat over \(T\) factors \emph{uniquely} through
  \(i\).

  This is part (ii) of Nitsure's flattening-stratification theorem in the
  special case \(n = 0\) [...] the reduced strata of \cref{lem:flat_locus_reduction_lean}
  do not witness it (over \(S = \Spec k[\varepsilon]\) with \(\F = \OO_S\) flat,
  the identity must factor through the stratification, forcing the canonical
  non-reduced stratum structure).
\end{theorem}
```
Proof environment (2257–2289):
```
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:flat_locus_open, def:stratum, def:stratum_immersion,
    thm:mem_range_stratum, thm:map_strata_ideal_basic_open}
  By the case \(n = 0\) of the flattening stratification
  (\cref{lem:flat_locus_open}), with its canonical scheme structure: [...]
  The local strata glue by this representing property into locally-closed
  subschemes \(S_e \subseteq S\) [...] every point has a well-defined fibre rank.

  For the universal property, let \(\varphi : T \to S\) have \(\varphi^*\F\) flat
  over \(T\). A finitely presented flat module is locally free, and its rank is
  locally constant, so \(T\) decomposes into clopen pieces \(T_e\) [...]; by the
  representing property each restriction \(\varphi|_{T_e}\) factors uniquely
  through \(S_e\) [...]. Uniqueness: the strata have disjoint images [...] the
  component through \(S_e\) is unique because \(\iota_e\) is an immersion, hence a
  monomorphism.
\end{proof}
```
Other occurrences of the label (cross-references only): line 2377 and 2415 (in the "Mathlib status" section).

**`Scheme.restrict`** — `AlgebraicGeometry/OpenImmersion.lean`:
```

There is **no `∣_ᵤ` notation defined** in v4.31 — the only occurrence of the glyph `∣_ᵤ` in the whole Mathlib tree is a stale doc-comment reference at `Restrict.lean:19`. lemma comap_primeIdealOf_appLE {f : X ⟶ Y} {x : X} (U : Y.Opens)
      (hU : IsAffineOpen U) (V : X.Opens) (hV : IsAffineOpen V) (hVU : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U) (hx : x ∈ V) :
    (hV.primeIdealOf ⟨x, hx⟩).comap (f.appLE U V hVU).hom = hU.primeIdealOf ⟨f x, hVU hx⟩ := by
  change Spec.map (f.appLE U V hVU) (hV.primeIdealOf ⟨x, hx⟩) = (hU.primeIdealOf ⟨f x, hVU hx⟩)
  simp only [IsAffineOpen.primeIdealOf, ← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, IsAffineOpen.isoSpec_hom,
    Scheme.Opens.toSpecΓ_SpecMap_appLE]
  simp only [Scheme.Hom.comp_apply]

instance : ObjectProperty.IsClosedUnderIsomorphisms (C := Scheme) (IrreducibleSpace ·) :=
  ⟨fun e ↦ e.hom.homeomorph.irreducibleSpace_iff.mp⟩

instance : ObjectProperty.IsClosedUnderIsomorphisms (C := Scheme) (ConnectedSpace ·) :=
  ⟨fun e ↦ e.hom.homeomorph.connectedSpace_iff.mp⟩

/-- A scheme `X` is reduced if all `𝒪ₓ(U)` are reduced. -/
class IsReduced : Prop where
  component_reduced : ∀ U, _root_.IsReduced Γ(X, U) := by infer_instance

attribute [instance] IsReduced.component_reduced

theorem isReduced_of_isReduced_stalk [∀ x : X, _root_.IsReduced (X.presheaf.stalk x)] :
    IsReduced X := by
  refine ⟨fun U => ⟨fun s hs => ?_⟩⟩
  apply Presheaf.section_ext X.sheaf U s 0
  intro x hx
  change (X.sheaf.presheaf.germ U x hx) s = (X.sheaf.presheaf.germ U x hx) 0
  rw [map_zero]
  change X.presheaf.germ U x hx s = 0
  exact (hs.map _).eq_zero

instance isReduced_stalk_of_isReduced [IsReduced X] (x : X) :
    _root_.IsReduced (X.presheaf.stalk x) := by
  constructor
  rintro g ⟨n, e⟩
  obtain ⟨U, hxU, s, (rfl : (X.presheaf.germ U x hxU) s = g)⟩ := X.presheaf.exists_germ_eq g
  rw [← map_pow, ← map_zero (X.presheaf.germ _ x hxU).hom] at e
  obtain ⟨V, hxV, iU, iV, (e' : (X.presheaf.map iU.op) (s ^ n) = (X.presheaf.map iV.op) 0)⟩ :=
    X.presheaf.germ_eq x hxU hxU _ 0 e
  rw [map_pow, map_zero] at e'
  replace e' := (IsNilpotent.mk _ _ e').eq_zero (R := Γ(X, V))
  rw [← X.presheaf.germ_res iU x hxV, CommRingCat.comp_apply, e', map_zero]

theorem Module.ext' {R : Type*} [Semiring R] {M : Type*} [AddCommMonoid M] (P Q : Module R M)
    (w : ∀ (r : R) (m : M), (haveI := P; r • m) = (haveI := Q; r • m)) :
    P = Q := by
  ext
  exact w _ _

data.content:
set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 800000 in
-- Heartbeat headroom for the slice-site `HasSheafify` synthesis triggered by
-- `GeneratingSections.map` across the slice equivalence, as elsewhere in this file.
/-- **Affine sections of a finitely presented module sheaf are finitely generated**
(Stacks 01PC, finite-type half; chart-level form). If `F` is quasi-coherent, `q` is
a quasi-coherence datum with finite presentations, and `V ≤ q.X i` is an affine
open, then `Γ(F, V)` is a finite `Γ(X, V)`-module. The generating sections of the
slice `F.over (q.X i)` geometrize, pull back to `Spec Γ(X, V)` along the
factorization of `hV.fromSpec` through the cover member, land on `tilde Γ(F, V)`
by the 01I8 identification, and force finite generation via
`module_finite_of_tilde_genSections`. Project-local. -/
theorem module_finite_sections_of_quasicoherentData {X : Scheme.{u}} (F : X.Modules)
    [F.IsQuasicoherent] (q : SheafOfModules.QuasicoherentData.{u, u, u, u} F)
    [q.IsFinitePresentation] (i : q.I) {V : X.Opens} (hV : IsAffineOpen V)
    (hle : V ≤ q.X i) :
    Module.Finite Γ(X, V) Γ(F, V) := by
  -- slice generators of the cover member, with their finiteness
  haveI hσ₀ : (q.presentation i).generators.IsFiniteType := inferInstance
  -- the open-immersion factorization `k` of `fromSpec` through the cover member
  have hrange : Set.range hV.fromSpec.base ⊆ Set.range (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i)).base := by
    rw [hV.range_fromSpec, Scheme.Opens.range_ι]
    exact hle
  haveI hklift : IsOpenImmersion
      (IsOpenImmersion.lift (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i)) hV.fromSpec hrange ≫
        Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i)) := by
    rw [IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac]
    infer_instance
  haveI hk : IsOpenImmersion
      (IsOpenImmersion.lift (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i)) hV.fromSpec hrange) :=
    IsOpenImmersion.of_comp _ (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))
  haveI : PreservesColimitsOfSize.{u, u, u, u, u + 1, u + 1}
      (Scheme.Modules.pullback
        (IsOpenImmersion.lift (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i)) hV.fromSpec hrange)) :=
    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction _).leftAdjoint_preservesColimits
  -- pseudofunctoriality: composite pullback = pullback of `fromSpec`
  let e₄ : (Scheme.Modules.pullback
      (IsOpenImmersion.lift (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i)) hV.fromSpec hrange)).obj
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj F) ≅
      (Scheme.Modules.pullback hV.fromSpec).obj F :=
    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp
        (IsOpenImmersion.lift (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i)) hV.fromSpec hrange)
        (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).app F ≪≫
      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac _ _ hrange)).app F
  -- the 01I8 tilde identification of the pullback along `fromSpec`
  obtain ⟨⟨e₅, -⟩⟩ := tildeIso_of_isQuasicoherent_isAffineOpen F hV
  -- transport the generators all the way onto the tilde
  let σ₆ : (tilde (ModuleCat.of Γ(X, V) Γ(F, V))).GeneratingSections :=
    (((((q.presentation i).generators.map
        (overRestrictEquiv (q.X i)).functor (overRestrictUnitIso (q.X i))).ofEpi
          (overRestrictPullbackIso (q.X i) F).hom).map
        (Scheme.Modules.pullback
          (IsOpenImmersion.lift (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i)) hV.fromSpec hrange))
        (pullbackOpenImmersionUnitIso
          (IsOpenImmersion.lift (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i)) hV.fromSpec hrange)).symm).ofEpi
      e₄.hom).ofEpi e₅.hom
  haveI : σ₆.IsFiniteType := inferInstanceAs
    ((((((q.presentation i).generators.map _ _).ofEpi _).map _ _).ofEpi _).ofEpi
      e₅.hom).IsFiniteType
  exact module_finite_of_tilde_genSections (ModuleCat.of Γ(X, V) Γ(F, V)) σ₆

/-- **Every point has arbitrarily small affine neighbourhoods with finitely
generated sections** (Stacks 01PC, finite-type half; chart-supply form). For a
finitely presented sheaf of modules `F` on `X`, every point `x` in an open `O` has
an affine open neighbourhood `V ≤ O` with `Γ(F, V)` a finite `Γ(X, V)`-module.
This is the chart supply consumed by generic flatness (Nitsure §4).
Project-local. -/
theorem exists_affine_finite_sections_nhds {X : Scheme.{u}} (F : X.Modules)
    [F.IsFinitePresentation] (x : X) (O : X.Opens) (hxO : x ∈ O) :
    ∃ V : X.Opens, IsAffineOpen V ∧ x ∈ V ∧ V ≤ O ∧
      Module.Finite Γ(X, V) Γ(F, V) := by
  obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ := SheafOfModules.IsFinitePresentation.exists_quasicoherentData F
  haveI := hq
  -- `x` lies in some member of the presenting cover
  obtain ⟨U', f, hf, hxU'⟩ := q.coversTop ⊤ x (TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_top x)
  obtain ⟨i, ⟨g⟩⟩ := hf
  have hxi : x ∈ q.X i := leOfHom g hxU'
  -- shrink to an affine open inside `O ⊓ q.X i`
  obtain ⟨_, ⟨V, hV, rfl⟩, hxV, hVle⟩ :=
    X.isBasis_affineOpens.exists_subset_of_mem_open
      (show x ∈ ((O ⊓ q.X i : X.Opens) : Set X) from ⟨hxO, hxi⟩) (O ⊓ q.X i).2
  have hVle' : V ≤ O ⊓ q.X i := hVle
  exact ⟨V, hV, hxV, hVle'.trans inf_le_left,
    module_finite_sections_of_quasicoherentData F q i hV (hVle'.trans inf_le_right)⟩

end Scheme.Modules

end AlgebraicGeometry

/-- A scheme is locally Noetherian if and only if it is covered by affine opens whose sections
are Noetherian rings.

See [Har77], Proposition II.3.2. -/
theorem isLocallyNoetherian_iff_of_iSup_eq_top {ι} {S : ι → X.affineOpens}
    (hS : (⨆ i, S i : X.Opens) = ⊤) :
    IsLocallyNoetherian X ↔ ∀ i, IsNoetherianRing Γ(X, S i) :=
  ⟨fun _ i => IsLocallyNoetherian.component_noetherian (S i),
   isLocallyNoetherian_of_affine_cover hS⟩

/-- A version of `isLocallyNoetherian_iff_of_iSup_eq_top` using `Scheme.OpenCover`. -/
theorem isLocallyNoetherian_iff_of_affine_openCover (𝒰 : Scheme.OpenCover.{v, u} X)
    [∀ i, IsAffine (𝒰.X i)] :
    IsLocallyNoetherian X ↔ ∀ (i : 𝒰.I₀), IsNoetherianRing Γ(𝒰.X i, ⊤) := by
  constructor
  · intro h i
    let U := Scheme.Hom.opensRange (𝒰.f i)
    have := h.component_noetherian ⟨U, isAffineOpen_opensRange _⟩
    apply isNoetherianRing_of_ringEquiv (R := Γ(X, U))
    apply CategoryTheory.Iso.commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv
    exact (IsOpenImmersion.ΓIsoTop (𝒰.f i)).symm
  · intro hCNoeth
    let fS i : X.affineOpens := ⟨Scheme.Hom.opensRange (𝒰.f i), isAffineOpen_opensRange _⟩
    apply isLocallyNoetherian_of_affine_cover (S := fS)
    · rw [← Scheme.OpenCover.iSup_opensRange 𝒰]
    intro i
    apply isNoetherianRing_of_ringEquiv (R := Γ(𝒰.X i, ⊤))
    apply CategoryTheory.Iso.commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv
    exact IsOpenImmersion.ΓIsoTop (𝒰.f i)

-- Also see `LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian`.
lemma isLocallyNoetherian_of_isOpenImmersion {Y : Scheme} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f]
    [IsLocallyNoetherian Y] : IsLocallyNoetherian X where
  component_noetherian U :=
    have : IsNoetherianRing ↑Γ(Y, f ''ᵁ ↑U) :=
      IsLocallyNoetherian.component_noetherian ⟨_, U.2.image_of_isOpenImmersion f⟩
    isNoetherianRing_of_surjective _ _ _ (f.appIso U).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv.surjective

instance {U : X.Opens} [IsLocallyNoetherian X] : IsLocallyNoetherian U :=
  isLocallyNoetherian_of_isOpenImmersion U.ι

/-
Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Christian Merten
-/
import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.EntryIdealStratum
import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.GenericFlatnessGeometric

/-!
# Universal property of the rank strata (Nitsure §4, `n = 0`, Stage D)

This file proves the *representability* layer of the flat-locus
stratification: a morphism `q : T ⟶ X` whose pulled-back module is flat of
constant fiber rank `e` factors uniquely through the rank-`e` stratum
`stratumι : stratum G e hcov ⟶ X` of `AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.EntryIdealStratum`,
and the stratum itself carries a flat pullback.  These are parts (i)+(ii)
of the special case `n = 0` of the flattening-stratification theorem in
[Nitsure §4], feeding `AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusStratification_universal`.

## Contents

* §1 — bridges: matrix presentations of pullback section modules
  (`exists_matrixPresentation_pullback_sections`, via the Lane F section
  formula `pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap_sectionLinearEquiv`), transport of
  flatness between the natural and the `appLE`-restriction module structures
  (`flat_compHom_congr`), and extraction of section flatness from
  `CoherentSheafFlat (𝟙 T)` (`flat_sections_of_coherentSheafFlat_id`,
  `coherentSheafFlat_id_of_charts`).
* §2 — the rank dictionary: `rankAtStalk` of an affine section module
  computes the `pointRank` (`rankAtStalk_sections_eq_pointRank`), and the
  point rank of a pullback module is the point rank at the image point
  (`pointRank_pullback`; no flatness hypotheses, via
  `Ideal.fiberRank_baseChange`).
* §3 — Stage D1: the kernel bound `strataData_le_ker` (a flat
  constant-rank-`e` pullback kills the strata ideal) and the unique
  factorization `existsUnique_stratumLift` through the closed immersion
  `stratumι` (`IsClosedImmersion.lift`).
* §4 — Stage D2: the stratum pullback is flat over the stratum
  (`coherentSheafFlat_stratum`): on chart preimages the section module is
  presented with vanishing relation matrix, hence free.

Blueprint chapter: `Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex`,
§`sec:flatstrat_universal`.
Source: [Nitsure], §4, proof of the flattening-stratification theorem,
special case `n = 0` (`references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/`
`nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex`, L1849–L1885).
-/

universe u

open TensorProduct CategoryTheory

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

open Module

namespace Scheme.Modules

/-! ## §1 Presentations of pullback sections and flatness bridges -/

section PullbackPresentation

variable {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X) (G : X.Modules) [G.IsQuasicoherent]

/-- Elementwise fibre-side `appLE` coherence: restricting the image of
`appLE` equals `appLE` into the smaller open. -/
private lemma appLE_res_apply' {W W' : Y.Opens} {V : X.Opens}
    (e : W ≤ g ⁻¹ᵁ V) (h : W' ≤ W) (u : Γ(X, V)) :
    (Y.presheaf.map (homOfLE h).op).hom ((g.appLE V W e).hom u) =
      (g.appLE V W' (h.trans e)).hom u := by
  have h1 := congrArg (fun (φ : Γ(X, V) ⟶ Γ(Y, W')) => φ.hom u)
    (Scheme.Hom.appLE_map (f := g) e (homOfLE h).op)
  simpa only [CommRingCat.hom_comp, RingHom.comp_apply] using h1

/-- Elementwise base-side `appLE` coherence: `appLE` of a restricted base
section equals `appLE` from the larger base open. -/
private lemma appLE_base_res_apply' {V' V : X.Opens} {W : Y.Opens}
    (h : V' ≤ V) (e' : W ≤ g ⁻¹ᵁ V') (e : W ≤ g ⁻¹ᵁ V) (u : Γ(X, V)) :
    (g.appLE V' W e').hom ((X.presheaf.map (homOfLE h).op).hom u) =
      (g.appLE V W e).hom u := by
  have h1 := congrArg (fun (φ : Γ(X, V) ⟶ Γ(Y, W)) => φ.hom u)
    (Scheme.Hom.map_appLE (f := g) e' (homOfLE h).op)
  simpa only [CommRingCat.hom_comp, RingHom.comp_apply] using h1

/-- **Matrix presentations pull back to section modules** (the geometric
base-change of a local presentation, [Nitsure §4]: "the pulled-back
sequence is exact by right-exactness of tensor products").  For a morphism
`g : Y ⟶ X`, an affine `V ⊆ X`, an affine `W ⊆ g⁻¹V` and an `ee`-generator
presentation `P` of `Γ(G, V)`, the section module `Γ(g^*G, W)` admits an
`ee`-generator presentation whose relation matrix is the image of that of
`P` under `g.appLE`. -/
theorem exists_matrixPresentation_pullback_sections
    {V : X.Opens} (hV : IsAffineOpen V) {W : Y.Opens} (hW : IsAffineOpen W)
    (e : W ≤ g ⁻¹ᵁ V) {ee mm : ℕ}
    (P : MatrixPresentation Γ(X, V) Γ(G, V) ee mm) :
    ∃ P' : MatrixPresentation Γ(Y, W)
      Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj G, W) ee mm,
      P'.relMatrix = P.relMatrix.map (g.appLE V W e).hom := by
  letI : Algebra Γ(X, V) Γ(Y, W) := (g.appLE V W e).hom.toAlgebra
  letI : Module Γ(X, V) Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj G, W) :=
    Module.compHom _ (g.appLE V W e).hom
  obtain ⟨⟨eqv, -⟩⟩ :=
    pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap_sectionLinearEquiv g G hW hV e
  refine ⟨(P.baseChange Γ(Y, W)).congr eqv, ?_⟩
  rw [Module.MatrixPresentation.congr_relMatrix,
    Module.MatrixPresentation.baseChange_relMatrix,
    RingHom.algebraMap_toAlgebra]

end PullbackPresentation

section FlatBridge

/-- Transport of `Module.Flat` between the canonical module structure and a
`Module.compHom` structure along a pointwise-identity ring endomorphism
(the `appLE` of the identity morphism is the identity restriction, but not
definitionally the identity ring hom). -/
private lemma flat_compHom_congr {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {M : Type u}
    [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] (f : R →+* R) (hf : ∀ r, f r = r) :
    (letI : Module R M := Module.compHom M f
     Module.Flat R M) ↔ Module.Flat R M := by
  have h : (Module.compHom M f : Module R M) = ‹Module R M› :=
    Module.ext' _ _ fun r m => by
      show f r • m = r • m
      rw [hf r]
  exact (congrArg (fun i : Module R M => @Module.Flat R M _ _ i) h).to_iff

variable {T : Scheme.{u}}

/-- The `appLE` of the identity morphism at equal opens is pointwise the
identity. -/
private lemma id_appLE_apply {W : T.Opens}
    (e : W ≤ (𝟙 T : T ⟶ T) ⁻¹ᵁ W) (r : Γ(T, W)) :
    ((𝟙 T : T ⟶ T).appLE W W e).hom r = r := by
  rw [Scheme.id_appLE]
  exact presheaf_map_self (X := T) e r

/-- **Extraction of section flatness from flatness over the identity**: if
`G` is flat over `T` via `𝟙 T` (the encoding of "the coherent sheaf `G` is
flat" in `Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat`), then the sections over every affine
open form a flat module over the section ring. -/
theorem flat_sections_of_coherentSheafFlat_id {G : T.Modules}
    (h : Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (𝟙 T) G) {W : T.Opens}
    (hW : IsAffineOpen W) : Module.Flat Γ(T, W) Γ(G, W) :=
  (flat_compHom_congr ((𝟙 T : T ⟶ T).appLE W W (le_refl W)).hom
    (id_appLE_apply _)).mp (h hW hW (le_refl W))

/-- **Flatness over the identity from an affine cover of flat sections**:
if the sections of `G` over each member of an affine open cover of `T` are
flat over the respective section rings, then `G` is flat over `T` via
`𝟙 T`. -/
theorem coherentSheafFlat_id_of_charts (G : T.Modules) [G.IsQuasicoherent]
    {ι : Type u} (Wc : ι → T.Opens) (hWc : ∀ j, IsAffineOpen (Wc j))
    (hcover : ∀ y : T, ∃ j, y ∈ Wc j)
    (hflat : ∀ j, Module.Flat Γ(T, Wc j) Γ(G, Wc j)) :
    Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (𝟙 T) G := by
  intro U hU V hV eV
  exact flat_section_of_affine_cover (𝟙 T) G Wc hWc Wc hWc
    (fun j => le_refl _) hcover
    (fun j => (flat_compHom_congr
      ((𝟙 T : T ⟶ T).appLE (Wc j) (Wc j) (le_refl _)).hom
      (id_appLE_apply _)).mpr (hflat j)) hU hV eV

end FlatBridge

/-! ## §2 The rank dictionary -/

section RankDictionary

variable {T : Scheme.{u}} (G : T.Modules) [G.IsQuasicoherent]

/-- A prime of the section ring of an affine open is the prime ideal of
the corresponding point. -/
private lemma primeIdealOf_fromSpec {W : T.Opens} (hW : IsAffineOpen W)
    (p : PrimeSpectrum Γ(T, W)) (hmem : (hW.fromSpec p : T) ∈ W) :
    hW.primeIdealOf ⟨hW.fromSpec p, hmem⟩ = p := by
  have hinj : Function.Injective hW.fromSpec :=
    hW.fromSpec.isOpenEmbedding.injective
  exact hinj (by rw [hW.fromSpec_primeIdealOf])

/-- **The stalk rank of an affine section module computes the point rank**:
for a quasi-coherent `G` with flat, finite sections `Γ(G, W)` over an
affine `W`, the rank of `Γ(G, W)` at a prime `p` is the point rank of `G`
at the corresponding point `fromSpec p` [Nitsure §4: flatness plus finite
presentation make the fibre dimension compute the local rank]. -/
theorem rankAtStalk_sections_eq_pointRank {W : T.Opens}
    (hW : IsAffineOpen W) [Module.Flat Γ(T, W) Γ(G, W)]
    [Module.Finite Γ(T, W) Γ(G, W)] (p : PrimeSpectrum Γ(T, W)) :
    Module.rankAtStalk Γ(G, W) p = pointRank T G (hW.fromSpec p) := by
  have hmem : (hW.fromSpec p : T) ∈ W := by
    rw [← hW.range_fromSpec]
    exact Set.mem_range_self p
  rw [Module.rankAtStalk_eq,
    pointRank_eq_chartFiberRank G (V := ⟨W, hW⟩) _ hmem]
  show p.asIdeal.fiberRank Γ(G, W) = chartFiberRank G _ hmem
  rw [chartFiberRank]
  exact Ideal.fiberRank_congr_ideal
    (congrArg PrimeSpectrum.asIdeal (primeIdealOf_fromSpec hW p hmem)).symm

end RankDictionary

section PointRankPullback

variable {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X) (G : X.Modules) [G.IsQuasicoherent]

/-- **The point rank of a pullback module is the point rank at the image
point** (no flatness or finiteness hypotheses; the geometric form of
`Ideal.fiberRank_baseChange`): for any morphism `g : Y ⟶ X` and
quasi-coherent `G` on `X`,
`pointRank (g^*G) y = pointRank G (g y)`. -/
theorem pointRank_pullback (y : Y) :
    haveI := pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom g G ‹_›
    pointRank Y ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj G) y = pointRank X G (g y) := by
  haveI := pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom g G ‹_›
  -- affine charts around the image point and the point
  obtain ⟨V, hV, hgyV, -⟩ :=
    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (x := g y) (U := ⊤) trivial
  have hyV : y ∈ g ⁻¹ᵁ V := hgyV
  obtain ⟨W, hW, hyW, hWle⟩ :=
    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (x := y) (U := g ⁻¹ᵁ V) hyV
  -- both sides through the two charts
  rw [pointRank_eq_chartFiberRank _ (V := ⟨W, hW⟩) y hyW,
    pointRank_eq_chartFiberRank G (V := ⟨V, hV⟩) (g y) hgyV]
  -- the section formula for the pullback module over the affine pair
  letI : Algebra Γ(X, V) Γ(Y, W) := (g.appLE V W hWle).hom.toAlgebra
  letI : Module Γ(X, V) Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj G, W) :=
    Module.compHom _ (g.appLE V W hWle).hom
  obtain ⟨⟨eqv, -⟩⟩ :=
    pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap_sectionLinearEquiv g G hW hV hWle
  -- fiber rank through the equivalence, then base change
  have h1 : ((hW.primeIdealOf ⟨y, hyW⟩).asIdeal).fiberRank
      Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj G, W) =
      ((hW.primeIdealOf ⟨y, hyW⟩).asIdeal).fiberRank
        (TensorProduct Γ(X, V) Γ(Y, W) Γ(G, V)) :=
    Ideal.fiberRank_congr _ eqv.symm
  have h2 := Ideal.fiberRank_baseChange (R := Γ(X, V)) (A := Γ(Y, W))
    Γ(G, V) ((hW.primeIdealOf ⟨y, hyW⟩).asIdeal)
  -- identify the contracted prime with the prime of the image point
  have h3 : ((hW.primeIdealOf ⟨y, hyW⟩).asIdeal).comap
      (algebraMap Γ(X, V) Γ(Y, W)) =
      (hV.primeIdealOf ⟨g y, hgyV⟩).asIdeal := by
    have h4 := IsAffineOpen.comap_primeIdealOf_appLE (f := g)
      V hV W hW hWle hyW
    have h5 : algebraMap Γ(X, V) Γ(Y, W) = (g.appLE V W hWle).hom := rfl
    rw [h5]
    exact congrArg PrimeSpectrum.asIdeal h4
  show ((hW.primeIdealOf ⟨y, hyW⟩).asIdeal).fiberRank
      Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj G, W) =
    ((hV.primeIdealOf ⟨g y, hgyV⟩).asIdeal).fiberRank Γ(G, V)
  rw [h1, h2]
  exact Ideal.fiberRank_congr_ideal h3

end PointRankPullback

end Scheme.Modules

end AlgebraicGeometry

/-! ## §3 Stage D1: unique factorization through the stratum -/

section StageD1

variable {X T : Scheme.{u}} (G : X.Modules) [G.IsQuasicoherent] {e : ℕ}

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in
/-- **The kernel bound** (the hard half of Nitsure's universal property,
`n = 0`, [Nitsure §4]: "`f^*𝓕` is locally free of rank `e` iff `f^*ψ = 0`
iff `f` factors through `V_e`"): if the pullback of `G` along `q : T ⟶ X`
is flat over `T` of constant point rank `e`, then the strata ideal sheaf is
contained in the kernel of `q` — every local relation matrix pulls back to
the zero matrix, so its entries die in `Γ(T, ·)`. -/
theorem strataData_le_ker (hcov : ChartsCover G e) (q : T ⟶ X)
    (hflat : Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (𝟙 T)
      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj G))
    (hrank : ∀ t : T,
      haveI := pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom q G ‹_›
      pointRank T ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj G) t = e) :
    strataData G e hcov ≤ q.ker := by
  haveI := pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom q G ‹_›
  refine Scheme.IdealSheafData.le_ofIdeals_iff.mpr fun U => ?_
  intro r hr
  rw [RingHom.mem_ker]
  -- a finite family of `e`-presentation charts covering `U`
  obtain ⟨N, Vc, mmc, Pc, hVcU, hcovU⟩ :=
    ChartsCover.exists_finite_charts G hcov U
  -- the pulled-back section dies on an affine neighbourhood of each point
  apply T.IsSheaf.section_ext fun t ht => ?_
  obtain ⟨i, hti⟩ := hcovU (q.base t) ht
  have htVc : t ∈ q ⁻¹ᵁ (Vc i).1 := hti
  obtain ⟨W, hW, htW, hWle⟩ :=
    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (x := t) (U := q ⁻¹ᵁ (Vc i).1) htVc
  have hV : W ≤ q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1 := fun y hy => hVcU i (hWle hy)
  refine ⟨W, hV, htW, ?_⟩
  -- the relation matrix of the chart pulls back to zero on `W`
  haveI hFlatW : Module.Flat Γ(T, W)
      Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj G, W) :=
    flat_sections_of_coherentSheafFlat_id hflat hW
  obtain ⟨P', hP'⟩ := exists_matrixPresentation_pullback_sections q G
    (Vc i).2 hW hWle (Pc i)
  haveI : Module.Finite Γ(T, W) Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj G, W) :=
    Module.Finite.of_surjective P'.proj P'.surjective_proj
  have h0 : P'.relMatrix = 0 :=
    P'.relMatrix_eq_zero_of_flat fun p =>
      (rankAtStalk_sections_eq_pointRank _ hW p).trans (hrank _)
  -- hence the entry ideal is killed by `q.appLE (Vc i) W`
  letI : Algebra Γ(X, (Vc i).1) Γ(T, W) := (q.appLE (Vc i).1 W hWle).hom.toAlgebra
  have hker : (Pc i).entryIdeal ≤
      RingHom.ker (algebraMap Γ(X, (Vc i).1) Γ(T, W)) :=
    Module.MatrixPresentation.relMatrix_map_eq_zero_iff.mp (hP' ▸ h0)
  -- the strata-ideal condition at the chart
  have hmem : X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hVcU i)).op r ∈ (Pc i).entryIdeal :=
    (mem_strataIdeal_iff G).mp hr (Vc i) (hVcU i) (mmc i) (Pc i)
  have hzero : (q.appLE (Vc i).1 W hWle).hom
      (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hVcU i)).op r) = 0 :=
    RingHom.mem_ker.mp (hker hmem)
  -- assemble: restriction of `q.app U r` to `W` is `q.appLE (Vc i) W` of
  -- the restricted section
  have hres : (T.presheaf.map (homOfLE hV).op).hom ((q.app U.1).hom r) =
      (q.appLE U.1 W hV).hom r := rfl
  show (T.presheaf.map (homOfLE hV).op).hom ((q.app U.1).hom r) =
    (T.presheaf.map (homOfLE hV).op).hom 0
  rw [map_zero, hres, ← appLE_base_res_apply' q (hVcU i) hWle hV r, hzero]

/-- **Unique factorization through the rank-`e` stratum** [Nitsure §4,
part (ii), `n = 0` case]: a morphism `q : T ⟶ X` whose pulled-back module
is flat over `T` of constant point rank `e` factors uniquely through the
closed immersion of the rank-`e` stratum. -/
theorem existsUnique_stratumLift (hcov : ChartsCover G e) (q : T ⟶ X)
    (hflat : Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (𝟙 T)
      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj G))
    (hrank : ∀ t : T,
      haveI := pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom q G ‹_›
      pointRank T ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj G) t = e) :
    ∃! l : T ⟶ stratum G e hcov, l ≫ stratumι G e hcov = q := by
  have hker : (stratumι G e hcov).ker ≤ q.ker := by
    have h1 : (stratumι G e hcov).ker = strataData G e hcov :=
      Scheme.IdealSheafData.ker_subschemeι (strataData G e hcov)
    rw [h1]
    exact strataData_le_ker G hcov q hflat hrank
  refine ⟨IsClosedImmersion.lift (stratumι G e hcov) q hker,
    IsClosedImmersion.lift_fac _ _ hker, fun l' hl' => ?_⟩
  rw [← cancel_mono (stratumι G e hcov), hl',
    IsClosedImmersion.lift_fac]

end StageD1

/-! ## §4 Stage D2: the stratum pullback is flat -/

section StageD2

variable {X : Scheme.{u}} (G : X.Modules) [G.IsQuasicoherent] {e : ℕ}

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in
/-- **The rank-`e` stratum flattens `G`** [Nitsure §4, `n = 0`: on `V_e`
the pulled-back presentation has vanishing relation matrix, so `𝓕|_{V_e}`
is free of rank `e`]: the pullback of `G` along the stratum immersion is
flat over the stratum. -/
theorem coherentSheafFlat_stratum (hcov : ChartsCover G e) :
    Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (𝟙 (stratum G e hcov))
      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (stratumι G e hcov)).obj G) := by
  haveI := pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom (stratumι G e hcov) G ‹_›
  refine coherentSheafFlat_id_of_charts _
    (ι := {V : X.affineOpens // IsPresentationChart G e V})
    (fun j => stratumι G e hcov ⁻¹ᵁ j.1.1)
    (fun j => j.1.2.preimage (stratumι G e hcov)) (fun z => ?_) (fun j => ?_)
  · -- the chart preimages cover the stratum
    obtain ⟨V, hzV, hchart⟩ := hcov ((stratumι G e hcov).base z)
    exact ⟨⟨V, hchart⟩, hzV⟩
  · -- flat sections on each chart preimage: the pulled-back presentation
    -- has vanishing relation matrix, so the section module is free
    obtain ⟨mm, ⟨P⟩⟩ := j.2
    obtain ⟨P', hP'⟩ := exists_matrixPresentation_pullback_sections
      (stratumι G e hcov) G j.1.2 (j.1.2.preimage (stratumι G e hcov))
      le_rfl P
    have h0 : P'.relMatrix = 0 := by
      rw [hP']
      ext a b
      simp only [Matrix.map_apply, Matrix.zero_apply]
      -- the entry lies in the strata ideal, the kernel of `stratumι.app`
      have hmem : P.relMatrix a b ∈
          RingHom.ker (((strataData G e hcov).subschemeι.app j.1.1).hom) := by
        rw [Scheme.IdealSheafData.ker_subschemeι_app]
        show P.relMatrix a b ∈ strataIdeal G e j.1
        rw [strataIdeal_eq_entryIdeal G P]
        exact P.relMatrix_mem_entryIdeal a b
      -- `appLE` at `le_rfl` is `app` followed by the trivial restriction
      show ((stratumι G e hcov).appLE j.1.1
        (stratumι G e hcov ⁻¹ᵁ j.1.1) le_rfl).hom (P.relMatrix a b) = 0
      have happ : ((stratumι G e hcov).appLE j.1.1
          (stratumι G e hcov ⁻¹ᵁ j.1.1) le_rfl).hom (P.relMatrix a b) =
          ((stratum G e hcov).presheaf.map (homOfLE le_rfl).op).hom
            (((stratumι G e hcov).app j.1.1).hom (P.relMatrix a b)) := rfl
      rw [happ, RingHom.mem_ker.mp hmem, map_zero]
    haveI := P'.free_of_relMatrix_eq_zero h0
    infer_instance

end StageD2

end Scheme.Modules

end AlgebraicGeometry

noncomputable def restrictBasicOpenₗ {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) {U : X.Opens} (f : Γ(X, U))
    [Module Γ(X, U) Γ(M, X.basicOpen f)]
    [IsScalarTower Γ(X, U) Γ(X, X.basicOpen f) Γ(M, X.basicOpen f)] :
    Γ(M, U) →ₗ[Γ(X, U)] Γ(M, X.basicOpen f) where
  toFun := fun x => M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f)).op x
  map_add' := map_add _

    change M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f)).op (r • x) = r • _
    rw [Scheme.Modules.map_smul M (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f)) r x,
theorem isLocalizedModule_basicOpen (M : X.Modules) [M.IsQuasicoherent]

(M ⧸ LinearMap.range (Fintype.linearCombination R mvec)) :=
      Module.notMem_support_iff.mpr hQpsub
    rw [Module.mem_support_iff_of_finite] at hnmem
    exact SetLike.not_le_iff_exists.mp hnmem
  have hg : ∀ x : M, g • x ∈ LinearMap.range (Fintype.linearCombination R mvec) := by
    intro x
    have hx := Module.mem_annihilator.mp hgann (Submodule.Quotient.mk
      (p := LinearMap.range (Fintype.linearCombination R mvec)) x)
    rwa [← Submodule.Quotient.mk_smul, Submodule.Quotient.mk_eq_zero] at hx
  refine ⟨g, hgp, ?_⟩
  intro R' _ _ _ N _ _ _ _ l _
  -- `N` is finitely presented over `R'`
  haveI : Module.FinitePresentation R' N :=
    _root_.FinitePresentation.of_isBaseChange l
      (IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange (Submonoid.powers g) R' l)
  -- the images `l mᵢ` generate `N` over `R'`
  have hgen : Function.Surjective (Fintype.linearCombination R' fun i ↦ l (mvec i)) := by
    rw [← LinearMap.range_eq_top, Fintype.range_linearCombination, ← top_le_iff]
    intro n _
    obtain ⟨⟨x, s⟩, hxs⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj (Submonoid.powers g) l n
    have hs : IsUnit (algebraMap R R' (s : R)) := IsLocalization.map_units R' s
    have hgu : IsUnit (algebraMap R R' g) :=
      IsLocalization.map_units R' ⟨g, Submonoid.mem_powers g⟩
    -- `l (g • x)` lies in the `R'`-span of the `l mᵢ`
    have hmem : l (g • x) ∈ Submodule.span R' (Set.range fun i ↦ l (mvec i)) := by
      obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := hg x
      have hsum : l (g • x) = ∑ i, c i • l (mvec i) := by
        rw [← hc]
        simp [Fintype.linearCombination_apply, map_sum]
      rw [hsum]
      exact Submodule.sum_mem _ fun i _ ↦ Submodule.smul_of_tower_mem _ (c i)
        (Submodule.subset_span ⟨i, rfl⟩)
    -- clear the units `s` and `g`
    have hxs' : (s : R) • n = l x := hxs
    have hn : algebraMap R R' ((s : R) * g) • n = l (g • x) := by
      rw [map_mul, mul_smul, algebraMap_smul, algebraMap_smul, smul_comm, hxs',
        ← map_smul]
    have hu : IsUnit (algebraMap R R' ((s : R) * g)) := by
      rw [map_mul]
      exact hs.mul hgu
    have hnn : n = hu.unit⁻¹ • l (g • x) := by
      rw [eq_inv_smul_iff, Units.smul_def, IsUnit.unit_spec]
      exact hn
    rw [hnn]
    exact Submodule.smul_mem _ _ hmem
  -- the kernel is finitely generated; its generators are the relation matrix
  obtain ⟨T, hT⟩ := Module.FinitePresentation.fg_ker
    (Fintype.linearCombination R' fun i ↦ l (mvec i)) hgen
  refine ⟨T.card, ⟨{
    relMatrix := Matrix.of fun i j ↦ (T.equivFin.symm j : Fin (p.fiberRank M) → R') i
    proj := Fintype.linearCombination R' fun i ↦ l (mvec i)
    surjective_proj := hgen
    exact_mulVecLin_proj := ?_ }⟩⟩
  rw [LinearMap.exact_iff, ← hT]
  apply le_antisymm
  · -- each generator is a column of the matrix
    rw [Submodule.span_le]
    intro t ht
    obtain ⟨j, hj⟩ := T.equivFin.symm.surjective ⟨t, ht⟩
    refine ⟨Pi.single j 1, ?_⟩
    funext i
    simp only [Matrix.mulVecLin_apply, Matrix.mulVec, Matrix.of_apply]
    rw [dotProduct_single, mul_one, hj]
  · -- every matrix-vector product is a combination of the columns
    rintro _ ⟨x, rfl⟩
    have hcol : (Matrix.of fun i j ↦
          (T.equivFin.symm j : Fin (p.fiberRank M) → R') i).mulVecLin x =
        ∑ j, x j • ((T.equivFin.symm j : Fin (p.fiberRank M) → R')) := by
      funext i
      simp [Matrix.mulVec, dotProduct, Finset.sum_apply, mul_comm]
    rw [hcol]
    exact Submodule.sum_mem _ fun j _ ↦ Submodule.smul_mem _ _
      (Submodule.subset_span (T.equivFin.symm j).2)

/-! ## §5 Stage E: chart supply and the chart locus (noetherian base) -/

section ChartSupply

/-- Any finitely presented module admits a matrix presentation (the
generators of the finitely generated kernel of a finite free surjection
are the columns of the relation matrix). -/
theorem _root_.Module.FinitePresentation.exists_matrixPresentation
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
    [Module.FinitePresentation R M] :
    ∃ (ee mm : ℕ), Nonempty (MatrixPresentation R M ee mm) := by
  classical
  obtain ⟨n, f, hsurj, hker⟩ :=
    Module.FinitePresentation.exists_fin (R := R) (M := M)
  obtain ⟨T, hT⟩ := hker
  refine ⟨n, T.card, ⟨{
    relMatrix := Matrix.of fun i j ↦ (T.equivFin.symm j : Fin n → R) i
    proj := f
    surjective_proj := hsurj
    exact_mulVecLin_proj := ?_ }⟩⟩
  rw [LinearMap.exact_iff, ← hT]
  apply le_antisymm
  · -- each generator is a column of the matrix
    rw [Submodule.span_le]
    intro t ht
    obtain ⟨j, hj⟩ := T.equivFin.symm.surjective ⟨t, ht⟩
    refine ⟨Pi.single j 1, ?_⟩
    funext i
    simp only [Matrix.mulVecLin_apply, Matrix.mulVec, Matrix.of_apply]
    rw [dotProduct_single, mul_one, hj]
  · -- every matrix-vector product is a combination of the columns
    rintro _ ⟨x, rfl⟩
    have hcol : (Matrix.of fun i j ↦ (T.equivFin.symm j : Fin n → R) i).mulVecLin x =
        ∑ j, x j • ((T.equivFin.symm j : Fin n → R)) := by
      funext i
      simp [Matrix.mulVec, dotProduct, Finset.sum_apply, mul_comm]
    rw [hcol]
    exact Submodule.sum_mem _ fun j _ ↦ Submodule.smul_mem _ _
      (Submodule.subset_span (T.equivFin.symm j).2)

variable {S : Scheme.{u}}

/-- The canonical `Γ(S, U)`-module structure on the sections of a module
sheaf over a basic open of `U` (restriction of scalars along the
restriction map); matches the canonical algebra instance on the section
rings. -/
noncomputable local instance moduleSectionBasicOpen (F : S.Modules)
    {U : S.Opens} (f : Γ(S, U)) :
    Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, S.basicOpen f) :=
  Module.compHom _ (S.presheaf.map (homOfLE (S.basicOpen_le f)).op).hom

local instance isScalarTowerSectionBasicOpen (F : S.Modules)
    {U : S.Opens} (f : Γ(S, U)) :
    IsScalarTower Γ(S, U) Γ(S, S.basicOpen f) Γ(F, S.basicOpen f) :=
  .of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ => rfl

/-- **Chart supply at every point** ([Nitsure §4], Nakayama prolongation of
a fibre basis; the geometric form of
`Module.FinitePresentation.exists_matrixPresentation_of_isLocalizedModule`):
on a locally noetherian scheme, every point of a finitely presented module
sheaf has an `e`-presentation chart around it with `e` its own point
rank. -/
theorem exists_presentationChart_mem [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
    (F : S.Modules) [F.IsFinitePresentation] (s : S) :
    ∃ V : S.affineOpens, s ∈ V.1 ∧
      IsPresentationChart F (pointRank S F s) V := by
  obtain ⟨V, hV, hsV, -, hfin⟩ := exists_affine_finite_sections_nhds F s ⊤ trivial
  haveI : IsNoetherianRing Γ(S, V) :=
    IsLocallyNoetherian.component_noetherian ⟨V, hV⟩
  haveI : Module.Finite Γ(S, V) Γ(F, V) := hfin
  haveI : Module.FinitePresentation Γ(S, V) Γ(F, V) :=
    Module.finitePresentation_of_finite _ _
  obtain ⟨g, hgp, hloc⟩ :=
    Module.FinitePresentation.exists_matrixPresentation_of_isLocalizedModule
      (M := Γ(F, V)) (hV.primeIdealOf ⟨s, hsV⟩).asIdeal
  haveI := hV.isLocalization_basicOpen g
  haveI := Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen F hV g
  obtain ⟨mm, ⟨P⟩⟩ := hloc Γ(S, S.basicOpen g) Γ(F, S.basicOpen g)
    (restrictBasicOpenₗ F g)
  have hrank : ((hV.primeIdealOf ⟨s, hsV⟩).asIdeal).fiberRank Γ(F, V) =
      pointRank S F s :=
    (pointRank_eq_chartFiberRank F (V := ⟨V, hV⟩) s hsV).symm
  refine ⟨S.affineBasicOpen g, ?_, mm, ⟨hrank ▸ P⟩⟩
  exact (hV.mem_basicOpen_iff_notMem_primeIdealOf g ⟨s, hsV⟩).mpr hgp

variable (F : S.Modules) (e : ℕ)

/-- The **chart locus**: the union of all `e`-presentation charts — the
open locus on which the rank-`e` stratum is a closed subscheme
[Nitsure §4: the ambient open `V` of the local construction]. -/
noncomputable def chartLocus : S.Opens :=
  ⨆ V : {V : S.affineOpens // IsPresentationChart F e V}, V.1.1

/-- Points of point rank exactly `e` lie in the chart locus. -/
theorem mem_chartLocus_of_pointRank_eq [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
    [F.IsFinitePresentation] {s : S} (h : pointRank S F s = e) :
    s ∈ chartLocus F e := by
  obtain ⟨V, hsV, hchart⟩ := exists_presentationChart_mem F s
  exact TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mpr ⟨⟨V, h ▸ hchart⟩, hsV⟩

/-- Points of the chart locus have point rank at most `e` (an
`e`-generator presentation bounds every fibre dimension). -/
theorem pointRank_le_of_mem_chartLocus [F.IsQuasicoherent] {s : S}
    (h : s ∈ chartLocus F e) : pointRank S F s ≤ e := by
  obtain ⟨⟨V, hchart⟩, hsV⟩ := TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mp h
  obtain ⟨mm, ⟨P⟩⟩ := hchart
  rw [pointRank_eq_chartFiberRank F (V := V) s hsV]
  exact P.fiberRank_le _

end ChartSupply

/-- Any finitely presented module admits a matrix presentation (the
generators of the finitely generated kernel of a finite free surjection
are the columns of the relation matrix). -/
theorem _root_.Module.FinitePresentation.exists_matrixPresentation
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
    [Module.FinitePresentation R M] :
